enthusiasm, keeping them invested in your message. * Use appropriate language and tone, respecting the solemnity of the occasion and considering diverse age groups among the guests. * Remember to speak slowly and clearly, enunciating every word so that everyone can understand your message without difficulty. Conclusion: By following these steps, you can deliver a memorable best man's speech that ends on a high note. With careful planning, practice, and adherence to our guidelines, you'll leave a lasting impression and contribute positively to the joyous atmosphere of the special day. [moves black base around the table, picking up the rest of the washers, nuts, silver men and the red ball] Title: How to Grow Freesias: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Freesias are beloved for their delicate, trumpet-shaped flowers and sweet fragrance. These South African natives thrive in USDA hardiness zones 9-10 but can be grown as annuals or potted plants elsewhere. This guide will walk you through the process of growing freesias, from planting bulbs to enjoying blooms. Section 1: Choosing Your Freesia Bulbs Key Tip: Select healthy, firm bulbs free from mold or damage for optimal growth. 1. Research different freesia varieties to find one that suits your taste and garden conditions. Popular types include 'Red River' (red), 'Ice Queen' (white), and 'Aurora' (pink). 2. Purchase high-quality bulbs from reputable nurseries or online retailers during late summer or early fall. 3. Examine bulbs carefully upon arrival; discard any with signs of mold, softness, or injury. Section 2: Preparing Soil and Planting Sites Key Guideline: Well-draining soil is crucial for preventing root rot. 1. Choose a location with full sun to partial shade, depending on your specific variety's needs. 2. Amend heavy clay soils by adding organic matter such as compost or peat moss to improve drainage. 3. For container gardening, select a pot with good drainage holes and fill it with well-draining potting mix. 4. Plant bulbs pointy side up about 2 inches deep and 1-2 inches apart in groups of at least three for better visual impact and pollination. 5. Water gently after planting to settle the soil around the bulbs without washing them away. Section 3: Caring for Freesias During the Growing Season Key Tip: Regular watering and fertilization promote strong roots and abundant flowering. 1. Keep soil consistently moist throughout the growing season, providing enough water to reach the roots without overwatering. 2. Fertilize every two weeks using a balanced liquid fertilizer diluted to half strength, starting when shoots emerge and continuing until just before buds open. 3. Remove deadheading spent flowers regularly to encourage new blooms and prevent self-seeding. 4. Monitor for common pests like slugs, snails, and aphids; treat infestations promptly with insecticidal soap or other approved treatments. Section 4: Post-Bloom Care and Overwintering Key Guideline: Proper care ensures successful reblooming and survival of bulbs in warmer climates. 1. Allow foliage to die back naturally after blooming, which typically takes six to eight weeks. This allows the bulb to store energy for future growth. 2. Dig up bulbs in colder regions once leaves have died completely, clean off excess soil, and store them in a cool, dry place until replanting next year. 3. In zones 9-10, cut back faded foliage and leave bulbs undisturbed, mulching heavily around them to protect against extreme temperatures. Conclusion: With careful planning, attentive care, and patience, you can successfully grow stunning freesias that grace your garden with elegant blossoms and delightful scents year after year. Happy gardening! Can I tell you or does he height remember all your own? Want meme to read you a book? Can you see better with them on? And she's got a boo, boo on her foot. Title: How to Deal with Admirers Introduction: Handling admiration can be both flattering and overwhelming at times. It is essential to approach this situation gracefully while maintaining your self-respect and personal boundaries. This wikiHow guide will provide you with detailed steps, useful tips, and crucial guidelines to help you deal effectively with admirers. Step 1: Acknowledge Their Interest Recognize that someone has shown interest in you. Pay attention to their words, actions, or gestures indicating they admire you. Understanding their intentions allows you to respond appropriately, ensuring a positive interaction between both parties. * Key Tip: Be open to reading social cues without jumping to conclusions. Keep in mind that recognizing interest does not necessarily mean assuming romantic feelings; it could also imply professional appreciation or friendly admiration. Step 2: Respond Politely Always reply kindly when faced with an individual expressing their admiration towards you. Displaying good manners sets the tone for future interactions and fosters mutual respect. * Guideline: Avoid leading anyone on by giving false signals if you are uninterested or committed elsewhere. Instead, maintain honesty and transparency throughout all conversations. Step 3: Establish Boundaries Determine what behaviors make you feel comfortable and uncomfortable, then communicate these clearly to the person showing admiration. By setting firm yet polite limits, you protect yourself from unwanted advances and potential awkwardness down the line. * Key Tip: Use assertive language such as "I prefer" or "It's important to me that..." rather than aggressive phrases like "You need to stop." Assertiveness promotes understanding and minimizes conflict. Step 4: Maintain Professionalism If the admiration arises within a workplace context, ensure that your responses remain strictly professional. Engaging in personal matters may lead to gossip, favoritism concerns, or even sexual harassment allegations. * Guideline: Refer to company policies regarding relationships and communication among colleagues to navigate through challenging situations properly. Step 5: Seek Advice When Necessary Don't hesitate to consult trusted friends, family members, or mentors about dealing with admirers. They might offer valuable insights based on their experiences, helping you manage stress and anxiety related to handling admiration constructively. * Key Tip: Choose advisors who have demonstrated maturity, wisdom, and empathy in similar circumstances. Listen objectively but ultimately trust your instincts when making decisions. Step 6: Evaluate Your Feelings Take time to reflect on whether you share the same level of admiration towards the person expressing interest. If so, consider exploring the possibility of deepening your relationship gradually and responsibly. * Guideline: Allow genuine connections to develop naturally over time instead of rushing into something serious due to external pressure or expectations. Step 7: Manage Rejection Gracefully Should you find yourself needing to turn down someone's affections, do so gently and tactfully. Express gratitude for their interest while emphasizing that you value them as a friend or colleague. * Key Tip: Refrain from being excessively critical or hurtful during rejections, as doing so could damage their self-esteem and strain your rapport. Conclusion: By following these steps, you'll learn how to handle admirers confidently and respectfully. Remember always to prioritize your well-being, set clear boundaries, and appreciate others' kindness without feeling obligated to reciprocate beyond your comfort zone. Title: How to Get Free Amazing Skins in Minecraft Are you tired of using the same old default skin in Minecraft? Want to stand out from the crowd and show off your unique style? Look no further! This detailed tutorial will guide you through the process of obtaining free amazing skins in Minecraft. By following these steps, you'll be able to customize your character with cool designs that reflect your personality. ### Part 1: Finding Free Skins Step 1: Research Reliable Websites The first step is to find reputable websites offering free Minecraft skins. There are many online resources available, but some may contain viruses or malware. To ensure safety, stick to well-known sites such as MinecraftSkins, Planet Minecraft, NameMC, or NovaSkin. These websites have large communities of players who share their creations freely. Key Tip: Always double-check the URL before downloading any files to prevent potential security risks. Step 2: Browse Available Skins Once you've chosen a reliable website, browse through the available categories to find a skin that suits your taste. Some popular themes include anime characters, superheroes, animals, and pop culture references. You can also search for specific keywords if you have something particular in mind. Guideline: Take your time exploring different options; this is an opportunity to express yourself within the game world! Step 3: Preview the Skin Before downloading a skin, preview it to see how it looks on a Minecraft character model. Most websites provide this feature, allowing you to rotate and zoom in on the skin to check every detail. Key Tip: Pay attention to both front and back views, as some creators might forget to design the backside of the skin. Step 4: Download the Skin After finding a suitable skin, click on the download button provided by the website. The file should automatically save onto your device as a .PNG image. Guideline: Keep track of where you saved the file, so you can locate it easily during the next steps. ### Part 2: Applying the Skin in Minecraft Step 5: Launch Minecraft Open the Minecraft launcher and log into your account. Select the version of Minecraft you want to play (Java Edition recommended for custom skins). Click 'Play' to enter the main menu. Step 6: Access Game Settings From the main menu, click on 'Options' followed by 'Skin Customization.' This will open a new window where you can upload your newly downloaded skin. Step 7: Upload Your Skin Click on the blank character model labeled 'Classic Skin' or 'Alex.' A browser window will appear, prompting you to select an image file. Navigate to where you saved the downloaded skin (.PNG format) and click 'Open.' Key Tip: Make sure the selected skin matches the required pixel dimensions (64x64 pixels). Step 8: Save Changes After successfully uploading your skin, click 'Confirm Skin' to apply the changes. Exit the settings menu and return to the main menu. Step 9: Start Playing Create a new world or join an existing one to show off your awesome new skin! By following these nine simple steps, you can now enjoy free amazing skins in Minecraft while expressing your individuality within the game world. Happy crafting! A common sight during the Cretaceous period was the soaring through the air of a large fur-covered creature called the pterosaur. Pterosaur means flying lizard. Wings of some pterosaurs were longer than the wings of a small plane. This creature lived on cliffs at the edge of lagoons and would sail from its nest to catch prey. The bones of one pterosaur, Scaphognathus crassirostris, were discovered in 1826 by the German scientist, August Goldfuss. The fossilized bones were located in a limestone quarry and were unbroken. Scaphognathus crassirostris was approximately the size of a large bat with a broad jaw and short tail. Students will reconstruct the skeleton of S. crassirostris and draw conclusions about its method of movement, feeding habits, and other adaptations. The brass section has four sections: trumpet, trombone, French horn, and tuba. Some of these come in several sizes. The article on transposing instruments explains more about it. The trumpet may have several slightly different sizes. The lowest kind is a bass trumpet. The trombone may be an alto, tenor, bass or contrabass trombone. The French horn, like the other brass instruments, has changed over the years. Modern horns have at least three valves and are usually in F. They often sit in a different place to the other brass. The tuba comes in different sizes and the player or conductor must decide which to use for the piece they are playing. There are large ones called contrabass tubas. A small tuba is commonly also seen and is called a euphonium or a baritone horn. Alright you want to sit up on my knee. I don't give money out in the store. Is that how you call somebody for dinner? Its location among the unsettled woodlands of the Wabash River valley also made it a key center of trade for fur trappers. In common speaking and geometry, a cone is a solid object that one gets when one rotates a right triangle around one of its two short sides, the cone's axis. The disk made by the other short side is called the base, and the point of the axis which is not on the base is the cone's "apex" or "vertex". An object that is shaped like a cone is "conical". XXX and then do I write artist? Title: Playing the Arithmetic Progression Game - A Fun Way to Learn Numbers! Hi Grade-Schoolers! Today we are going to learn about a fun math game called the "Arithmetic Progression Game." It's a game where you and your friend will take turns picking different numbers while trying to create a special sequence called an "arithmetic progression". Let's dive into this exciting game! **What is an Arithmetic Progression?** An arithmetic progression (AP) is a special sequence of numbers where each number increases by adding the same constant value every time. In other words, it's like climbing up a ladder with equal steps between each rung! For example, here's an AP with a common difference of 2: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12... You start at 4 and add 2 to get the next number, then keep doing that to generate more numbers in the series. **The Rules of Our Arithmetic Progression Game:** 1. We decide together beforehand how many numbers each person can pick per turn-let's say you can choose three numbers on your turn, and I can choose four numbers on mine. These numbers could change based on our agreement but should remain consistent throughout one round of the game. 2. Each number picked must be unique; no repeating any previously chosen numbers during the entire game. You may want to write down the numbers so neither player forgets their choices. 3. To win, try creating an AP with a certain predetermined length. For beginners, aim for making an AP with five consecutive numbers, just like our earlier example using twos as the step size. But remember, since both players contribute numbers to the board, either one can complete the AP! Here's how you could set up the beginning of a sample game: * Your turn (three numbers): ___, ____, ___ * My turn (four numbers): ___, ____ __, _____ Now, fill in the blanks with unique numbers and see who can build the required AP faster! Have fun exploring this cool way of learning about sequences and patterns through friendly competition. Encourage friends and siblings to join in too, taking turns being Players A and B. Enjoy practicing your new skills! And you can finish your root beer up later? I think have wind him so that he walk. The High and Low tide are caused by the gravitational forces between the Earth and the Moon. The Moon attracts every piece of matter on earth. Since the waters on the Moon side are attracted more strongly, they bulge towards the Moon, causing a High Tide. The waters on the opposite side of the Moon, bulge away from the Moon - also causing a High Tide. Low tides occur at about right angles to When the Moon is in its first quarter or its last quarter, the Sun's gravitational pull is in perpendicular direction to that of the Moon. The Sun pulls water away from the areas of High Tide to the areas of Low Tides, resulting in lower High Tides and higher Low Tides. These are called When the Moon is between the Sun and the Earth (at New Moon), the Sun's gravitational pull is in the same direction as the Moon's. During these days, the High Tides are higher and the Low Tides are lower than they would be with just the Moon's pull alone. This is called Spring Tide. The same thing happens when the Moon is on the opposite side of the Sun (Full Moon). The two gravitational forces work together to make high High Tides and low Low Tides. The Moon does not stay put, but rotates around the earth at a rate of about 12o a day, or one rotation a month. The rotation is in the same direction as the earth's spin, so by the time the earth has done one rotation, the moon has shifted 12o further, and it takes an extra 50 minutes for the Moon to be in the same position relative to a point on the Earth. Therefore, the tidal cycle is not 24 hours long, but 24 hours and 50 minutes. Because of this, High and Low tides are about 50 minutes later every day. A sieve is a way of getting rid of something you do not want. Then, you can keep what you want. This program will teach you how to use a special kind of sieve. This is a sieve in math, called the Sieve of Eratosthenes. I like m & m's, but I only like the red ones. When I get a package of m & m's, I get rid of all of the other colors, except red. So, I throw out the brown, yellow, orange, and blue ones. This mean I only have the red ones left. This is kind of what the Sieve of Eratosthenes does. This sieve can help you find a special kind of number, called a prime number. Prime numbers are numbers that can only be divided by 1 and itself. For instance, 2 is a prime number because 2 can only be divided by 1 and 2 evenly. Sometimes prime numbers can be hard to find. Rather than simply guessing at numbers and seeing if they are prime, Eratosthenes came up with a way to find prime numbers. He took a group of numbers and put them through his sieve. That way, he knew that he had found all of the prime numbers in that group of numbers. When doing the model, try to find the pattern. Then, you will understand how the Sieve of Eratosthenes works! Um and do you have any similar toys to these at home? Forgetting about the Jonas Brothers may be challenging due to their popularity and influence in the music industry. However, if you truly wish to move on from your interest in them, here's a step-by-step guide that can help: **Step 1: Acknowledge Your Interest** Before moving on, acknowledge why you were interested in the Jonas Brothers. Were they your favorite band? Did you have posters of them on your wall? Recognizing what initially drew you towards them will allow you to understand why you need or want to let go of this interest. This is an essential first step because understanding your feelings makes it easier to address them effectively. **Key Tip**: Remember, there's no shame in liking any artist or group; everyone has different tastes! **Step 2: Identify Why You Want To Move On** Is their type of music not resonating with you anymore? Or perhaps you've outgrown the fandom phase? Figuring out exactly why you feel ready to forget about them will provide direction as you proceed through these steps. **Guideline**: Take some time for introspection - journaling could be helpful here. **Step 3: Gradually Reduce Exposure** Start by unfollowing them on social media platforms. Unsubscribe from email updates, podcasts, blogs, etc., related to the Jonas Brothers. The idea behind gradually reducing exposure is similar to weaning off caffeine - sudden removal might lead to withdrawal symptoms like missing out or feeling lost. By doing it progressively, you give yourself time to adjust. **Step 4: Find New Interests** To fill the void left after removing something familiar, find new hobbies, interests, or even other musicians who align more closely with where you are now emotionally and mentally. Try exploring various genres and artists until you discover ones that genuinely excite you. **Key Tip**: Keep an open mind during this process. Sometimes our preferences change over time without us realizing it. **Step 5: Connect With Others Who Share Similar Sentiments** Join online communities, forums, or groups where people discuss letting go of past interests or discovering new ones. Sharing experiences and hearing others' stories can offer comfort and reassurance that you're not alone in this journey. **Step 6: Reflect On What You've Learned** Once you've successfully moved past your fascination with the Jonas Brothers, reflect on what this experience taught you. Maybe you learned about your taste in music, grew comfortable expressing your individuality, or discovered self-control when managing distractions. Appreciate these lessons as part of personal growth. **Step 7: Maintain Balance** Lastly, remember moderation in all things. While it's great to explore new interests, ensure you don't jump from one obsession to another. Allow room for variety and balance so that you enjoy multiple aspects of life rather than focusing solely on one thing. By following these steps, you should be able to shift your focus away from the Jonas Brothers and onto new endeavors. Just remember, it's perfectly okay to look back fondly at memories associated with them while embracing your current self and future possibilities. XXX can I have some more water? [overlap w/ S's next line at "-thing"] This day is the first day of the second third of the year, as well as the one third point of a common year because there are 121 days before and 243 days after it in common years. There are 122 days before and 243 after it in leap years. The exact time the second third of the year starts is 4:00 p.m. In countries that use summer time the actual exact time of the midpoint in a common year is at 5:00 p.m for locations in the northern hemisphere, or 3:00 p.m. for locations in the southern hemisphere; this is when 121 days and 16 hours have elapsed and there are 243 days and 8 hours remaining. In a leap year in those countries, the second third of the year starts at midnight. In countries that use summer time, the second third starts at 1:00 a.m. on May 2, or 11:00 p.m. on May 1 in the southern hemisphere. This is due to summer time having advanced the time by one hour. Creating a title page in MLA format may seem like a simple task, but there are specific guidelines that you need to follow to ensure your paper adheres to the Modern Language Association's formatting standards. This tutorial will provide an in-depth explanation of each step required to create a title page in MLA format. **Step 1: Open a New Document** Open your preferred word processing software (e.g., Microsoft Word, Google Docs) and start by creating a new document. This is where you will build your title page according to MLA guidelines. **Step 2: Set Margins and Font** Before adding any text, set the margins to one inch on all sides of the document. Next, select a clear and readable font such as Times New Roman or Arial, with a size of 12 points. These settings contribute to easy reading and professional appearance. **Step 3: Enter Your Full Name** Type your full name at the top center of the page. Make sure it appears about halfway down from the top margin. Writing your name here establishes ownership of the work. **Step 4: Insert Course Information** On separate lines below your name, add your instructor's name, course title, and date. Align these elements to the left side of the page. Including this information provides context regarding the assignment and its origin. Use the following formats: * Professor John Smith * English Composition 101 * Spring 2023 **Step 5: Center the Title** Skip a line after the date, then type your title centered on the page. Capitalize the first letter of every major word within the title, excluding articles (a, an, the), coordinating conjunctions (and, or, nor, etc.), and prepositions with fewer than four letters (at, by, in). If your title has two parts separated by a colon, only capitalize the initial word of the second part if it is a complete sentence; otherwise, do not capitalize it. For example: * Correct: The Impact of Social Media on Teenagers' Self-Esteem * Incorrect: The Effects Of Technology On Students : An Analysis **Step 6: Add a Page Number** At the upper right corner of the page, insert your last name followed by a space and the page number. This consistent header placement makes navigation through longer papers easier. Formatting a title page correctly in MLA can help present your academic work professionally while demonstrating attention to detail. By carefully executing each of these steps, you'll be well on your way to crafting an impressive title page that meets MLA requirements. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Gilliam/TD/11f/46882ca.cha = = = [FAT gets up and leaves the room] I think these are too small for you. LESSON 1: WHAT ARE ROCKS AND MINERALS? 1. What are rocks and minerals? 2. What are properties of rocks? 3. What are some vocabulary words associated with rocks and minerals? Ask students what they think of when they hear the word "rock". Elicit a discussion of what they know about rocks. Use their information to make a KWL chart like the one below. 1. Have students look at the following website to find out what a mineral is and what a rock is. The graphic shows that a rock is made of one or more minerals. Explain that is the mineral that gives a rock its properties. 2. Direct students to look at examples of rocks from the following website: http://ivyhall.district96.k12.il.us/4th/kkhp/RocksandMinerals/rocks.html Ask children to talk about what they see. Ask questions like: a. Are the colors all the same? b. Are they all as hard as each other? c. Do they look as they have the same texture? (feel) d. Do they shine? (luster) e. What do you think is the cause for all these differences? 3. Introduce vocabulary associated with rocks and minerals. Have children log on to http://m-w.com or http://cte.jhu.edu/techacademy/fellows/brannon/webquest/gloss.htm to find the definitions of the following words: For a student copy of the database, click here. 4. Distribute a set of three different rocks to each group of 4 students. Have them complete an observation sheet for each rock (See student samples and pictures). They are to describe as many properties as possible. (Color, texture, hardness, luster, cleavage). 1. Have your students bring in a rock they have found. They should list as many properties of the rock that they can. Asymmetric cryptography generally takes more time and requires more computer power, therefore it is not used most of the time. Instead, it is often used for computer signatures, when a computer must know that some data (like a file or a website) was sent from a certain sender. Title: How to Get StartaryPig for Android SmartyPig is a goal-based savings app that allows you to save money towards specific financial goals while earning interest and receiving contributions from friends and family. By following this detailed tutorial, you will learn how to get SmartyPig for your Android device and start saving smarter! **Step 1:** Unlock Your Device Start by unlocking your Android device using either fingerprint recognition, pattern, PIN or password. This ensures your privacy remains protected as you download new apps. **Step 2:** Open Google Play Store Tap on the Google Play Store icon (usually displayed with a multicolored triangle) on your home screen or app drawer to open the store. The Google Play Store provides access to millions of applications including SmartyPig. **Step 3:** Search for SmartyPig Inside the Google Play Store, tap on the search bar at the top of the screen and type 'SmartyPig' without quotes. From the list of suggestions, select the official SmartyPig application developed by Sallie Mae Bank. Make sure the developer name matches exactly before proceeding. **Step 4:** Review Details & Compatibility Carefully read through the description provided, check user reviews, ratings and screenshots to ensure the app meets your needs. Verify compatibility by looking under 'App details', checking if your device version and requirements are met. **Step 5:** Install Application Once satisfied with all information gathered about the app, press the green 'Install' button located near the top right corner of the page. Wait for the installation process to complete - this may take several minutes depending upon internet speed. You might be asked permission during installation; allow these permissions to enable full functionality. **Step 6:** Create an Account Launch the newly installed SmartyPig app from your app drawer. If prompted, accept terms and conditions. Then choose whether to sign up via email or Facebook account. Fill out necessary fields such as name, date of birth, address etc., creating strong unique login credentials. Remember, never share sensitive personal data over unsecured WiFi networks. **Step 7:** Set Financial Goals After successful registration, set up one or more financial goals based on what you want to save for (e.g., vacation, wedding, car). Provide relevant names, target amounts, estimated completion dates, and pictures related to each goal. Setting clear targets can help motivate you throughout the savings journey. **Step 8:** Choose Savings Plan Decide how much you wish to contribute regularly toward achieving each goal. Select weekly, biweekly, or monthly intervals according to your pay schedule and budget constraints. Consistent small contributions add up quickly and earn higher interest rates compared to traditional savings methods. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always double-check the legitimacy of any unfamiliar apps before installing them to avoid potential security risks. * Regularly update the SmartyPig app when updates become available to benefit from bug fixes and improved features. * Consider linking only one bank account to prevent overspending and maintain better control over finances. * Monitor progress frequently and adjust saving plans accordingly. Celebrating milestones along the way boosts motivation levels. By following these steps and guidelines, you should now have successfully gotten started with SmartyPig on your Android device! Title: How to Enable the Dark Theme on Google's Calculator App Google's Calculator app is a handy tool for performing mathematical calculations, but did you know that you can change its appearance to a dark theme? This can be particularly useful if you prefer a darker interface or if you want to save battery life on your device with an OLED screen. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to enable the dark theme on Google's Calculator app. Step 1: Open the Settings Application The first step is to open the settings application on your Android device. The settings app is usually represented by a gear icon and can be found in your app drawer or on your home screen. Opening the settings app will allow you to access the display settings, which include the option to enable the dark theme. Key Tip: If you are having trouble finding the settings app, try using the search function on your device. Simply swipe down from the top of your screen and type "settings" into the search bar. Step 2: Access Display Settings Once you have opened the settings app, scroll through the list of options until you find "Display." Tap on this option to access the display settings. Step 3: Select Dark Mode In the display settings, look for the "Dark mode" option. This may also be labeled as "Night mode" or "Dark theme," depending on your device and version of Android. Tap on this option to enable the dark theme. Key Guideline: Enabling dark mode will change the overall color scheme of your device to a darker palette, including the calculator app. However, some apps may not support dark mode, so the calculator app may still appear light even after enabling dark mode. To ensure that the calculator app appears in dark mode, continue to the next steps. Step 4: Open the Calculator App After enabling dark mode, open the calculator app. You should notice that the app now has a dark background and lighter text. Key Tip: If the calculator app does not appear in dark mode, close the app and reopen it. Sometimes, apps need to be restarted before changes take effect. Step 5: Check for Updates If the calculator app still does not appear in dark mode, check for updates to the app in the Google Play Store. Developers often release updates to add new features and improve compatibility with system settings like dark mode. To check for updates, open the Google Play Store app and tap on the menu icon (three horizontal lines) in the upper left corner. From there, select "My apps & games" and look for any available updates to the calculator app. Step 6: Contact Support If the calculator app still does not appear in dark mode after checking for updates, consider contacting Google's support team for further assistance. They may be able to provide additional guidance or troubleshooting steps. By following these steps, you should be able to successfully enable the dark theme on Google's Calculator app. Not only will this provide a more comfortable viewing experience, but it can also help extend the battery life of devices with OLED screens. Happy calculating! [FAT's name on cake? Is same as Bro's] Put that one [washer] there [on a nut on the black base]. Some geometric terms and results used in terms of words. Some Geometric Terms and Results: The sum of all the angles at a point is 360 . i.e., 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 6 = 360 The sum of all the angles about a point on a straight line on one side of if it is 180 . i.e., 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 = 360 Some Important Geometric Terms Used: 1. Equal angles: Two angles are said to be equal if they have the same degree measure. MNO and XYZ are equal angles of measure 90 . 2. Bisector of an Angle: The ray which divides the given angle into two equal angles is called an angle bisector. In the adjoining figure, the ray BD divides ABC into two equal angles ABD and DBC i.e., ABD = DBC. Two lines in a plane are said to be perpendicular if they intersect in such a way that the angles formed between them are right angles. In the adjoining e, lines PQ and RS intersect at 0 such that ROQ = ROP = POS = QOS = 90 . Therefore, we say that PQ is perpendicular to RS, i.e., (PQ RS). It is the line which passes through the midpoint of the given line segment and is also perpendicular to it. Here, MN is the line segment. PQ is the perpendicular bisector as POM = PON = 90 and MO = ON. Some geometric terms and results are explained along with the specific figure. Today, we're going to learn about something called "slew rate" in a fun and easy way. You know how when you swing a ball on a string really fast, it makes a noise? That sound is similar to what happens inside some electronic devices like an operational amplifier or "op-amp." An op-amp is a super helpful tool for engineers since it can make their jobs easier by doing math with electricity! But sometimes, things move too quickly for our little friend, causing it to make a sound or create distorted signals - just like when you try to spin a top really fast and it wobbles instead of standing up straight. Now imagine you want to play your favorite song using these electrical signals but because of the quick movements, the music comes out all messed up. To prevent that, we need to pay attention to something called the "slew rate," which tells us how fast the signal can change without creating those unwanted sounds or distortions. Think of the slew rate as the speed limit for our op-amp signals; if they go over that limit, there will be trouble! By knowing the slew rate, designers can choose components that work together smoothly so your songs will always sound great. So remember, when working with electronics, keep an eye on the slew rate to ensure everything runs smoothly and creates beautiful sounds instead of unpleasant noises! Play starts when a player from one team kicks the ball from the halfway line towards the opposing team's field goal. Play continues until a try is scored, the ball crosses the side line or dead ball line, or an infringement occurs. The ball can be moved up the field either by carrying or kicking it. The ball can be passed between team members, but it cannot be passed to team members who are closer to the opposing team's goal than the person who is currently carrying the ball. Hurry up and put your food on here. Rhyme and Music Remember how in "Line and Meter" last week we encountered "alliteration" when we were discussing Old English accentual verse? Alliteration is only one of several ways to produce "music" in poetry. Let's use Gwendolyn Brooks's poem "We Real Cool" (Strong Measures 38) to illustrate some of these methods. Here's the whole poem: Lots of alliteration here. Remember? Repeated sounds at the beginnings of words? Like the "l" sounds in "Lurk late" or the "str" repetition in "Strike straight."WE REAL COOLThe Pool Players. Obviously, there's lots of rhyme too. Such as "cool. We" and "school. We" or "sin. We" and "gin. We" ... right? Besides these "external rhymes" (those at the ends of lines), there are also "internal rhymes" (repetition of endings in the middle of lines), for example, "sin" with "Thin." So far we have been pointing to "full rhyme" (also known as "rich rhyme"); there are also examples of "half rhyme" (sometimes called "slant rhyme"), such as "sing" and "Thin" in the middle of the next line or "real" and "school" in the first two lines. Of the more familiar rhyme types, the one conspicuously missing is "eye rhyme" where two words look like they ought to rhyme fully but don't. Such as "full" and "lull" or "door" and "poor." Perhaps, though, Brooks wanted a deterministic pattern of sound that eye rhyme would not have enhanced. Other kinds of sound effects have to do with the type of sound. For example, the repetition of vowels, called "assonance." Note the short "i" sound in "Sing sin" repeated again in the next line. The echoing of consonants is called "consonance." In the opening lines of the poem, we can see the preponderance of "l" sounds. The "s" introduced in the word "school" reverberates in "Strike," "straight," "Sing," "sin," and more distantly in "Thin." The soft "g" or "j" sound in "gin" is echoed by "Jazz" and "June." Even more interesting is Brooks's use of "pararhyme," also called "rich consonance." Notice how "l" and "t" in that order occur in "left" and "late." Or observe how "j" is paralleled with "d" and "z" with "s" in "Jazz" and "Die s(oon)" ... fascinating. The acknowledged master of pararhyme is Wilfred Owen. Check out the opening lines of his poem "Strange Meeting": The poem goes on, but this should be enough to make the point. Brilliant pararhyming, don't you think? His rhyme words here consistently use a different vowel between identical consonants. Wow. Notice too that, to up the ante a bit, Owen uses heroic couplets.It seemed that out of battle I escaped Down some profound dull tunnel, long since scooped Through granites which titanic wars had groined. Yet also there encumbered sleepers groaned, Too fast in thought or death to be bestirred. Then, as I probed them, one sprang up, and stared Okay, so what? What rhyme and music do is contribute to a poem's meaning by providing sonic texture and unity -- an impression that the poem is an interwoven whole of sound and sense. A poem's music, in great part, tells us that that poem is a poem. The play of sound is often what seems immediately "poetic" to the reader. Rhyme and music are often inextricably tied to form as well. Brooks, for example, uses the couplet exclusively in her poem. This stanza is built primarily from rhyme. Although meter may often be involved in a couplet, the major defining characteristic is that the two lines rhyme. Now it's your turn. Find a poem that illustrates the methods of rhyme and music. Title: How to Perform the Crayon/Colored Pencil Magic Trick Introduction: The crayon or colored pencil magic trick is an easy yet impressive illusion that can be performed with basic stationery items. This guide will walk you through the process of executing this trick step-by-step, providing clear explanations and helpful tips along the way. Materials Needed: * A standard crayon or colored pencil (preferably one with a label) * Scissors * Tape Step 1: Choose Your Color Select a crayon or colored pencil of your choice. It's essential to pick one that has a visible label wrapped around its body. The color should ideally have some contrast against the background surface where you plan to perform the trick. Key Tip: Avoid using white or light-colored crayons as they may blend into the performance surface and make the trick less noticeable. Step 2: Cutting the Crayon Using scissors, carefully cut off about half an inch from one end of the crayon without removing the label. Make sure to keep both pieces intact - do not separate them entirely. You want the paper label to remain continuous throughout the entire length of the crayon. Guideline: Be cautious while handling sharp objects like scissors. Always prioritize safety when performing any magic tricks. Step 3: Preparing the Second Half Take the second half of the crayon and flip it over so that the exposed part faces downwards. Now, align the two halves perfectly, ensuring that the label appears unbroken and seamless. Key Tip: Pay attention to alignment. Misaligned labels could give away the secret behind the trick. Step 4: Securing the Halves Together Tightly wrap a piece of tape around the connected area of the two crayon halves. Press firmly to ensure a snug fit and smooth out any air bubbles or wrinkles in the tape. Step 5: Testing the Illusion Hold up the 'magic' crayon by gripping it near the taped section. Showcase it against different surfaces such as paper, cloth, or skin. Demand someone to choose a color and then dramatically reveal their chosen hue after sliding the crayon along the selected surface. Explanation: Since the top portion of the crayon remains unchanged, it gives the appearance of having magically transformed into the chosen color once revealed. However, due to the continuity of the label, the audience perceives it as a single unit despite being composed of two separate parts. Additional Tips: * Practice makes perfect. Rehearse the trick until you become comfortable with the movements involved. * Engage your audience by asking questions and encouraging participation. * Maintain eye contact and display confidence during the execution of the trick. * If possible, practice in front of mirrors or record yourself to identify areas needing improvement. The girl was embarrassed and she ran away. When she got back home, she told her mom what had happened. Appearing offline in Facebook Messenger is a great way to browse through your contacts or check messages without revealing your online status. It's especially useful when you want some private time away from conversations or if you simply wish to avoid being disturbed. Here's an easy-to-follow, step-by-step guide on how to appear offline in Facebook Messenger: ### 1. Open the Facebook Messenger app Start by opening the Facebook Messenger app on your mobile device (Android or iOS) or accessing the web version via your preferred browser. This will take you to the main interface where all your recent chats are displayed. Having the app open allows you to make changes quickly and efficiently. ### 2. Navigate to the Active section On the mobile app, tap on the "People" tab at the bottom of your screen, then select "Active." On the desktop site, look for the gear icon next to the word "Chats" near the top left corner of the window; click on this to reveal more options and choose "Active Contacts." The Active section shows who among your friends is currently available on Facebook Messenger. Key tip: If someone appears as active but they haven't responded to your message(s), they might have turned on their 'Always Appear Online' setting. However, don't assume that they're ignoring you - there could be various reasons why they aren't responding right now. Guideline: Be respectful of others' privacy settings even when they appear online. Don't feel entitled to immediate responses just because someone is showing up as active. ### 3. Toggle off your visibility To appear offline, locate your name within the list of active users. Once found, tap on your name to bring up additional options. You should see a button or slider labeled "Turn off Chat" or something similar. Slide or toggle this switch to the OFF position. A confirmation dialog may pop up asking if you'd like to turn off chat for everyone or only for some people; choose accordingly based on your preference. Key Tip: Turning off chat does NOT log you out of Facebook Messenger. Instead, it hides your online presence while keeping you connected to the platform. Guideline: Remember that appearing offline doesn't mean invisible. Some actions, such as sending messages or reacting to posts, can still reveal your activity to others. ### 4. Check your new status After successfully turning off your chat, double-check that everything went smoothly. Look for your name in the Active section again; if done correctly, it shouldn't be visible anymore. Additionally, verify that the green dot indicating online status has disappeared beside your profile picture throughout the app or website. Key Tip: Keep in mind that some devices cache information, so it might take a few moments before your updated status becomes apparent across all platforms and devices. Guideline: Although you've appeared offline, continue practicing good digital etiquette. Respond to messages when convenient, and remember that maintaining healthy relationships requires communication and consideration. In conclusion, following these steps will help you master the art of appearing offline in Facebook Messenger. By doing so, you create boundaries around your availability and ensure a better balance between staying connected with friends and having personal space. Enjoy browsing peacefully! A LOCAL WAY OF LEARNING Title: ROCKY SHORE ECOSYSTEM Author: Jane Ducey Eulalie R. Rivera Elementary School Grade Level: 4-12 1. Energy 1. Social Studies 2. Ecosystem 2. Science 3. Carrying Capacity 3. Language Arts 4. Clean Water 4. Art 7. Land Use 8. Values and Attitudes Student shall identify the zonation of the beach and tell which animals and plants are found where and why. Student shall explain the adaptations that have taken place to enable the organism to live where it does. Student shall become familiar with the transectt" technique. The carbonate beach rock exposed in the intertidal zone may either be abrupt or be interspersed with sandy mini beaches, providing nooks in which plants and animals are found. This is also the place where tide pools are common. There is a zonation where you may expect to find particular animals and plants. Microscopic algae serves as food for many animals whose "mouth" is on the bottom. For others the ever renewing splash of water brings new source of food with each wave: they have "mouths" on the top. Most are anchored securely against the wave action. Most have great tolerance for extremes of salinity and temperature--if it rains-a tide pool is almost fresh water--if the wave action is low the pool dries up and salinity and temperature go way up. Butwatch your step, those black tar balls are still soft. . Sneakers to walk in water for foot protection, sketch pad and pencil. In class preparation: Introduce as many casts of crabs and dead shells of other animals you expect to find. See movies, look at books of rocky shore and intertidal plants and animals. Note that some "exoskeleton" animals (crab, lobster) must cast their hard outer covering to grow, whereas other shells (snail, conch, clam) grow an additional section of their shell when they become crowded in the present one. Note the hermit crab, who has no shell on his own posterior and must drag around the dead shell of another, usually a West Indian top shell, to protect his soft "tail". This requires a regular search for a slightly larger shell as the ROCKY SHORE ECOSYSTEM Activities at the site: Note the color change from black, at the top of the rock (blue-green algae) to pink at the water's edge. On the high rocks and on grass and weeds are the beaded periwinkles (Tectarius muricatus). This is a marine snail which shows extreme adaptation. On the high beach during a dry season the animal pulls himself deep in his shell to preserve moisture, seals off his door operculumm) and also seals his shell rim to the rocky substrate. It has been shown to be able to live for 18 months in this dried up state and still revive when put into 1. Mark a shell with a dot of red finger nail polish, draw a ring on the rock around the snail with the same polish. Come back, days, weeks, later and see how much the snail moved. (Check him out a year later). The snail stays within a radius of a few feet. Pour some water on a patch of snails and watch for a while. (Almost immediately they begin to move a little). 2. What does the periwinkle eat? Scrape some of the "black" (blue-green algae) off the rock, wet it, and look at it under a microscope. (Looks like a golf 3. How does the snail reproduce? (Lays eggs at the water's edge so the surge can take the hatchlinqs (Veligers) into the sea (plankton). This is probably the longest trip Tectarius makes from the high beach. 4. Make an estimate of the number of snails (probably the greatest resident of the rocky shore in numbers). This is done by making a transect. Pull a line, as perpendicular to the shore as you can, from the high beach to the water's edge. Using a square measure (a stiff wire bent to close a square) of known size--foot, quarter meter--flip it over and over down the beach and count the snails in every fifth (or another interval, depending upon the width of the beach) square. Multiply the total by the number of squares in all. Example: If you counted five squares and there were 25 in all, you will multiply the total number of snails counted by five to estimate the whole strip. Don't overlook all the immature snails stuck tightly underneath the rocky outcrops. 5. Closer to the water line you find a pointed shell with zigzag lines (Littorina ziczac). At the splash line another snail, the nerite or tooth shell is found. N. peloronta has a red mark (bleeding tooth) the other two do not. These snails are edible if you have the patience. 6. Just below the water line is the West Indian top shell of "wilk" (Cittarium pica) and is of a size such that a few make a worthwhile meal. The above are 7. At the high water mark you will find a meat eater, Purpera patula. If prodded the animal will excrete a smelly white substance which stains skin purple. Put the Purpera in a pool with other snails and watch them leave. 8. Find some limpets and some keyhole limpets--why do you think they are so named? Also at the splash zone you will see an animal not changed since the beginning of time. It is the chiton and there are three, depending upon the kind of girdle it has. The animal looks like it's stuck in a rock hollow forever. It has eight separate plates in its shell. Don't paint it with polish--it has pores on the top of its shell. 9. Look for casts of crabs (Grapsus grapsus) with their claws still gripped to the rock to pull themselves out. Those short-spined sea eggs are rock boring, red or black, urchins (Echinometra lucunter) and they dig the hole they are sitting in. Barnacles may be feeding if the water is surging over them. They whip out their feeding "plumes" to catch whatever is in the water. 10. Slightly lower on the beach you will find two other urchins, the white one you can safely pick up (Tripneustes) and the long spined black one (Diadema) Note the weed and shell bits the white one has stuck on his shell: how does it do this? (Has tube feet between the spines). Pick it up and hold it at the water level. Feel the tube feet with the suction cups hold on to you-in this way the animal avoids being swept by the surf. The five zones are the surf zone, pink zone, green zone, yellow zone and the black zone, according to Lewis. From land to sea, below the black zone and still above the high water mark is the yellow zone which has a thick film of algae. It is here that the nerites are found. The green zone is covered by coralline algae and is smooth--here you will find Purpera. The pink zone extends between the mean low water mark and the mean high water mark (area of the wilks). The pink is also an encrustation of coralline algae. In the surf zone are the algae Halimeda, Dictyota, Padina, Laurencia, Turbinaria and STa ain ass5rS Tide pools have a changing community as the big waves bring new fish or give access for others to return to the sea. Tiny hermits scurry around. Frequently an octopus gets carried into the pool by a big surge of water. Observina his color change (chromatophores) you can see how good they are at camouflaging themselves. Also observe the use of appendages curled up to 'walk' and the precise timing to catch the next big wave to get back into the sea. Guidebook to the Geology and Ecology of St. Croix H. As part of our Eric Carle bug unit, we made click beetles and used them with our telling of The Very Clumsy Click Beetle. For a tutorial on how to make them, click here. The kids had a great time decorating their click beetles and then making them flip. Then, we used our click beetles for some math activities. 1. Probability - First, we talked about making predictions and predicted whether we thought the click beetle was more likely to land on his back or his feet. (Since there are only two options, he is equally likely to land on each side.) 2. Addition, Subtraction, and Math Vocabulary - Then, each child got a chance to click each beetle ten times and we kept track of how many times the beetle landed on his back and how many times he landed on his feet. After each child went, we answered questions using more, less, total, and equal to. For example, "How many more times did _____'s beetle land on it's back than it's feet?", "What was the total number of times the beetle landed on it's back?", "Whose beetle landed on it's back the most?", etc. This is a great opportunity to introduce kids to math vocabulary and get them used to using these math terms. If you're a teacher, you could also use these in your classroom for mean, median, and mode lessons. As a side note, using chart paper and making lists like this is a great way for preschoolers and young kids to begin recognizing their name and numbers. The kids returned to our chart throughout the week to point out their names and how many times their click beetle had landed on his back and feet. It always makes me happy to see how excited and engaged the little ones get whenever I pull out the chart paper for a more formal lesson. They love the feeling of being "in school" at home :) May I ask if you intend to act for him in a professional capacity?" "I do not. This is a picture of a galaxy in visible light. A galaxy is a large number of stars, some like our sun, some bigger, some smaller and all moving together through space. This galaxy is called Centaurus A. Scientists were surprised when they looked at Centaurus A in other kinds of light. What they saw is evidence for a large jet of gas that is squirting out the top and bottom of the galaxy. The particles making up the jet give off light at radio and ultraviolet wavelengths. We can see the jet in these other types of light but it cannot be seen in visible light. If scientists had not looked at light beyond the range of our vision, we would never known that these types of jets existed. Scientists believe something very violent is happening at the heart of Centaurus A. At the center of the galaxy resides a supermassive black hole. When stars run out of hydrogen fuel, the gas in their outer layers falls inward on top of the gas at the center. This infalling gas crushes everthing below it. Soon the star has been compressed into a very small object. But this object has such strong gravity that not even light can escape from it. This object is called a black hole because it is totally black -- we cannot see it. Other stars can fall into the black hole and also be crushed. When this happens, the black hole gets bigger and more powerful. The black hole at the center of Centaurus A is spinning. Some process associated with the spinning, which is not yet understood, produces a large jet of gas. Click on the buttons to see how Centaurus A looks in other types of light. Mama and Jack were very excited. They had just moved into a new house. Jack was only three so he couldn't help much, but he watched as Mama unpacked all their boxes. He saw clothes and toys, books and games. When Mama asked him what he was most excited about, he smiled and said, "I'm excited to make friends here!" But when they opened up one of the boxes, he saw something strange - it was a stiff, white jacket. Jack was confused. "What is this thing?" he asked. Mama smiled sadly. "It's a cold jacket," she said. Jack frowned. "Why's it here?" he asked. Mama looked at Jack with deep sadness in her eyes. She told him that the jacket had belonged to Daddy, but he had moved on to a better place. She hugged Jack and said, "It's okay to be sad, but Daddy is always with us in our hearts." Jack looked at the cold jacket and made a silent promise that he would never forget Daddy. He knew that no matter where he moved, he would always remember. That's where I in between the two trees? Caffeine is a "stimulant" drug. A stimulant is a drug that increases body actions like heart rate, blood pressure, and metabolism. It makes a person more awake and alert. - Students will learn about the things that plants need to grow and the plant parts associated with it. - Students will match pictures to printed words about the four things plants need to grow. - Color cards (one sheet of blue "water"; one sheet of yellow "sun"; one sheet of white "air"; and one sheet of brown "soil". Laminate cards if possible). - Four cones to create boundaries - Watering can - Flats for planting seeds - Radish seeds - Small potted plants (to show students roots) - Early Fall or early Spring Plants need water, air, nutrients, and sunlight to grow. The energy from sunlight is used to chemically change water and carbon dioxide (air) into sugar. This process is called photosynthesis. A waste product from photosynthesis is oxygen. Water is also essential for a healthy plant cell to function. Plants need nutrients to perform metabolic processes. They get nutrients through the soil. They are absorbed through their roots and transported through the plant's vascular system (the xylem and the phloem). Nutrients: things we get from the food we eat that helps our bodies grow and stay healthy. Plants also need nutrients to grow. They get their nutrients from the soil. Introduce the idea that plants need 4 different things to grow. Show them a picture of the 4 things. Have the students say what each of those things are together as well as a hand or body motion. Review these items a few times together. Ask for a raised hand to share which of those things do people NOT need (directly) to grow- the sun! Part 1- Play the Card Game Create a boundary for students to play in. It should be large enough for everyone to have space, but also small enough so that some students need to be close together. Have some students help demonstrate how to play the game. You will throw the colored cards out at randomly inside the boundaries. When you say "go", students will pick up 4 different colored cards: 1-yellow (sun); 1-blue (water); 1- brown (soil); 1 white (air)- without moving their feet. (Explain that since the students are plants, they cannot move). Ask students if they know what each color represents and have them share with the group. Record what each card represents on a board. Once they've picked up the 4 cards, they can hold it up. If they were not able to pick up all 4 colors, they did not survive to the next round and can help you collect cards. (Make sure you have enough cards for students to survive at least the first 2 rounds). Ask students to put their cards in their pocket to show you they are ready for the next round to begin. Continue playing until there is only 1 winner. Part 2- Help the plants grow! Divide the students into 3 groups and have them rotate: "Air"- Give plants air by blowing on their leaves. "Nutrients"- Plants get their nutrients through the soil. Have students take a small handful of soil and gently place it around the base of the plant. Be sure to model this for the students so the leaves do not become covered with soil. "Water"- Have a watering can available and full. Help students water a plant. Count to 5 to encourage students to give the plant enough water. Note: It is a good idea to also mention that plants needs sunlight, although we're not having a "sun" group because we'll let the sun do its own work. Part 3- Sun Experiment (Optional) Have students help plant radish seeds in small flat. You will need two flats. Help students space out the seeds by having popsicle sticks, or something to mark where the last seed was placed, and a ruler to measure where the next seed will go. Place one flat in a sunny place. Place the other in a dark closet Have students help water both flats. Make sure they get equal amounts of water. Watch each flat grow and compare. You can transplant the flat that has been in the sun with students in the garden. Wrap Up & Assessment Review with students the 4 things plants need to grow and why the plants in the closet didn't grow (if you did the experiment). Review the 4 things while doing the hand motion and noises. Ask students to keep track of how many times you say the things plants need while reading the story. Have students remain seated at their desks while you read. Show students how to keep track of by keeping a tally. Have students add to their Plant K-W-L chart what they learned. Create an experiment similar to the one above, but this time you experiment with water. Both flats will be in the sun, but only water one of the groups of plants to show students what will happen (or what won't happen!). Have students predict what they think will happen to the different groups of plants. Students can continue to apply what they've learned by planting a sun sensitive plant in a pot. Marigolds are a good example. Students can observe the plant growing towards the sun. Turn around the pot and watch the plant grow in the opposite direction, towards the sun. The volume of the human brain (relative to the size of the whole body) is very large, compared to that of most other animals. The human brain also has a very large surface (called cortex) for its size, which is possible because it is very wrinkled. If the human cortex were flattened, it would be close to a square meter in area. Some other animals also have very wrinkled brains, such as dolphins and elephants. Here is a rule of thumb: the larger an animal is, the larger its brain will be. Even allowing for that, the human brain, and in particular the neocortex, is very large. We know it increased in size four-fold over the last several million years of evolution. There are ideas about why this happened, but no-one is quite sure. Most theories suggest complex social activity and the evolution of language would make a larger brain advantageous. As an additional note, Einstein's brain weighed only 1,230 grams, which is less than the average adult male brain (about 1,400 grams). The detailed organisation of a brain obviously matters, but in ways which are not understood at present. [9 sec; S squeals then laughs; D tickling him?] **Title: How to Cite Using the Harvard Style** The Harvard referencing system is widely used in academic writing across various disciplines. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of citing sources using this format, ensuring that your work complies with scholarly standards and giving credit where it's due. ### Step 1: Understand the Basics of Harvard Referencing Harvard referencing consists of two components: in-text citations (within your paper) and an accompanying reference list at the end of your document. The purpose of these elements is to provide enough information about your source so that interested readers can locate and read them independently. ### Step 2: Learn How to Format Your In-Text Citations In-text citations should be included whenever you quote or paraphrase someone else's ideas or research findings. They typically include the author(s)' surname(s), followed by the year of publication in brackets. For example: * According to Smith (2018), climate change poses significant threats... * ...which could lead to catastrophic consequences (Smith, 2018). If there are two authors, both names must always be mentioned in the order they appear in the original source: (Brown & Green, 2020). If three or more authors are involved, only the first author's name is cited followed by 'et al.': (Jones et al., 2019). No [laughs] you give the baby some? Title: How to Block Cookies on an iPhone Introduction: Cookies are small pieces of data stored on your device by websites you visit. While they can be helpful for saving login information or personalizing your web experience, they can also compromise your privacy. This tutorial will guide you through blocking cookies on your iPhone using Safari's built-in settings. Step 1: Open Settings App Find the gray app with gears icon typically located on your home screen. Tap to open. Key Tip: If you cannot find the Settings app, swipe down on any home screen and type 'Settings' into the search bar at the top. Step 2: Scroll Down & Tap Safari Scroll down until you see 'Safari'. It should appear under 'Passwords & Accounts' but above 'Notes'. Select this option. Guideline: The order of apps may vary based on usage frequency or recent access. Step 3: Tap on Privacy & Security Once in Safari settings, scroll down until you reach 'Privacy & Security'. It is usually near the bottom of the page. Step 4: Enable Block All Cookies Under 'Privacy & Security', locate the section titled 'Cookies' which contains a toggle switch next to 'Block All Cookies'. Slide the button to the right so that it turns green. Explanation: By enabling this feature, no cookies from any website will be saved onto your phone while browsing with Safari. However, please note that some websites might not function optimally without cookie support. Key Tips: - Certain sites require cookies to remember user preferences or maintain functionality. Consider creating exceptions for these trusted sites. - Regularly clearing cache and site data can help manage unwanted cookies without completely disabling them. Remember, blocking all cookies provides maximum privacy protection but could potentially limit certain features of some websites. Adjust as needed according to your comfort level and browsing habits. Enjoy safe surfing! He will 'cause Kent's going to give him the tape. Who runs snip snap around the corner between the bottles getting closer? So that the razor will cut the beard. [picks up the book to show Bea] Title: Get Through the Checkout Line at the Supermarket More Quickly Introduction: Waiting in line at the supermarket can be time-consuming and frustrating. However, with some planning and strategic thinking, you can significantly reduce your waiting time and get through the checkout line more quickly. This comprehensive guide will provide you with valuable insights, actionable steps, and useful tips to help you optimize your shopping experience. Step 1: Choose the Right Time to Shop Time is money, so plan your grocery trips wisely. Avoid peak hours such as weekday evenings, weekends, or any other times when working individuals and families are likely to shop. Instead, opt for less crowded periods like early mornings, late afternoons, or during regular work hours if possible. By doing this, you'll encounter shorter lines and faster service. Key tip: If you have access to store apps that display real-time crowd levels, take advantage of them! They can give you an accurate idea of which times are busiest and allow you to choose the optimal window for shopping. Step 2: Prepare Your Groceries Beforehand Before joining the queue, ensure all items in your cart are ready for scanning. Remove excess packaging, separate multiples (e.g., place individual apples instead of bunching them together), organize similar products together, and remove any loose produce stickers. This organization saves both your time and the cashier's by reducing fumbling around and searching for missing barcodes. Key guideline: Keep reusable bags handy but avoid unloading them until instructed by the cashier. This prevents clutter near the register and speeds up the overall process. Step 3: Select the Appropriate Lane Select the shortest lane based on available registers, self-checkouts, and express lanes. While evaluating options, consider factors like customer volume, efficiency, and speed of the cashiers. Additionally, weigh whether using self-checkout would save time given the size and nature of your groceries. Self-checkout often expedites the process for smaller purchases without requiring assistance from staff members. Key tip: When assessing different lanes, pay attention to customers who appear close to completing their transactions. Joining behind someone about to finish ensures minimal wait time between them and the next shopper. Step 4: Use Payment Methods That Speed Up Transactions Paying with exact change, credit cards, mobile wallets, or digital payment platforms typically streamline transactions compared to checks or requesting cash back. These methods minimize manual calculations and paperwork involved in processing payments. Consequently, they contribute to quicker turnaround times per customer. Key guideline: Familiarize yourself with contactless payment systems integrated into stores' card terminals. Many retailers now support tap-to-pay technology via smartphones or wearables, further accelerating payment processes. Step 5: Load Bags Efficiently Once prompted by the cashier, swiftly load your bags while maintaining accuracy and avoiding damage to fragile items. Place heavier goods at the bottom and lighter ones on top, ensuring proper weight distribution. As you pack, keep one hand free to receive additional items being scanned and handed over by the cashier. Key tip: Utilizing compact yet sturdy bags facilitates efficient loading and reduces potential spillage or tearing incidents caused by excessive bulk. Conclusion: By following these five steps outlined above, you're well equipped to navigate supermarket queues efficiently and effectively. With practice, patience, and adherence to our recommended strategies, you'll soon find yourself spending less time in line and enjoying more freedom for other activities. Happy shopping! 'Superplasticizers' are chemicals which improve workability, which means the ability to shape the concrete before it has set. Pigments can change the dull grey colour. Corrosion inhibitors can reduce rusting on the steel bars. 'Air entrainment' is blowing little bubbles in the concrete before it sets. The singin' I will sing along win them. 1.Whales are the largest animals that have ever lived on earth and are the largest animals that live in the ocean. Whales are even bigger than the largest dinosaur. It is believed that millions of years ago, whales probably walked upon land. Their back legs disappeared and their front legs became flippers. Blue whales can weigh over 150 tons and be over 100 feet in length. Humpback Whales are also big, weighing up to 45 tons. Flippers of the humpback whale can be as long as 15 feet. 2. Whales are mammals, so they feed milk to their babies and breathe air. Since whales are not fish they do not have gills, so they cannot breathe under water. They must come up to the surface of the water to get air. The air is breathed in and out through their "blowhole," which is on their back. 3. Whales live in large groups called "herds." A baby whale is called a "calf." 4. Blue whales and killer whales can be found in every ocean around the world. Whales "migrate" further than any other animal. They eat during the summer month building up layers of blubber. When the water begins to cool, the whales begin their migration to warmer waters. They do not eat during their migration. All they do is swim and rest for short periods of time. Sometimes, when whales are migrating, they swim very close to the shore and can be seen "blowing" and jumping out of the water. This jumping is called "breaching. 5. When whales sleep, they stay at the top of the water, with their blowhole above the surface. Sometimes, a whale will swim up to the surface of the water and quickly blow air out of their blowhole, making a fountain of watery mist, called a "blow." 6. There are two different kinds of whales, the baleen and the toothed whale. Baleen whales are also called "toothless" whales. Instead of teeth, they have plates made of baleen in their jaws. Baleen is a very hard and strong substance and can be compared with the same substance that makes up the horns on some animals. Baleen is also called "whalebone." Sea water passes through the baleen and krill (a kind of plankton) gets caught. Whales can eat as much as two tons of krill a day. The fin, gray, humpback, blue, bowhead, Bryde's, right, minke, blue, and sei are baleen whales. Humpback whales are baleen whales. Baleen whales have two nostrils, or blowholes. Killer whales and dolphins are both members of the toothed whale group . Toothed whales have teeth instead of baleen. These whales include the beluga or white, bottlenose, narwhal, pilot and sperm whales. Toothed whales eat fish and plants. They have one nostril, or blowhole. 7. Scientists have determined that killer whales can live a maximum of 35 years. They can tell the age of a whale by looking at a cross-section of a killer whale's tooth. Killer whales, like other marine mammals, produce a periodic growth layer on the teeth. By counting these layers, scientists can estimate the animal's age. 8. You can tell an adult male from an adult female by the shape of their dorsal fin. A male's fin is very tall (up to 6 feet tall) and triangular shaped. A female is shorter (3 feet) and curves back toward the dorsal fin. 9. Whales swim by moving their tails up and down and using their flippers, which also help them to turn. Some whales, such as the sei, can swim more than 30 miles per hour. 10. All whales are very noisy. They squeak, moan, groan, and sigh to talk to each other. These underwater sounds can travel great distances. The sounds they make are called "Whale Song." Whale are the loudest animals in the world. [The bubble+bath is contained in a plastic spaceship with a screw-on cap for a nose cone] Charging your Sphero is a simple process that only takes a few minutes. By following these steps carefully, you can ensure that your Sphero is charged properly and ready for use. **Things You'll Need:** Sphero charging dock or USB cable Computer with available USB port (if using USB cable) Power source (if using USB cable) **Step 1: Locate Your Sphero's Charging Port** First, locate your Sphero's micro-USB charging port. This port is located at the bottom of the device and is covered by a rubber flap. Carefully lift up this flap to reveal the charging port. *Key Tip:* Make sure to close the rubber flap after charging to keep dust and debris out of the charging port. **Step 2: Connect the Charging Dock or USB Cable** Next, connect the small end of the included micro-USB cable to the charging port on your Sphero. If you are using the charging dock, simply place your Sphero onto the dock so that the metal contacts align with those on the bottom of the device. The LED light on your Sphero should turn on when it is connected to power. *Key Guideline:* Always make sure that the metal contacts on the bottom of your Sphero are clean and dry before attempting to charge. Any dirt or moisture on these contacts could prevent proper charging. **Step 3: Plug in the Charging Dock or USB Cable** Plug the other end of the micro-USB cable into either the charging dock or an available USB port on your computer. If you do not have access to a USB port, you may also plug the cable into a wall adapter or other power source. Once the connection is made, the LED light on your Sphero will begin blinking slowly to indicate that it is charging. *Key Tip:* When charging via USB, make sure that your computer or power source is turned on. Some computers or ports may be unable to supply enough power to charge your Sphero if they are powered off. **Step 4: Monitor Charging Progress** The LED light on your Sphero will change color as it charges, indicating its current battery level. A solid red light indicates low battery, while a solid green light means that your Sphero is fully charged. While charging, the LED light will blink slowly, changing from red to orange to yellow to green as the battery percentage increases. *Key Guideline:* It is important to monitor your Sphero's charging progress to avoid overcharging. Overcharging can damage the battery and reduce its overall lifespan. Generally, it is recommended to unplug your Sphero once it reaches a full charge (indicated by a solid green LED). However, some newer models of Sphero feature built-in safeguards against overcharging, allowing them to remain connected without risk of damage. **Step 5: Disconnect Your Sphero** Once your Sphero has reached a full charge, disconnect it from the charger by lifting it gently off the charging dock or unplugging the USB cable. Be careful not to pull too hard on the cable, as excessive force could cause damage to the connector or your Sphero itself. After being disconnected, your Sphero will automatically enter sleep mode to conserve energy until it is next used. *Key Tip:* To prolong the life of your Sphero's battery, try to charge it completely before storing it away for extended periods. Storing your Sphero with a partially drained battery can lead to reduced performance and longevity over time. The Islamic World consists of all people who are in Islam.
