Title: How to Perform a Cool Manual on a Skateboard A manual is an essential skateboarding trick where you balance on only two wheels while rolling. It adds flair to your tricks or serves as a transition between other moves. This tutorial will guide you through performing a cool manual on a skateboard with clear instructions and helpful tips. ### Things You'll Need: * A skateboard in good condition * Smooth, flat ground * Protective gear (optional but recommended) ### Step 1: Choose Your Setup Before attempting a manual, ensure that your skateboard setup suits this trick. While manuals can be done on any skateboard, having a lighter complete board or separate components could make learning easier. ### Step 2: Familiarize Yourself With the Stance Stand on your skateboard as if you are about to push off. Shift your weight onto your back foot so that the tail of the skateboard lifts slightly. Keep both feet close to the edges of the deck for better control during the manual. Key Tip: Bend your knees and keep your center of gravity low throughout the entire process. ### Step 3: Practice Balancing on Two Wheels Lift the nose of the skateboard by applying pressure with your front foot while keeping your back foot stationary. The goal here is to find equilibrium and roll smoothly on just the two rear wheels without tipping forward or backward. Guideline: Start practicing at slow speeds until you get comfortable balancing on two wheels. Gradually increase speed once you feel confident. ### Step 4: Perfect Timing Timing is crucial when initiating and ending a manual. To start a manual, compress the springs of the shock absorbers (if present) by bending your legs further right before lifting the nose. Release these stored energies to maintain balance during the manual. Key Tip: Try counting "one-thousand-one" after starting the manual; this mental cue might help maintain stability. ### Step 5: Master Different Distances Once you have mastered short manuals, challenge yourself by covering greater distances. Remember to look ahead rather than down at your board. Maintain steady speed and focus on maintaining balance over longer periods. Guideline: Regularly changing the spot where you begin and end your manuals can improve spatial awareness and overall skill level. ### Step 6: Transition Into Other Tricks After gaining proficiency in doing manuals, incorporate them into more complex routines like noseslides, tailslides, pop shove-its, ollies, etc. Combining different tricks enhances your street credibility among fellow skaters! Remember, practice makes perfect - don't get discouraged if progress seems slow initially. Patience and persistence pay off when learning new skills like manuals. Stay safe, enjoy the ride, and remember to always wear protective gear when necessary. Happy skating! Title: How to Get Over Being Dumped on Facebook Introduction: Breakups are never easy, but when they happen over social media platforms like Facebook, the pain can be amplified due to the public nature of the event. However, with time, self-care, and some strategic steps, you can move past this experience and regain your confidence. This guide will provide you with a comprehensive plan to help you get over being dumped on Facebook. Step 1: Allow Yourself Time to Grieve Before diving into any coping strategies, give yourself permission to grieve. It's okay to feel sad, angry, or confused after a breakup. These emotions are normal parts of the healing process. By acknowledging them, you take the first step towards recovery. Key Tip: Don't rush through this stage; allow yourself at least a few days to process what has happened. Step 2: Limit Social Media Use Avoid logging onto Facebook immediately after the breakup. Seeing pictures or posts about your ex may prolong your emotional distress. Instead, consider taking a short break from all social media until you feel stronger emotionally. If quitting cold turkey seems daunting, try setting specific times during the day for checking Facebook rather than constantly refreshing your feed. Guideline: Set aside 30 minutes in the morning and evening for Facebook usage only. Stick to these designated times strictly. Step 3: Remove Reminders Remove any reminders of your ex from your Facebook profile. Unfollow or unfriend them if necessary. Remember that this action does not mean you have to erase them completely from your life - just that you need space right now to heal. Key Tip: You might want to mute their activity temporarily so you don't see updates about them in your newsfeed. Step 4: Reach Out to Friends Lean on your support network during this challenging time. Reach out to friends who uplift you and make you feel good about yourself. Spend quality time together doing activities you enjoy. Their companionship will aid in distracting you from dwelling on negative thoughts related to the breakup. Step 5: Practice Self-Care Take care of your physical health by eating nutritious meals, exercising regularly, getting enough sleep, and engaging in stress-relieving activities such as yoga or meditation. When we prioritize our wellbeing, we boost our mood and energy levels, which in turn speeds up the healing process. Step 6: Reflect on Lessons Learned Once you've had some distance from the situation, reflect on what led to the relationship's end. Identifying patterns or behaviors that contributed to the breakdown can help prevent repeating similar mistakes in future relationships. Key Tip: Focus on personal growth instead of placing blame. Recognize areas where you could improve without judging or criticizing yourself harshly. Step 7: Seek Professional Help (Optional) If feelings of depression persist despite efforts to cope, consider seeking professional help. Therapists and counselors are trained to assist individuals dealing with difficult transitions, including breakups. They offer objective guidance and tools to manage emotions effectively. Conclusion: Remember, recovering from a breakup takes time. Be patient with yourself throughout this process. While getting over being dumped on Facebook presents unique challenges, implementing these steps can facilitate healing and pave the way for new beginnings. Some trout, though, become very large migratory fish and travel more in their seven years than some people do in a lifetime. In this way, they are similar to their salmon relatives. In fact, they may migrate from their lake or stream to the ocean and back three or four times. spawn through hatch At two years old, trout are ready to reproduce. Their colors change by getting brighter, and they find mates. Some species mate in the fall, and other species mate in the spring. A female trout lays her eggs which are fertilized by a male in a redd, or nest, built in the gravels of a freshwater lake or stream. Baby trout hatch before they're ready to swim. These tiny trout, called alevins, live on the yolk from their egg sacs. hatch through adult Young trout, called fry, use up the food in their egg sacs and swim around in the lake or stream where they were born. They now have to find their own food - mostly tiny organisms called zooplankton. Over the next few years, fry grow up, eating mostly insects and worms. They get the spots, stripes and brilliant colors of an adult trout. "Don't regard this as a woman's caprice," she said. "Even if you hadn't passed this way, I would have heard that music soon. Title: Do 10 Easy Hairstyles for School Introduction: These hairstyles are simple, quick, and perfect for school days. They require minimal supplies and can be done with little to no experience. With these easy-to-follow steps, you'll have a new hairstyle every day of the week! 1. The Classic Ponytail Step 1: Brush your hair thoroughly to remove any tangles or knots. This will make the ponytail look sleeker and more polished. Step 2: Gather all your hair and pull it back towards the crown of your head. Make sure to smooth out any bumps as you go. Step 3: Secure the ponytail with an elastic band. Try to choose one that matches your hair color for a less noticeable finish. Step 4 (optional): To add some volume, gently tug on small sections of your hair near the elastic band to loosen it slightly. Be careful not to pull too hard, as this could cause damage. Key Tips: Keep the ponytail at mid-height; neither too high nor too low. Also, ensure the elastic is tight enough so the ponytail doesn't sag throughout the day. 2. The Braided Headband Step 1: Begin by brushing your hair to remove any tangles. Step 2: Take a section of hair from just above your left ear and start a French braid. Continue braiding until you reach the nape of your neck. Step 3: Once you reach the end of your hair, secure the braid with a clear elastic band. Step 4: Repeat steps 2 and 3 on the right side. Step 5: Cross the two braids over the top of your head like a headband and pin them down using bobby pins. Guidelines: Start the braid close to the scalp for better hold and invisibility. Use hairspray if needed to keep flyaways under control. 3. The Half Up Twist Step 1: After detangling your hair, take equal parts from both sides of your head, about two inches wide, near the temples. Step 2: Twist each section towards the back of your head. Step 3: Combine the twisted sections together and secure them with bobby pins where they cross paths. You can also use a clip or barrette here. Step 4: Hide the ends by tucking them under the secured twists. Tips: If your hair is thin, tease the crown area first to create fullness before starting the twist. 4. The Low Bun You have a shadow bigger than you? Can you stand him up with yours? there's something in there for juice. On Monday, he ate through one apple, but he was still hungry. We call that layer the "O" horizon or sometimes "topsoil". The next layer (horizon) is the "B" horizon. The next lower layer of soil is the "C" horizon. The longest day of the year comes at the summer solstice, but the hottest weather always follows a month or two later. Why? The phenomenon of the hot weather following the summer solstice by a month or two is called the lag of the seasons. You can understand it if you've ever visited a beach in June. On northern hemisphere beaches in June, you'll notice how cold the ocean feels. Or think about mountaintops in June. Ice and snow still blanket the ground on some high mountains. The sun has to melt the ice - and warm the oceans - before we feel the most sweltering summer heat. That's why the hot weather lags behind the year's longest day and highest sun. By August, ocean water on that same beach will be much warmer. And the snow line will have crept up the mountaintops. That's why the hottest weather comes some months after the year's longest day. The land and oceans simply need those extra months to warm up after the cold of winter. Mmm. Beach Bottom line: The solstice marks the height of the sun, but the hottest weather comes a month or two later. That's because the land and oceans have to warm up, too, before the truly hot summer heat can begin. This phenomenon is called the lag of the seasons. Like I said the vehicles anything that moves. Title: How to Avoid Abandonment of a Trademark: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: A trademark is a valuable asset that represents your brand's identity and distinguishes it from competitors. However, if you fail to maintain and protect your trademark properly, it may be considered abandoned by the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). This guide will provide an in-depth explanation of how to avoid abandoning your trademark, ensuring its continued protection and validity. Step 1: Understand What Constitutes Trademark Abandonment Trademark abandonment occurs when there has been no intent to resume use of the mark after a continuous period of nonuse exceeding three years. Nonuse means that the trademark owner did not utilize the mark in connection with any goods or services for which it was registered. To prevent this, ensure consistent usage of your trademark in commerce. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Regularly monitor and enforce your trademark rights against potential infringers. * Keep accurate records of your trademark usage and sales activities. Step 2: File Required Documentation With USPTO To maintain your trademark registration, file all necessary documentation within specified timeframes. These documents include Declaration of Use under Section 8 and Combined Declaration of Use and Application for Renewal under Sections 8 and 9. The former verifies ongoing use of the trademark while the latter extends the life of the registration. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Set reminders for important deadlines related to these filings. * Consider engaging a trademark attorney to assist with filing requirements. Step 3: Maintain Continuous Use Of Your Trademark Continuously use your trademark in commerce as registered. Intermittent or sporadic use can weaken your claim to exclusivity and potentially lead to abandonment. If you plan to discontinue using your trademark temporarily, consider submitting a Statement of Inability to Use before the three-year deadline. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Implement quality control measures to ensure proper usage of your trademark across various channels. * Monitor market trends and adapt your marketing strategies accordingly to maintain visibility. Step 4: Police Infringement And Counterfeiting Activities Vigilantly police instances of trademark infringement or counterfeiting. Failure to do so might indicate that you have abandoned your trademark due to lack of interest in protecting it. Prompt legal action against violators reinforces your commitment to safeguarding your intellectual property rights. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Develop a comprehensive enforcement strategy involving both online and offline monitoring tools. * Consult with a trademark lawyer regarding appropriate responses to suspected infringements. Step 5: Expand Or Adapt Usage As Needed Expand or modify your trademark's usage as needed to keep up with changing business conditions or product offerings. However, always seek professional advice before making significant changes to ensure they don't compromise your trademark's distinctiveness or recognition. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Periodically review your portfolio to identify opportunities for growth or diversification. * Preserve essential elements of your trademark to maintain consumer association and goodwill. Conclusion: By following these steps and implementing best practices, you can effectively avoid abandonment of your trademark and preserve its value for years to come. Remember, proactive management and vigilant protection are crucial components of successful trademark stewardship. I had I have the same thing as Daddy as you as Daddy had. A good way to help other people think about a subject is to ask them a question about it. Being asked a good question can really help us put information together, evaluate our existing ideas and create new ideas. Asking questions that are specifically intended to help others learn is known as Socratic questioning, named after Socrates in Ancient Greece. Socratic questions require you to listen very carefully to the other person to help you judge and phrase your question in a helpful, constructive, and hopefully non-confrontational way. Here are some examples of such questions: Questions of clarification - What do you mean when you say ______? - What is your main point? - How does _____ relate to _____? - Could you put that another way? - Let me see if I understand you; do you mean _____ or _____? - How does this relate to our problem/discussion/issue? - Jane, can you summarize in your own words what Richard said? ... Richard, is this what you meant? - Could you give me an example? - Would _____ be a good example of that? Questions that probe assumptions - What are you assuming here? - What is Jenny assuming? - What could we assume instead? - You seem to be assuming _____. Do I understand you correctly? - All of your reasoning depends on the idea that _____. Why have you based your reasoning on _____ instead of _____? - You seem to be assuming _____. How do you justify taking that for granted? - Is that always the case? Why do you think the assumption holds here? - Why would someone make that assumption? Questions that probe reasons and evidence - Could you explain your reasons to us? - How does that apply to this case? - Is there a reason to doubt that evidence? - Who is in a position to know that is true? - What would you say to someone who said that ____? - Can someone else give evidence to support that view? - By what reasoning did you come to that conclusion? - How could we find out if that is true? Questions about viewpoints or perspectives - What are you implying by that? - When you say _____, are you implying _____? - But, if that happened, what else would happen as a result? Why? - What effect would that have? - Would that necessarily happen or only possibly/probably happen? - What is an alternative? - If _____ and _____ are the case, then what might also be true? - If we say that ____ is ethical, how about _____? Questions that probe implications and consequences - How can we find out? - What does this question assume? - Would _____ ask this question differently? - How could someone settle this question? - Can we break this question down at all? - Is this question clear? Do we understand it? - Is this question easy or hard to answer? Why? - Do we all agree that this is the question? - To answer this question, what other questions must we answer first? - How would _____ state the issue? - Why is this issue important? - Is this the most important question, or is there an underlying question? - Can you see how this might relate to ________? Questions adapted from Paul, R. (1993). Critical Thinking: How To Prepare Students for a Rapidly Changing World: Foundation for Critical Thinking, Santa Rosa, CA. See this like a baking pan so we can pretend that we're making cupcakes. Title: How to Ask a Boy Out on a Date Introduction: Asking a boy out on a date can be both exciting and nerve-wracking. It's important to approach this situation with confidence and respect while considering his feelings and interests. This guide will walk you through the process step by step, ensuring that you feel prepared and self-assured when asking him out. Remember, rejection is a possibility; however, handling it gracefully showcases your maturity and strength of character. Step 1: Identify Your Interest Before making any moves, ensure that you have genuine interest in getting to know him better. Consider if he shares similar values, hobbies or goals as you do. Having common ground increases the likelihood of building a meaningful connection during the date. Step 2: Get To Know Him Spend some time interacting with him casually before asking him out. Engage in conversations about topics of mutual interest, observe his behavior, body language, and responses towards you. Building rapport establishes trust and paves the way for comfortable interactions in the future. Key Tips: Show genuine curiosity about who he is as a person. Listen attentively when he speaks. Share aspects of yourself naturally without overwhelming him. Step 3: Choose an Appropriate Venue Plan the details of the date thoughtfully. Select a venue where both of you would enjoy spending time together based on what you've learned from previous conversations. A casual setting like coffee shop or movie theater could help ease initial tension, but don't shy away from suggesting something unique such as mini golf or cooking class if those activities align more closely with his preferences. Step 4: Formulate Your Question Prepare a clear, concise question that expresses your intentions clearly yet respectfully. Avoid using ambiguous phrases - instead, opt for directness without coming off too strong. Here are two examples: Example 1 (Casual): "Hey, I was thinking of checking out this new caf downtown over the weekend. Would you maybe want to join me?" Example 2 (Activity Based): "I've always wanted to try indoor rock climbing. They offer beginner lessons at XYZ gym. Fancy giving it a shot together one day after school next week?" Step 5: Timing Is Everything Choose the right moment to ask him out. Make sure there aren't distractions around so he can focus solely on your invitation. Also consider his schedule and commitments; proposing a date far in advance shows consideration and allows flexibility for rescheduling should conflicts arise. Step 6: Handle Rejection Gracefully Despite careful planning and preparation, rejection may still occur. If he declines, respond calmly and maintain composure. Thank him for his honesty, wish him well, and move forward positively. Remember, experiencing romantic disappointment doesn't diminish your worth nor does it preclude potential connections with others in the future. Guidelines: Respect his decision regardless of whether it meets your expectations. Refrain from arguing or persuading him further once he has declined. Keep communication open for possible friendship afterwards. Conclusion: Asking a boy out on a date requires courage, emotional intelligence, and foresight. By following these steps and keeping key tips in mind, you increase chances of creating memorable experiences and fostering deeper connections. Regardless of outcomes, remember that every interaction serves as valuable practice for navigating social situations confidently and compassionately. [mother goes over and helps Nina open the door] In his second term in 1986, Reagan nominated William Rehnquist to replace Warren E. Burger as Chief Justice. He named Antonin Scalia to fill the empty seat left by Rehnquist. Hello young learners! Today we are going to talk about something called "dual norm," which is a concept used in mathematics. Don't worry, it's not as scary as it sounds! I promise to make it fun and easy to understand using things you see around you every day. Imagine you have a basket full of apples and oranges. Each fruit has its own size - some apples are bigger than others, and so are the oranges. To keep track of all those sizes, we need a way to measure them. In math, this measurement is called a "norm". It tells us how big or small each fruit is. Now, let's think about scales. When you weigh yourself on a scale, it shows your weight. But did you know there are special types of scales that tell you not just your weight but also other interesting information? These scales are like our "dual norm". They take two pieces of information - the weight of the fruit and its type (apple or orange) - and give us a new number based on both of these values. This new number helps us better understand the relationship between different fruits in the basket. So, when mathematicians work with vectors (which are just fancy lists of numbers), they often want to find relationships between various vectors. That's where the idea of a "dual norm" comes in handy. It combines information from two vectors into one single value. How cool is that?! Here are some key points to remember about dual norms: 1. A dual norm relates to another norm called the "primal norm", similar to how the scale relates to the actual weights of the fruits. 2. The dual norm measures the strength of interaction between a vector and a functional (something that operates on vectors). Think of it like counting calories: instead of looking at individual foods, we consider how many total calories come from eating several food items together. 3. Just like how there are many kinds of fruits and vegetables, there are many types of dual norms depending on the original primal norm. Some common ones include the Euclidean norm and p-norms. 4. Lastly, dual norms follow certain rules, just like how addition follows specific order (you don't add numbers backwards!) These properties ensure consistency within mathematical theories involving dual norms. And that's a basic overview of dual norms! Remember, even though we didn't dive deep into equations or complex terminology, understanding fundamental ideas behind concepts like dual norms will serve as a strong foundation as you continue learning advanced mathematics. Happy exploring! But how much better, so she reasoned, that the name and might of her house as rulers of Holland should be upheld by a brave and fearless knight. Unlike most mammals, when cats walk, they use a "pacing" gait (walking style); that is, they move the two legs on one side of the body before the legs on the other side. This trait is shared with camels and giraffes. As a walk speeds up into a trot, a cat's gait will change to be a "diagonal" gait, similar to that of most other mammals: the diagonally opposite hind and forelegs will move at the same time. Most cats have five claws on their front paws, and four on their rear paws. On the inside of the front paws there is something which looks like a sixth "finger". This special feature on the inside of the wrists is the carpal pad. The carpal pad is also found on other cats and on dogs. Title: How to Change Your Hourly Rate on Upwork (Formerly Elance) Upwork is an online platform that connects businesses with freelancers from various fields such as programming, design, writing, sales, and customer service. As a freelancer, you can create your profile, showcase your portfolio, and set your own hourly rate or fixed-price projects based on your skills and expertise. If you wish to change your existing hourly rate on Upwork (formerly known as Elance), this comprehensive guide will walk you through the process while providing helpful tips along the way. ### Step 1: Log into your Upwork account Navigate to <https://www.upwork.com/> and click 'Sign in' at the top right corner. Enter your email address and password associated with your Upwork account to log in. This initial step grants access to your dashboard and allows you to modify any personal settings, including your hourly rate. ### Step 2: Access your Profile Settings Once logged in, hover over your name displayed at the top right corner and select 'My Account Settings.' From there, choose 'Profile' located in the left sidebar menu under the Personal Info tab. Clicking on 'Profile' takes you to your public-facing page where potential clients view your information and services offered. ### Step 3: Edit your Freelancer Profile Scroll down until you locate the section labeled 'Hourly Rate,' which should be just below the Overview heading. Here, you are presented with two options - either display a range or keep it hidden. To make changes to your hourly rate, click on the pencil icon next to 'Displayed hourly rate' to edit. A new window will appear prompting you to enter your preferred hourly rate. Remember that setting realistic yet competitive rates enhances your chances of attracting quality clients. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Research competitors within your niche to establish appropriate pricing. * Consider factors like location, experience level, demand, and specialization when adjusting your rates. * Offering lower rates initially may help secure more job opportunities; however, ensure they reflect your true value and skillset. ### Step 4: Save Changes After entering your updated hourly rate, review it carefully one last time before saving. Keep in mind that changing your rate does not affect ongoing contracts but only applies to future proposals and invitations. Once satisfied, hit the blue 'Save' button found at the bottom right corner of the pop-up window. The system then confirms the successful modification via a green notification banner across the screen. Additional Tip: Periodically reassess your hourly rate based on market trends, inflation, and increased proficiency levels. Adjusting your rate accordingly ensures fair compensation for your work and maintains a strong reputation among peers and prospective clients alike. Congratulations! You have now successfully changed your hourly rate on Upwork. By following these steps and implementing our suggested best practices, you position yourself competitively within the freelancing community while receiving fair remuneration for your talents and efforts. Happy freelancing! Most Hindus who do eat meat do not eat beef. Some do not even use leather products. This is most likely because many Hindus have relied so heavily on the cow for all sorts of dairy products, tilling of fields and fuel for fertiliser that its status as a willing 'caretaker' of humanity grew to identifying it as an almost motherly figure. Thus, while most Hindus do not worship the cow, and rules against eating beef arose long after the Vedas had been written, it still has an honored place in Hindu society. It is said that Krishna is both Govinda (herder of cows) and Gopala (protector of cows), and Shiva's attendant is Nandi, the bull. With the stress on vegetarianism (which is usually followed even by meat-eating Hindus on religious days or special occasions) and the sacred nature of the cow, it is no wonder that most holy cities and areas in India have a ban on selling meat-products and there is a movement among Hindus to ban cow-slaughter not only in specific regions, but in all of India. With this kind, an opening is made that is called adit. Tunnels into the ground are dug, blasted with dynamite, or drilled out. These tunnels are called Shafts are dug straight down [vertically] into the ground. Each shaft has a purpose. One shaft might be for miners to use to go in and out of the mine. One shaft might be just for mine machinery. Another shaft is used for air or When miners get to the bottom of the shafts, there is another tunnel that leads to the rocks or minerals. In pictures, this tunnel always looks horizontal but it doesn't have to be. Tunnels can be slanted or sloped. When we thought of the inside of a mine, we thought about all of the diagrams that we had seen. They make it look like a few shafts and a horizontal tunnel at the bottom. That isn't really the whole picture. You might say that mines are like an underground building. You go down in the shaft, and reach floor number one. There can be other floors under floor one. For example, we went to a zinc mine. There was something called a 'man cage' that was a seat on an inclined machine. Click on the picture on the right to see a larger one. The miner would sit on this and travel between 'floors' of the mine. The man cage travels 800 feet per minute. There were lots of floors under the floor we were on. They weren't open anymore and were filled with water. We were told that there is so much water down there that if there was a drought, the town wouldn't have to worry about water at all. The tunnels in a hard rock mine are divided into rooms with rock pillars. In the picture on the left, the pillars are colored black. When we visited the coal mine, these pillars were made of coal. With the room and pillar way of mining, the miner has more than one way to escape if he has to. When the mine owners and the engineers decide that they will stop taking coal from the mine, they do something called "robbing the pillars." This means that as they back out of the tunnels, they take the coal out of the pillars that hold up the roof. This means that the roof doesn't have support anymore so it caves in. This is done very carefully and they get lots of coal out of the pillars before they leave. Hard rock mining is one of the most dangerous kinds of mining. In the first place, it is underground. It can be very deep underground. Some mines [like coal ones] have deadly gases in them. Miners have to always be ready for cave-ins, explosions, and gases. This is a very hard and dangerous job. Because there was no clear heir to the throne anymore, the Scottish people decided to ask Edward I of England to choose their king. The strongest candidate was called Robert Bruce. Robert Bruce had castles all around the country, and had a private army. But Edward wanted to invade Scotland, so he chose the weaker candidate, who was John Balliol. And he's going to put in a pillar even though the pillar might block the view. When you cuddle with your child while reading a story, your child begins to associate reading with a sense of security. Children learn better when they feel safe. Provide Sound Effects Use silly voices and sounds to keep your child interested in the story. Hearing different sounds in language also helps your child develop critical listening skills. Try singing, too! Make Connections (1) Help your child connect the words you are reading and the words she is hearing. Follow along with your finger as you read to show how print moves from left to right. Point out the pictures in the book and ask your child what they see. Ask him open-ended questions, like "What do you think will happen next?" or "What would you do?" Make Connections (2) Letters and words surround us. Children need experience with all kinds of print - from shopping lists to the Internet to street signs. Point out letters and words around you. Connect the letter symbol to the name of the letter. Read It Again Children need to hear favorite stories over and over. This helps them recognize and remember words. It also helps them learn how to predict what's coming next. Most importantly, as kids become familiar with a story, it gives them confidence about reading and improves their comprehension and background knowledge. Keep It Active Let your child touch and hold the book. Ask her to help you turn the pages. And you don't always have to sit when you read or listen to a story. Try clapping out a fun rhyme or dancing to a silly poem. Too tired to hold a book? Tell a story that you know, or make one up together. Making up a story with your child stimulates creativity. It's also a nice change. Read with Expression Provide sound effects and show enthusiasm. Encourage your toddler to point out objects, repeat words, and talk about the story. Dialogic (shared) Reading Use the technique of dialogic (shared) reading to help a child stay actively involved with a story and develop reading comprehension. Instead of reading the story straight through, ask the child open-ended questions about the story: "Why do you think Goldilocks ate Baby Bear's porridge?" "What do you think will happen next?" Encourage 'picture walking'; allow your child to retell the story using the illustrations as guides. Read or Tell... stories in the language you are most comfortable with. It doesn't have to be English! Help Your Child Develop Phonological Awareness The understanding that words are made up of smaller sounds--by playing games with the sounds of words and repeating rhymes. Many people speak Chinese - Mandarin and other Chinese dialects - in Singapore. Just over 50% of Singaporeans speak it at home, so it is the most common language in homes. Singaporean Mandarin is based on simplified Chinese and it is similar to the system used in mainland China. The forms of Mandarin spoken in Singapore range from Standard Mandarin to a pidgin known as Singdarin. Besides Mandarin, many southern Chinese dialects are also spoken in Singapore. Title: Understanding Mean and Standard Deviation through Moped Speeds Imagine you and your friends have a moped racing game. You all want to know how fast each moped can go and if some mopeds are faster than others by a lot or just a little bit. To do this, we can learn two important concepts in statistics: mean and standard deviation! Mean (or average): Let's say you raced 5 mopeds and got these top speeds: 45, 47, 46, 49, and 44 kilometers per hour (km/h). To find the mean speed, simply add up all those numbers and divide by how many there are: (45 + 47 + 46 + 49 + 44) / 5 = 46.2 km/h So, the mean speed of your mopeds is 46.2 km/h. Standard Deviation: Now, let's see how spread out the speeds are around our mean speed. Calculate the difference between each moped's speed and the mean speed, square them, then take the average of those squared differences. Finally, take the square root of that number. In math, it looks like this: [( 1 + 2 + ... + n )/n] Here, represents the difference between each data point and the mean; n is the total count of mopeds. This formula tells us how far, on average, the moped speeds differ from the mean speed. In our example case, the standard deviation turns out to be approximately 1.75 km/h. Now, imagine a new rule for your moped race - any moped going more than 1.5 standard deviations (about 2.6 km/h) faster OR slower than the mean should get special recognition! Using our calculations, we can now predict which mopeds will stand out in the crowd: Mean 1.5 Standard Deviation: 46.2 (1.5 1.75) = (42.9, 49.5) km/h This means mopeds with speeds near the ends of the range (42.9 to 49.5 km/h) show greater variation compared to other mopeds close to the mean speed. By understanding mean and standard deviation, you not only know who'll likely receive special recognition but also grasp key statistical ideas behind measuring variability and central tendency! The Reverend John Lingon became very talkative over his second bottle of port, which was opened on his nephew's arrival. He was not curious about the manners of Smyrna, or about Harold's experience, but he unbosomed himself very freely as to what he himself liked and disliked, which of the farmers he suspected of killing the foxes, what game he had bagged that very morning, what spot he would recommend as a new cover, and the comparative flatness of all existing sport compared with cock-fighting, under which Old England had been prosperous and glorious, while, so far as he could see, it had gained little by the abolition of a practice which sharpened the faculties of men, gratified the instincts of the fowl, and carried out the designs of heaven in its admirable device of spurs. From these main topics, which made his points of departure and return, he rambled easily enough at any new suggestion or query; so that when Harold got home at a late hour, he was conscious of having gathered from amidst the pompous full-toned triviality of his uncle's chat some impressions, which were of practical importance. Once upon a time there was a very wealthy family who owned many things, but the family's most cherished possession was the puppy that the father had bought for the children that same day. The puppy was small and fluffy with big, black eyes. The family went outside to play with the puppy, and they laughed with joy as they watched the puppy chase around butterflies. Suddenly, the puppy started to shiver with cold and the family looked up to see storm clouds gathering in the sky. The mother said to the father, "Let's go inside quick and get the pup warm." But the father said, "It's ok, the pup will be fine in this weather," and he began to walk away. The children were about to shiver too, but the mother quickly picked up the puppy and rushed everyone inside to safety. The family was so thankful that the mother had acted quickly and that the puppy was safe. The mom said to the dad, "See, that's why I always listen to my instincts." The family smiled and hugged their beloved puppy tight, thanking the mom for her wisdom. I'll fix your clothesline while you're gone. Let's play with them on the table. This scepticism made the minister uneasy: he had great belief in the old political watchwords, had preached that universal suffrage and no ballot were agreeable to the will of God, and liked to believe that a visible "instrument" was forthcoming in the Radical candidate who had pronounced emphatically against Whig finality. In communications, such as radio and television, broadcasting means sending information such as television shows or music electronically to a large audience. The information is sent through the air in radio waves, through a wire, or by a communications satellite, and then the television viewers or radio listeners pick up the signal using their television sets and radio receivers. Gugliemo Marconi invented wireless telegraphy, in December 1901 he transmitted first radio signals across the Atlantic Ocean. This was point to point. Experiments with voice broadcasting began a few years later, and it grew rapidly in the 1920s. Abraham Lincoln was born on February 12, 1809, in Hodgenville, Kentucky, United States. His parents were Thomas Lincoln and Nancy Hanks. His family was very poor. Abraham had one brother and one sister. His brother died in childhood. They grew up in a small log cabin, with just one room inside. Although slavery was legal in Kentucky at that time, Lincoln's father, who was a religious Baptist, refused to own any slaves. " Well how would you like to use your money to buy some G I Joe pants? No accurate portraits of Genghis Khan exist, and any surviving depictions of him are considered to be artistic interpretations. Persian historian Rashid-al-Din recorded in his ""Chronicles"" that the legendary "glittering" ancestor of Genghis was tall, long-bearded, red-haired, and green-eyed. Rashid al-Din also described the first meeting of Genghis and Kublai Khan, when Genghis was shocked to find that Kublai had not inherited his red hair. Also according to al-Din, Genghis's Borjigid clan had a legend involving their origins: it began as the result of an affair between Alan-ko and a stranger to her land, a glittering man who happened to have red hair and bluish-green eyes. Modern historian Paul Ratchnevsky has suggested in his Genghis biography that the "glittering man" may have been from the Kyrgyz people, who historically displayed these same characteristics. Controversies aside, the closest depiction generally accepted by most historians is the portrait currently in the National Palace Museum in Taipei, Taiwan (see picture above). Creating a Gacha Life meme video can be a fun and creative way to express yourself online. This tutorial will guide you through the process, from start to finish, using the Gacha Life app. Step 1: Install Gacha Life Download and install the Gacha Life app on your mobile device or computer. The app is free and available on both iOS and Android platforms. Step 2: Familiarize Yourself with the Interface Once installed, open the Gacha Life app and familiarize yourself with its interface. You'll find several options such as Studio Mode, Character Mode, Scene Mode, etc. For creating meme videos, we'll primarily focus on Studio Mode. Step 3: Create Characters for Your Meme Video Go to Character Mode to design characters that fit the theme of your meme video. Make sure they are distinct so viewers can tell them apart easily. Remember, clear communication is key in memes. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Use bold colors and exaggerated expressions to make your characters stand out. - Keep character designs simple yet recognizable. Avoid overly complex designs that might confuse viewers. - Consider popular culture references when designing your characters. Step 4: Set Up Your Stage Move into Scene Mode to set up the backgrounds and environments for your scenes. Choose settings appropriate for your meme concept. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Select plain backdrops if you want all attention focused on your characters. - If your meme involves movement, select dynamic backgrounds like streets or parks. - Be mindful of lighting; bright scenes usually work best for humor. Step 5: Position Your Characters Now arrange your characters within the scene according to your script. Adjust their size, rotation, and position until everything looks right. Step 6: Add Dialogue Bubbles or Text Plates Add dialogue bubbles or text plates to convey what your characters are saying or thinking. Keep text short and funny for maximum impact. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Limit text length to two lines per bubble/plate. - Use font sizes wisely - larger texts draw more attention but smaller ones allow more content. - Align text bubbles correctly depending on who's speaking (left for one character, right for another). Step 7: Animate Your Scenes Switch to Studio Mode to animate your scenes. Here, you can create skits consisting of multiple posed frames. Each frame acts as a 'slide', advancing the storyline bit by bit. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Plan ahead! Storyboard your ideas before animating. - Vary camera angles and positions between frames for visual interest. - Don't rush animation; smooth transitions matter even in humorous contexts. Step 8: Save and Export Your Creation After finishing your masterpiece, save it within the app then export it. Most devices let you share directly to social media sites, messaging apps, or cloud storage services. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Always preview your creation before sharing publicly. - Double check spelling and grammar. - Respect copyright laws and avoid infringing upon others' intellectual property rights. In conclusion, making a Gacha Life meme video requires creativity, patience, and understanding of basic visual storytelling principles. By following these steps meticulously, you're bound to produce something entertaining and memorable. Happy editing! Is it going to go all the way over there? Change the value of a variable. 4. Control your robot's movement or other things in your robot Step 1: A BIT of information. Each bit represents a certain number to add. the first bit (Far right) represents the ones place, similar to the common decimal system. 1 = 1 and 0 = 0. the next bit's value is multiplied by two, so: 10 = 2, 11 = 3, 00 = 0 and 01 = 1. 100 = 4, 1000 = 8, 10000 = 16, 100000 = 32, 1000000 = 64 10000000 = 128. Now these bits can form a value up to 255 (11111111). This value can be turned into ASCII encoded symbols and letters. It began being used in 1582. It replaced the previous Julian calendar because the Julian calendar had an error: it added a leap year (with an extra day every four years) with no exceptions. The length of the Julian year was exactly 365.25 days (365 days and 6 hours), but the actual time it takes for the Earth to go around the Sun once is closer to 365.2425 days (about 365 days, 5 hours and 49 minutes). This difference is about eleven minutes each year. It's the stuff that we had the other night. See that's how the clothes go back on. We can build something here [black base]. The wrist needs to be straight and "hovering" when the kid is writing, but should not rest on the table. Also don't let kids lean down too much. Title: How to Deal with a Teen Who Talks Back Introduction: Teens talking back is a common challenge faced by many parents. It can be frustrating and disrespectful, but understanding the reasons behind this behavior and learning effective strategies to respond can help improve communication and maintain a healthy parent-teen relationship. This tutorial will provide you with in-depth guidance on dealing with a teen who talks back. Step 1: Stay Calm and Composed When your teen talks back, it's essential to remain calm and composed. Responding angrily or aggressively may escalate the situation and damage your relationship. Take deep breaths, count to ten if necessary, and remind yourself that maintaining control of your emotions sets a positive example for your teen. * Key Tip: Modeling self-control demonstrates constructive ways to manage stress and strong emotions. Step 2: Analyze the Situation Consider the context surrounding the incident. Was there a trigger? Were they tired, hungry, or stressed? Understanding the underlying causes can help address the root issue effectively. * Guideline: Avoid jumping to conclusions; gather information before reacting. Step 3: Acknowledge Their Feelings Validate their feelings and perspectives, even if you don't agree with them. Show empathy and let them know you understand why they might feel frustrated or upset. * Key Tip: Acknowledgment fosters open communication and trust between you and your teen. Step 4: Set Clear Boundaries Establish clear rules about respectful communication. Explain what behaviors are acceptable and unacceptable, using specific examples when possible. Be consistent in enforcing these boundaries. * Guideline: Make sure consequences for disrespectful behavior are reasonable, related, and immediate. Step 5: Encourage Healthy Expression Teach your teen appropriate ways to express themselves without being disrespectful. Roleplay different scenarios so they can practice assertiveness while still being polite. * Key Tip: Emphasize the importance of active listening during conversations. Step 6: Choose Your Battles Wisely Determine whether the situation warrants intervention or if it's better to ignore minor instances of backtalk. Focus on teaching respect rather than always needing to win arguments. * Guideline: Prioritize issues based on safety, values, and family harmony. Step 7: Reinforce Positive Behavior Praise your teen when they communicate respectfully. Providing positive reinforcement encourages repetition of desirable actions. * Key Tip: Catch them doing something right and acknowledge it verbally or through other rewards. Step 8: Seek Professional Help If Needed If your teen consistently displays disrespectful behavior despite repeated attempts to address the issue, consider seeking professional assistance from a counselor or therapist specializing in adolescent development and communication. * Guideline: Early intervention can prevent further deterioration of the parent-teen relationship and promote healthier communication patterns. Conclusion: Dealing with a teen who talks back requires patience, consistency, and understanding. By following these steps and remaining committed to improving communication, you can foster a stronger bond with your teenager and teach them valuable life skills. Remember that every child is unique, and finding what works best for both of you may take time and experimentation. Two of these men were James and John. One digraph that will help children develop this ability is the sh=/sh/. Shoe Man By, Steck Vaugn and Kunka, Alice, chart with pictures of /sh/ words and non /sh/ words, and a worksheet 1. Introduce the lesson by asking the children what sound would you make when telling someone to be quiet. After they have told me the sound /sh/ I will then ask then what do you think the two letters are that make that sound. 2. The /sh/ sound is made of two letters the s and the h. Let's practice the /sh/ sound all together. Now I would like you to tell me what words you hear the /sh/ sound in: Fish or Kiss; List or Dish; ect. 3. Now let's try a tongue twister with the sound /sh/:"Shy Shelly shouted loudly shaking Sharon." Now let's all say it together; again. Now let's try stretching out the /sh/ sound at the beginning of each word. "Shshy Shshelly shshouted loudly Shshsaking Shsharon." Great job! 4. Now I want you to look at these pictures on the board and tell me which ones have the sound /sh/ in them? Now I want you to tell me if you hear the /sh/ sound in the front of the word or the end of the word. 5. Now I am going to give you a book that is called Shoe Man and I want you to get wih a friend. You and your friend need to look throught the book to find /sh/ words. Ask the children to then read you some of the words they have found in the text. Then have them read the text with a buddy while you walk around and help them with difficult words. 6. Ask children to stand up behind their desk and tell them that whenever they hear a word with the sound /sh/ to shake their bodies. Have a list of words with the sound /sh/ and other sounds so the children will alternate shaking their bodies. 7. Next give the children a worksheet and have them put the /sh/ in the correct blank so that it will make a word. Kunka, Alice. Shoe Man. Title: How to Create and Maintain Farm Fly Traps in RuneScape Introduction: In RuneScape, farming is one of the most rewarding skills that players can train. It allows them to grow various types of produce which are useful in cooking, herblore, and other skills. However, pests such as flies can damage crops and reduce overall yield. To mitigate this issue, you can create farm fly traps. This guide will provide an in-depth explanation of creating and maintaining farm fly traps in RuneScape. Step 1: Gather Required Materials Before starting, make sure you have all the necessary materials to construct a fly trap: * A knife (can be bought from any general store) * A piece of silk (spun by spinning flax at level 1 Spinning or purchased from another player) * A raw bird meat (caught using Fishing or purchased from another player) Having these items will enable you to craft your own fly trap without needing to travel back and forth between locations. Key Tip: Keep extra pieces of silk and raw bird meats handy; you'll need more than one fly trap if you want to protect multiple farming patches effectively. Step 2: Travel to a Suitable Location Head over to any farming patch where crop rotation occurs - e.g., fruit tree, hops, or allotment patches. These areas are ideal because they frequently experience issues with fly infestation due to their crop variety. Guideline: Creating a fly trap near the affected area saves time when placing and checking on the traps later. Step 3: Prepare the Silk and Bird Meat Use your knife on the raw bird meat to remove its feathers. Then, combine the clean bird meat with the piece of silk in your inventory to form a completed fly trap. You should now see a new item called 'fly trap' in your backpack. Key Tip: Make several fly traps at once since they degrade after catching five flies each. Step 4: Place the Fly Trap Near Infested Crops Locate the most damaged plants within the farming patch, then right-click on the ground nearby and select 'use' followed by 'place fly trap.' The game will automatically place the trap close to the targeted area. Guideline: Placing multiple fly traps around the perimeter of the farming plot ensures maximum coverage against pesky flies. Step 5: Monitor and Empty the Fly Traps Regularly check your fly traps every few hours to ensure they haven't reached their capacity of five dead flies. Once full, collect the trapped flies by right-clicking on the trap and selecting 'pick up.' Dispose of the used fly traps or re-stock them with new bird meat if needed. Key Tip: Setting reminders throughout the day can help manage your fly traps efficiently while minimizing potential crop losses. Step 6: Maintaining Your Farm Fly Traps To maintain optimal performance, replace old and worn-out fly traps with fresh ones regularly. Also, keep an eye out for new infestations and add additional fly traps accordingly. Guideline: Checking your farming plots daily enables early detection of problems like disease or pests, ensuring healthier crops and higher yields. Conclusion: Creating and maintaining farm fly traps in RuneScape requires some effort but significantly reduces crop loss caused by flies. With this comprehensive guide, you're well equipped to handle fly infestations and protect your valuable farming investments. Happy farming! Lisa and Raymond got home from school. Size: 10-12 lbs; 16" body/ 24" tail Mating: 21-30 months Gestation: 134-138 days No. of Young: 1 Lifestyle: Family units Diet: Fruit, flowers. Leaves, bark, sap & herbs Lifespan: Up to 25 years The ring-tailed lemur is the size of a large cat. Its nose looks like a fox's face and its tail is like a raccoon's but it is not related to a cat, a fox, or a raccoon. Instead, lemurs are primates related to monkeys, apes, and humans. Lemurs are "prosimians" or early monkeys. Lemurs smear branches with scent from glands on their body. In this way, they mark their territory. The scent also helps them identify one another. Ring tailed lemurs are social animals. They live in groups of 5-30. Females dominate the group. There are more than 20 species of lemur. The ring-tailed is terrestrial. They spend much of their time on the ground. They live in rocky areas with few trees. The palms and soles of their feet are smooth and leather-like, giving them a good grip on slippery rocks. Early in the morning they like to find a sunny spot. Lemurs sit upright facing the sin, spreading their arms and legs apart and exposing their white belly. Native people thought that lemurs were "sun-worshippers." However, they are only warming themselves. They live only on Madagascar, an island off the east coast of Africa. Lemurs are an endangered species. let's hear you sound like a kitty. Phrasal verbs are mainly used in spoken English and informal texts. (The more formal a conversation or text, the less phrasal verbs are found.) Phrasal verbs consist of a verb and a particle (preposition, adverb). The particle can change the meaning of the verb completely. For example:: - look up - consult a reference book (look a word up in a dictionary) - look for - seek (look for her ring) - look forward - anticipate with pleasure (look forward to meeting someone) There are no rules that might explain the meaning of phrasal verbs. All you can do is look them up in a good dictionary and study their meanings. Title: How to Change an Apple Computer's Background to Black and White **Summary:** This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on changing your Apple computer's background to black and white. It includes detailed steps, explanations, tips, and guidelines to ensure clarity and ease of application. **Step 1: Understanding Your Desktop Background** The desktop background, also known as wallpaper, is the image or color that appears on your screen when no windows are open. Changing this can be done for various reasons such as personal preference, improving visibility, or reducing eye strain. In our case, we want to change it to black and white. **Step 2: Accessing System Preferences** Click on the Apple logo located at the top left corner of your screen. From the drop-down menu, select 'System Preferences.' This action opens up a new window filled with different settings related to your Mac's functionality and appearance. **Step 3: Navigating to Desktop & Screen Saver Settings** In the System Preferences window, look for 'Desktop & Screen Saver'. It should be under the Personal section towards the right side of the window. Click on it to proceed. **Step 4: Choosing Desktop Pictures** Once clicked, you'll see two tabs - Desktop and Screen Saver. Make sure you're on the Desktop tab. Here, there are several folders containing preloaded images; these are what you'd normally choose from if you wanted a picture as your wallpaper. However, since we aim for a monochrome backdrop, ignore these options. **Key Tip**: If you wish to have a specific black-and-white photo as your wallpaper later, convert it into grayscale before setting it as your background. **Step 5: Selecting Solid Colors Option** To get a solid colored background, click on the 'Color' option located below the folder list. A spectrum of colors will appear along with some default patterns like stripes and speckles. Ignore those too because they don't fit our requirement. **Step 6: Picking The Right Shade Of Grey** Now, let's turn this colorful palette into shades of grey. Look closely at the bottom row of the color picker where you find sliders controlling Hue, Saturation, and Brightness. Set the hue anywhere between 0 and 360 (it doesn't matter much here), then drag the saturation all the way down to zero. What remains is pure brightness which now represents varying intensities of grey. **Guideline**: Choose a shade closer to black but not entirely black to avoid extreme contrast while working with text documents or other applications against the backdrop. **Step 7: Applying Changes** After deciding upon the perfect grey tone, simply close the Color Picker by clicking outside its box or pressing Enter/Return. You'll notice your selected shade filling up the preview pane next to the word 'Desktop', giving you a glimpse of how your newly set background would look. **Step 8: Confirmation And Exiting** If everything looks good, just exit out of System Preferences either by clicking the red dot at the upper left corner of the window or using Command + Q shortcut keys. Voila! You now have a sleek, professional-looking black-and-white background. Remember, customizing your workspace enhances productivity and comfort. So feel free to explore more visual tweaks whenever needed! [Nina puts the chimney in place, but it falls off again] What do you want to pretend your green eggs are? How do you fit the Geometry Standard into your Preschool Classroom? 1) Patterning Blocks! Let kids manipulate different shapes. Ask questions about the different shapes? How many sides does the hexagon have? Are they all the same length? 2-4) Art! Let kids explore different shapes - this 'circle art' is great for young learners who are just learning their shapes. 5-6) Building! The kids need to be able to experiment with different 3 dimensional shapes. This is a great math concept because the kids can actually SEE that it takes more blocks to build the longer side of a shape. Page 1 of 520 Examples of Wave Calculations. Q1. A sound wave has a frequency of 3250 and a wavelength of 0 1 m. What is its velocity? A1. Use v = f x l = 3250 x 0 1 = 325 m/s. Q2. A sound wave travels with a velocity of and has a frequency of 500 Hz. What is its wavelength? A2. Use l = v f l = 330 = 0 66 m. Q3. A wave at sea travels with a velocity of 25 m/s. If it has a wavelength of 10 m, what is its frequency? A3. Use f = v l 25 10 = 2 5 Hz. Note - always make sure that you give the units for your and that the units are correct. If the wavelength is given in centimetres, convert it to metres before doing the calculation. Adding "jammers" (items that alter your character's appearance) on Animal Jam is an exciting way to customize your animal and express yourself within the game! This guide will walk you through the process with clear, detailed steps and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Log into Your Account To begin, visit the Animal Jam website and enter your username and password to log into your account. Make sure you remember these details securely as they protect access to your account and its contents. Step 2: Navigate to the Diamond Shop Once logged in, look at the top menu bar and click on "Shop." From there, select "Diamond Shop," which contains all purchasable items using Diamonds - one of the two currencies available in Animal Jam. Key Tip: If you don't have enough Diamonds, consider completing daily quests or trading with other players for more Diamonds before proceeding. Step 3: Choose the Jammers Category Inside the Diamond Shop, locate and click on the category labeled "Jammers" found on the left side of the page under "Clothing & Accessories." Here, you can browse various subcategories such as hats, shirts, pants, etc., making it easier to find what you're looking for. Guideline: Consider matching jammers based on theme or color scheme for optimal outfit coordination. Step 4: Preview Items Click on any item to preview how it would look on your current animal. A pop-up window will appear showing your character wearing the selected jammer. You may rotate your character by clicking and dragging the mouse to view the item from different angles. Step 5: Purchase the Desired Item If you like the appearance of the chosen jammer, click the green button saying "Buy Now" located below the image. The cost of the item will be deducted from your total Diamond count, displayed beneath the shopping cart icon at the upper right corner of the screen. Key Tip: Be mindful of your spending; make sure you are purchasing items that truly reflect your personal style and preferences. Step 6: Equip the New Jammer After successfully buying a new jammer, head back to the main play area called Jamaa Township. To equip the purchased item, open up your backpack by clicking on the blue backpack icon near the bottom left part of the screen. Inside your backpack, navigate to the section where your newly bought jammer resides. Drag and drop the item onto the corresponding body part slot above your inventory. Step 7: Save Changes and Share Your Look Don't forget to save changes after equipping the new jammer. Click on the pink floppy disk icon next to the chat bubble to permanently keep your updated appearance. Feel free to share your fresh look with friends or showcase it during parties and social events inside Animal Jam. By following this comprehensive tutorial, adding jammers to enhance your Animal Jam experience has become simple and enjoyable. Happy jamming! Creating Art with Computer Programs and Climate Data Have you ever thought about combining your love for art and science? In this activity, we'll learn how to use computer programs and climate data to create cool visual art! This is similar to what Mundo Kumo, a band, did when creating their music video using algorithmic art and real climate data. First, let's understand what cellular automata means. Imagine a grid like a checkerboard, where each square or "cell" can have different colors. Now, imagine setting a rule that tells us how each cell should change its color based on the colors of the cells around it. When we apply these rules repeatedly, we get patterns and pictures that emerge from seemingly random starting points - that's cellular automata! Now, let's see how we can connect this to climate data. Climate data includes information about temperature, rainfall, wind speed, etc., collected over time. By representing this data visually through cell colors, we can observe changes in weather patterns, seasons, or even long-term climate trends! Here's a step-by-step guide to make your own artwork using Python, a programming language: 1. **Get Familiar With Python**: You don't need prior knowledge of AI or complex algorithms; just basic familiarity with Python syntax would suffice. There are many online tutorials available to learn Python basics. 2. **Download the Code**: Visit the creator's GitHub page (<https://github.com/JoseHidalgo98/AlgorithmicArt>) and download the necessary files for generating cellular automata images. 3. **Understand the Code Structure**: Look into the provided code and try to grasp its structure. It mainly consists of defining the grid size, assigning initial values, setting up rules for changing cell states, applying those rules iteratively, and saving the final image. 4. **Visualize Climate Data**: Obtain some climate datasets from websites like NOAA or NASA, then convert them into formats compatible with the program. Assign specific ranges within the dataset to distinct colors, allowing variations in climate conditions to translate directly into beautiful gradients across the grid. 5. **Run Your Creation**: Once everything is set up correctly, run the script, and watch your unique artwork come alive! Remember, small modifications in rules or input data may lead to drastically different outcomes, so keep experimenting until you achieve something fascinating. In conclusion, by combining cellular automata concepts with climate data visualization, you too can contribute towards raising awareness about environmental issues creatively while learning valuable programming skills! Happy exploring! What did you do out in the yard today? Two things to hold, may I have it please? Um I bought them some of those at The Stratus Store? Today we're going to put our detective hats on and explore a cool question about guns, angles, and distances. You might have heard people say that "one minute of angle (MOA) equals one inch at 100 yards." But does this old saying hold up when we put on our thinking caps and examine it more closely? Let's dive into some fun math and find out! Imagine holding a quarter and looking at its edge. The distance around the edge of the coin is called its circumference. Now let's pretend this quarter is part of a giant circle with a huge radius - like it was drawn around the Earth! This would make the coin seem really small compared to the whole circle. Even though the coin has a fixed size, the same distance along different circles can represent tiny changes or massive leaps depending on their sizes. Now, imagine shooting an arrow using a bow. When you aim at a target, you need to consider two things: where you want the arrow to go and the actual place it hits after traveling through the air. There could be differences between these two points due to wind, gravity, or even your own skill. These little shifts are sometimes measured in something called minutes of angle (MOA). Think of them like degrees on a protractor but smaller; each degree divides into 60 equal parts called minutes. One MOA is approximately equivalent to 1/60th of a degree. The claim says that, at 100 yards (which is just over 91 meters), moving your gun sights by 1 MOA results in your bullet straying off target by 1 inch (around 2.54 cm). Essentially, it assumes that the bullet travels parallel to the ground without any outside forces acting upon it. To check if this assumption holds water, we'll use a formula connecting arcs, angles, and radii: Arc Length = Angle Radius Let's apply this to our problem. We know the angle (1 MOA) and the distance (100 yards): 1 inch 1 MOA 100 yards If both sides were equally divided by 100 yards, then 1 MOA would be equivalent to 1 inch / 100 yards, which simplifies to roughly 0.025 inches per yard. That means every yard traveled increases the effect of 1 MOA by 0.025 inches - not exactly 1 inch. However, since most bullets don't travel very far before hitting their targets, the difference isn't noticeable within common hunting ranges. Thus, the original statement works well enough for practical purposes, despite being slightly incorrect! So, next time someone tells you that 1 MOA always equals 1 inch at 100 yards, remember your detective skills and share your newfound knowledge. While it may not be perfectly accurate, understanding why makes us appreciate the simplicity behind the idea and enjoy exploring connections between real life, geometry, and mathematics! Title: How to Get a Dog to Sleep Introduction: Dogs, like humans, need adequate sleep to maintain their physical health and mental well-being. However, sometimes dogs may have difficulty settling down or may be suffering from sleep disorders that prevent them from getting the rest they need. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to help your dog get a good night's sleep. Step 1: Establish a Routine Like humans, dogs benefit greatly from having a consistent routine. Try to establish regular feeding times, exercise times, and playtimes. This consistency will help regulate your dog's internal clock, making it easier for them to wind down at bedtime. * Key Tip: Aim to feed your dog around the same time every day. This can help regulate their digestive system and signal to their body when it's time to start winding down for the night. Step 2: Provide Plenty of Exercise A tired dog is a sleeping dog. Make sure your dog gets plenty of exercise during the day. This could include walks, runs, games of fetch, or trips to the dog park. The amount of exercise needed will depend on your dog's age, breed, and size. * Key Guideline: As a general rule, aim for at least one hour of moderate to intense exercise per day for adult dogs. Puppies and high-energy breeds may require more. Step 3: Create a Comfortable Sleeping Environment Your dog needs a comfortable, quiet, and safe place to sleep. This could be a dog bed, crate, or even a designated area on the couch or bed. Make sure the space is free from drafts and has a comfortable temperature. * Key Tip: If your dog seems uncomfortable in their current bed, consider investing in an orthopedic dog bed. These beds are designed to support your dog's joints and muscles, providing extra comfort. Step 4: Limit Distractions Before Bedtime Try to limit distractions and stimulating activities before bedtime. This includes turning off the TV, dimming the lights, and avoiding energetic play sessions. Instead, try calming activities such as petting, grooming, or reading a book together. * Key Guideline: Avoid feeding your dog right before bedtime. Digestion can disrupt sleep, especially if your dog tends to experience stomach issues. Step 5: Use Calming Techniques There are several calming techniques you can use to help your dog relax and fall asleep. These include playing soft music, using essential oils, or providing a calming aid such as a ThunderShirt or Adaptil diffuser. * Key Tip: Always consult with your vet before introducing new products or supplements into your dog's routine. They can provide guidance on what is safe and effective for your specific dog. Step 6: Be Patient It may take some time for your dog to adjust to their new sleep routine. Be patient and consistent. If your dog continues to struggle with sleep, consider consulting with a veterinarian or animal behaviorist. * Key Guideline: Never punish your dog for struggling to sleep. This can create anxiety and further disrupt their sleep patterns. Conclusion: Helping your dog get a good night's sleep involves creating a consistent routine, providing plenty of exercise, creating a comfortable sleeping environment, limiting distractions before bedtime, using calming techniques, and being patient. By following these steps, you can help ensure your dog gets the restful sleep they need to stay healthy and happy. All these great toys and you're just going to eat that one? I'm going to hide in 'ere, and he's going to look all around for me, but he won't see me. Well I don't think that that matters. Put the car on top of the truck? You want the nurse to sit down? Title: How to Perform the Upflip Trick on a Tech Deck Introduction: The upflip is an advanced fingerboarding trick that involves flipping the board while jumping it off a surface. This move requires precision, coordination, and practice. By following this step-by-step guide, you'll learn how to perform the upflip like a pro. Materials Needed: * A Tech Deck fingerboard * Smooth flat surface or rail Step 1: Master the Ollie Before attempting the upflip, ensure you have mastered the ollie technique. The ollie serves as the foundation for most fingerboard tricks, including the upflip. Practice performing consistent ollies until they become second nature. Key Tip: Keep your fingers close together for better control over the board during takeoff and landing. Step 2: Position Your Finger Place your index finger at the edge of the deck, just behind the front truck. Make sure your thumb supports the bottom side of the board near the back trucks for added stability. Maintain a firm yet relaxed grip throughout the trick. Guideline: Avoid gripping too tightly; otherwise, you may hinder the board's motion and increase the risk of injury. Step 3: Popping the Board To pop the board into the air, snap downward with your index finger while applying pressure on the tail using your thumb. As soon as the board leaves the ground, slide your index finger forward along the edge of the deck. Key Point: Timing is crucial when executing the pop. Practicing will help develop muscle memory and improve timing accuracy. Step 4: Initiate the Flip As the board reaches its peak height after the initial pop, lift your index finger gently upward, causing the nose of the board to rise slightly higher than the tail. Simultaneously, flick the tail using your thumb to generate spin. Important Note: To produce enough force for a full flip, make sure your thumb flicks firmly but smoothly. Overdoing it can cause uncontrolled spins or even send the board flying out of reach. Step 5: Jump Off Surface Once initiated, jump off any available surface such as a table, ledge, or ramp. Coordinate your jump so that it coincides with the board reaching its maximum rotational speed midair. Key Guideline: Properly timed jumps allow for smooth landings and reduce chances of injuries. Step 6: Catching the Board While still airborne, extend your middle finger under the spinning deck, preparing to catch it between your thumb and index finger. Focus on guiding the board towards your waiting hand rather than grabbing it abruptly. Critical Tips: * Always maintain visual contact with the board throughout the entire process. * Be prepared to adjust your catching position based on the board's rotation direction. Step 7: Landing the Trick After successfully catching the board, bend your knees slightly upon impact to absorb shock. Gradually straighten them once stable to regain balance. Congratulations! You've executed an upflip on your Tech Deck. Remember, repetition leads to perfection. Don't be discouraged by failed attempts - keep practicing, stay patient, and enjoy improving your skills! Understanding Ringworm -- the Basics What Is Ringworm? Worms don't cause ringworm. Rather, this superficial skin infection, also known as tinea, is caused by fungi called dermatophytes. Fungi are microscopic organisms that can live off the dead tissues of your skin, hair, and nails, much like a mushroom can grow on the bark of a tree. Ringworm is characterized by a red ring of small blisters or a red ring of scaly skin that grows outward as the infection spreads. Though children are especially susceptible to catching ringworm, it can affect adults as well. What Causes Ringworm? Ringworm is caused by a fungus that grows on the skin. Once the fungus is established, it spreads out in rings. The center of the ring may clear up, while a new ring of infection develops at the edge of the old ring. Children are most likely to get ringworm. Ringworm of the scalp can spread from child to child when children share hats, combs, or brushes. Ringworm of the body can be spread on towels, clothing, or sports equipment. Personal hygiene is important in preventing the spread of ringworm. Dogs and cats can be infected with ringworm, too, and they can pass it to people through direct contact. It is kept in the Abbey, and has been used at every coronation since 1308. From 1301 to 1996 (except for 1950 when it was briefly stolen by Scottish nationalists), the chair also housed the Stone of Scone upon which the kings of Scotland are crowned. Although the Stone is now kept in Scotland, at future coronations the Stone will be returned briefy to St Edward's Chair for the moment of coronation. Look at these things go up and down.
