[Mag points at many pictures on the page] Title: How to Get into a Small Hiding Place Quietly Introduction: There are many situations where you might need to get into a small hiding place quietly, such as during a game of hide-and-seek or if you're trying to avoid detection. This tutorial will guide you through the process step by step, ensuring that you can enter your chosen spot without making any noise. By following these instructions carefully, you'll be able to master the art of quiet entry into tight spaces. Step 1: Choose Your Hiding Spot Before attempting to enter your hiding place, take some time to select an appropriate location. Ideally, this should be a snug fit so that you can minimize movement once inside. Common options include crawlspaces, underneath beds, or behind furniture. Make sure there is enough room for you to breathe comfortably and remain hidden for an extended period. Key Tip: Selecting a hiding spot close to the ground can help reduce noise levels further since footsteps tend to create more sound than movements at lower elevations. Step 2: Prepare Your Body To ensure smooth entrance into the hiding space, prepare yourself physically. Bend your knees slightly and crouch down low to the ground. Keep your limbs close to your body and relax your muscles as much as possible. This position reduces the chance of bumping into objects or walls while moving quietly towards your destination. Guideline: Maintain control over your breathing to prevent sudden intakes of air from causing unnecessary sounds. Take slow, deep breaths to keep your body calm and focused. Step 3: Clear Obstacles Inspect the area between your current position and the intended hiding place. Remove any debris, toys, or other items that could make noise when stepped on or brushed against. If necessary, gently move lightweight furniture pieces out of the way, taking care not to let them scrape against the floor. Key Tip: Use your hands and feet to feel for obstructions instead of relying solely on sight. This approach minimizes the risk of knocking something over accidentally. Step 4: Approach Slowly Begin walking slowly and deliberately towards your hiding place, keeping your weight distributed evenly across both feet. Plant each foot gently onto the ground, avoiding heavy stomps or quick steps that may produce loud noises. Guideline: Focus on placing one foot directly in front of the other, maintaining balance and control throughout the approach. Step 5: Enter the Hiding Space As you reach your hiding place, bend down further to slide into the space quietly. Aim to have as little contact with the surrounding surfaces as possible to reduce friction-based sounds. Once inside, shuffle backward until you find a comfortable position that allows for minimal movement. Key Tip: If needed, remove shoes or bulky clothing before entering the hiding space to make it easier to maneuver silently. Step 6: Remain Still and Silent Once situated within your hiding place, maintain complete silence and stillness. Avoid fidgeting or shifting positions unnecessarily, as even slight movements can cause rustling noises that give away your presence. Stay patient and wait for the opportune moment to emerge from your hiding spot. Guideline: Continue focusing on controlling your breathing, taking shallow breaths to minimize any potential sounds produced by exhaling. Conclusion: By following these six steps, you've now learned how to get into a small hiding place quietly. With practice, this skill will become second nature, enabling you to navigate confined spaces discreetly and confidently. Remember to always prioritize safety and consideration for others when engaging in activities requiring stealth and secrecy. What do ya want me to do. You eat them one after another out of the bowl Mark that's rude. Grammar page 2 The second page of the grammar lessons. In Japanese, grammatical parts of the sentence are shown very clearly by "particles." These particles are placed after the word (or phrase) they modify. The best way to learn to use them is to memorize useful examples and say them! See also Particles page. wa - overall topic particle wa - overall topic particle - shows the main topic of the conversation [NOTE: it is a hiragana ha but pronounced as "wa"] anata wa yasashii desu. You are nice. [Makes "you" the main topic] ga - the subject particle ga - the subject particle - sometimes the difference between wa and ga are hard to tell. Sometimes they can be used interchangeably with only a slight change in meaning. Don't worry about this now! neko ga hen desu. The cat is strange. [Makes the "cat" the subject] Comparing and The topic particle can easily be confused with the subject particle . That is because overrides , in other words in a sentence something can very easily be both the topic and the subject of that sentence. In such cases the 'disappears' and it looks like the is acting as a subject marker. Take this simple sentence. watashi wa kurei desu. I am Clay. ["I" (that is the speaker, Clay) is the topic and now this is known, it won't be repeated unless the topic changes] What is the subject of the sentence? That's right - Clay is. But because Clay is also the topic only the topic marker is used. Now we'll let Clay continue and say another sentence ... neko ga suki desu. (I) like cats. ["cats" is the subject here. "I" is still the topic. He could have said "watashi wa neko ga suki desu." but that is unnecessary because he has already said "watashi wa" establishing the topic in the previous sentence.] if both are in a sentence, the wa is first o - The Direct Object particle o - The Direct Object particle hon o yomimashita. (I) read a book. [NOTE: it makes "book" the object. If we were to say "I" it would be watashi wa at the beginning.] ni - usually shows movement (to) ni - usually shows movement (to) nihon ni ikimashou! Let's go to Japan! [There is movement going to Japan] or shows time (at) roku ji ni ikimashou! Let's go at 6. de - Shows location (at, in) de - Shows location (at, in) nihon de asobimashou! Let's play (have fun) in Japan! [Notice there is no movement] mo means "also" or "too" and like other particles, it is placed after the word it modifies. Let's see some examples: watashi wa neko ga suki desu. I like cats. watashi wa neko ga suki, soshite inu mo suki desu. I like cats, and I also like dogs. N.B. The mo after inu replaces ga. You can't say "ga mo" watashi mo neko to inu ga suki desu. I also like cats and dogs. NOTE: watashi mo by itself means "Me too." STOP and test yourself. Adjectives (part 1) There are 2 types of adjectives: -i adjectives - adjectives that end in 'i', if you like their 'dictionary form' ends in 'i'. -na adjectives - adjectives that add -na when placed before nouns The -i adjectives change: | atsui - (It's) hot||i| | atsukunai - not hot||-i + kunai| | atsukatta - was hot||-i + katta| | atsukunakatta - wasn't hot||-i + kunakatta| The -na adjectives don't change! But when placed before nouns they add a -na genki (healthy, active, fine) genki na ko (healthy child) The basic definition of adjectives is that they go before a noun to modify or further define it. For example in English "Car." -> "Red car." Japanese does just the same " kuruma" -> " akai kuruma". More on what can be done with adjectives in Japanese later. There are several ways to say "and" (connecting things). Let's look at 2 of them to - connecting nouns watashi wa nihongo to eigo to furansugo ga hanasemasu. I can speak Japanese and English and French. soshite - connecting phrases watashi wa nihongo ga hanasemasu. soshite, doitsugo ga yomemasu. I can speak Japanese and I can read German. But, a small word, but... There are other "buts" but demo is the most common. Learn this first and you can pick the others up later. demo - but nihongo ga suki demo, furansugo wa kirai desu. I like Japanese, but I hate French. In English, we have our "um." in Japanese, they have their "eeto." This is the sound you make when you can't think of what to say, but want to say something! nan no doubutsu ga suki desu ka? What animal do you like? eeto..., neko ga suki desu. Um..., I like cats. Sometimes mom's cooking isn't just oishii (delicious) it is VERY OISHII! Add totemo before adjectives to say "very" totemo oishii desu. It's very delicious! totemo ookina ki desu. It is a very big tree. OTHER VERY WORDS hijou ni chou (kind of slang - chou means "super-") I think This goes at the end to show that you believe what you say, but are not 100% sure. It is also used to show one's opinion. If there is a desu change it to da which is the more casual form and add to omoimasu 1. The speaker is not totally sure of the accuracy of his info... kuma no pu-san wa kuma da to omoimasu. Winnie the Pooh is a bear, I think... Next is an example of showing one's opinion. It is true for the speaker, but may not be so for the listener. nattou wa oishii to omoimasu. I think Natto is delicious Basically you can say any sentence and if you want to soften it or show you are not sure, or show your opinion add to omoimasu To want Saying "I want (something)" is pretty easy. Just say the thing you want and add ga hoshii to it. nomimono ga hoshii desu. (I) want a drink. NOTE: The desu is optional and is usually dropped. nomimono ga hoshii. is perfectly fine in spoken Japanese. Next, let's ask a question. Can you figure out how to do it? That's right add a ka ke-ki ga hoshii desu ka. Do you want cake? Want to do~ First get the masu form of the verb you want to do. Then drop the masu and add tai. tabemasu (to eat) tabe tabetai (want to eat) nomimasu (to drink) nomi nomitai (want to drink) shimasu (to do) shi shitai (want to do) Of course if you want to say "do you want to..." Just add ka ke-ki o tabetai desu ka. Do you want to eat cake? There is / There are For inanimate objects (objects, plants...), end the sentence with ga arimasu ki desu. It's a tree. [lit. tree is.] ki ga arimasu. There is a tree(s). For living things (people and animals) use ga imasu. neko ga imasu. There is a cat(s). To show the negative just add -sen to the end arimasu arimasen Another more casual form of arimasu that you don't have to learn now is... aru nai imasu imasen Another more casual form of imasu that you don't have to learn now is... iru inai Maybe you know these useful phrases: onegai ga arimasu. I have a favor to ask. mondai nai. No problem! [this is the casual form of arimasen] To like... It is easy to like something and to say it! Just add ga suki after the object that you like: neko ga suki desu. I like cats. [note: Nouns don't change in number (no s) so it could mean "a cat". Also note the desu is often dropped in speech - "neko ga suki." is fine!] 2 ways to say "why" are: - naze - why - doushite - why They are basically interchangeable and start at the beginning of the sentence and are followed by the question naze (doushite) watashi no ke-ki o tabemashita ka? Why did you eat my cake? [There isn't a "you" but obviously you wouldn't be asking yourself this question.] + reason or excuse + kara nazenara hara ga hetta kara. Because, (I'm) starving! Title: How to Grow a Tea Plant Introduction: Tea is one of the most popular beverages globally, with millions of people enjoying its soothing warmth every day. While many purchase pre-packaged tea leaves from stores, growing your own tea plant (Camellia sinensis) offers numerous benefits such as access to fresh leaves, cost savings, and even potential income if you choose to sell excess produce. This extensive guide will walk you through all necessary steps to successfully cultivate and maintain a thriving tea plantation at home or in your garden. Step 1: Choose the Right Location Selecting an appropriate site for your tea plants is crucial for their healthy growth. They prefer partial shade but can tolerate full sun if there's ample moisture availability. Avoid locations exposed to strong winds which may damage the delicate branches and foliage. Also, ensure good drainage since waterlogged soil could lead to root rot. Step 2: Purchase Healthy Seedlings Purchasing healthy seedlings from reputable nurseries increases the chances of successful establishment. Look for compact, bushy plants about 6 inches tall with vibrant green leaves free from discoloration, spots, or holes. It's recommended to buy multiple seedlings to allow cross-pollination, enhancing fruit production. When Nine Inch Nails plays at concerts, other musicians are also part of the band. And let's look at the next page. This problem appeared: If the points (a,2a) (2a,a), (a,a)and enclose a triangle of area 18 sq units, the centroid of the triangle is ? Let's solve it with geogebra! 1)Create a slider called a and range it from 0 to 10. 2) Enter points (a,a),(a,2a)(2a,a). 3) Use the polygon tool to create a triangle with those 3 points as the vertices. 4) Get the midpoints of the 3 sides of the triangle using the midpoint tool. 5) Draw line segments from each vertices to the opposite midpoint. 6) Get the intersection of those 3 line segments and call it point G. That is the centroid. 7) Move the slider until the triangle has an area of 18. You should be able to get it exactly. 8) Read off the coordinates of the centroid. What did you get? Living things are all different from each other. Even living things of the same species look, move, and behave differently. Some differences make it easier for living things to survive and have babies. Differences may make it easier to find food, hide from danger, or have babies. The babies keep some of the differences that made it easier for their parents to have them. Over time, these differences get bigger and living things change enough to be a new species. Title: How to Capo a Fret on an Acoustic Guitar Introduction: A capo is a device used on the neck of a stringed instrument to shorten the playable length of the strings, which raises the pitch. This can be useful when you want to change the key of a song without changing the fingerings you're using. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to capo a fret on an acoustic guitar. Step 1: Understand the Purpose of a Capo Before we start, it's important to understand why we use a capo. A capo allows us to transpose songs into different keys while maintaining the same hand shape or fingering pattern as open chords. It essentially functions like your barring hand but requires less strength because it clamps down on all the strings at once. Step 2: Choose Your Capo There are several types of capos available, including trigger (or quick-change) capos, screw-on capos, locking capos, and partial capos. For beginners, atrigger capois recommended due to its ease of use. However, any kind will do the job; just make sure it fits your guitar well. Step 3: Decide Which Frets To Capo The choice depends on what key you need for the song. Each fret raised increases the pitch by approximately half step. So if you need to go up one whole step from standard tuning (EADGBE), you would place the capo on the second fret. Key Tip: If you don't know which fret corresponds to which note, there are numerous online tools and apps that can help you figure this out. Step 4: Position the Capo Correctly Slide the capo along the fretboard until it rests just behind the desired fret. The curvature should match the radius of the fretboard so that each string is pressed evenly against the fret. Key Tips: - Avoid placing the capo directly over the metal frets, as this could dampen the sound or cause buzzing. - Make sure the capo isn't too tight; it shouldn't bend the neck or warp the strings. Step 5: Secure the Capo If you're using a spring-loaded capo, squeeze the handle gently to release the tension, position it correctly, then release the handle. The spring should now hold the capo firmly in place. With other kinds of capos, you might have to manually turn a knob or lever to secure it. Step 6: Check Tuning After Applying the Capo Even though capos are designed to maintain tune, they may slightly alter it. Therefore, after applying the capo, check your guitar's tuning with a chromatic tuner. Retune any strings that seem sharp or flat. Step 7: Play Your Guitar Now you should be able to play your guitar as usual, except everything will sound a bit higher pitched. Remember, since the capo effectively moves the nut further up the neck, open chord shapes will produce notes as if played further up the neck. Conclusion: Using a capo opens up many possibilities for playing around with keys and voicings. While this guide focuses on acoustic guitars, these steps generally apply to electric guitars and other similar instruments as well. Practice makes perfect, so keep practicing until you feel comfortable using a capo. Happy strumming! What did the boys get to do that you couldn't do? In the USSR, the October Revolution of 1917 was celebrated in November. In 1917 the Russian Empire still used the old Julian calendar. Title: How to Argue with a Jonas Brothers Fan Introduction: While it's generally best to avoid arguments and enjoy friendly discussions instead, sometimes disagreements arise, especially when it comes to passionate fandoms like that of the Jonas Brothers. This guide will provide you with steps to argue constructively while maintaining respect for the other person's viewpoint. Remember, the goal is not necessarily to change their mind but rather to have a meaningful conversation where both parties can learn from one another. Step 1: Understand Your Audience Before diving into an argument, take some time to understand who you are talking to. Are they a casual fan or a hardcore stan? What aspects of the Jonas Brothers do they appreciate most - music, personalities, or perhaps acting careers? Knowing this information will help tailor your argument in a way that resonates with them. Key Tip: Show genuine interest in understanding why they love the Jonas Brothers so much. Ask open-ended questions and listen attentively. Step 2: Choose Your Points Carefully When arguing about something as subjective as musical taste, it's essential only to bring up valid points backed by evidence. Stick to facts such as album sales figures, critical reception, or lyrical analysis. Avoid making assumptions or using personal attacks. Guideline: Always be prepared to support your claims with solid evidence. A quick Google search should give you enough ammunition for any debate! Step 3: Stay Calm and Composed It's easy to get carried away during heated debates, leading to raised voices and hurt feelings. However, remember that staying calm and composed can lead to more productive conversations. Speak slowly, maintain eye contact, and keep your tone even. Key Tip: Take deep breaths between exchanges to collect your thoughts and prevent yourself from getting overly emotional. Step 4: Encourage Dialogue Rather than simply stating your opinion, encourage dialogue by asking thoughtful questions related to your point. For example, if discussing the quality of the Jonas Brothers' latest album, ask what they think about specific songs or lyrics. Guideline: Use phrases like "I wonder" or "Have you considered..." to frame your questions neutrally. Step 5: Respect Their Opinion Even if you strongly disagree with someone else's views, it's crucial to acknowledge and respect their right to hold those opinions. By doing so, you create a safe space for open discussion without fear of judgment or ridicule. Key Tip: Preface your statements with expressions of understanding, such as "I understand where you're coming from, but " Step 6: Agree To Disagree Sometimes, despite our best efforts, we won't see eye-to-eye with others. When faced with this situation, agreeing to disagree allows both parties to walk away gracefully without further escalating the argument. Guideline: Thank the other person for sharing their perspective and express hope for future civil discourse. Conclusion: Arguing with a Jonas Brothers fan doesn't have to be a negative experience. By following these steps, you can engage in constructive dialogues that foster mutual understanding and growth. Keep an open mind, stay respectful, and always prioritize learning over winning. [examining the contents of the toy container] Title: How to Call Spain: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Making an international call can be daunting at first, especially when calling a country like Spain with its unique dialing code. However, by following this simple guide, you'll learn exactly how to make that call quickly and efficiently. This article will provide step-by-step instructions, including key tips and guidelines for clear communication. Step 1: Identify Your Type of Phone Determine whether you are using a landline or mobile phone as different procedures may apply slightly depending on your device. Most modern phones have similar processes so these steps should serve as a universal guide. Step 2: Check the Current Time Before making the call, ensure it is not too late or early in Spain. The country spans several time zones (Central European Standard Time and Central European Summer Time), but generally, their day starts about an hour ahead of Greenwich Mean Time (GMT). Always respect the local customs regarding appropriate times to call. Step 3: Know the Spanish Number Structure Spanish numbers consist of nine digits, often preceded by a two-digit area code starting with '9'. For instance, Madrid's area code is '91', while Barcelona's is '93'. When calling from abroad, drop the initial '0' from the area code. Step 4: Dial the Exit Code Every international call requires exiting your home country's network first. From the United States, this exit code is '011'; from the UK, it's '00'; and from Canada, it's also '00'. After dialing the exit code, there will usually be a pause before continuing - no need to rush! Step 5: Enter Spain's Country Code Next, input Spain's country code which is +34. Remember to replace the plus sign (+) with whatever equivalent your phone uses if manually entering symbols isn't possible. On many devices, holding down the zero key produces the plus sign. Step 6: Add the Area Code and Local Number Now combine the previously noted area code without its leading zero and then add the eight-digit local number. So if you wanted to reach a Madrid number (91 XXXX XXXX), after the exit code and country code, you would enter '91' followed by the remaining eight digits. Step 7: Initiate the Call Press the call button or equivalent symbol on your device. Wait for the ring tone indicating connection; remember, due to distance, this might take longer than domestic calls. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Consider cost: International calls can be expensive, particularly from landlines or non-contract cellular plans. Look into VoIP services like Skype or Google Voice, or consider adding an international calling plan to your current service. - Speak clearly and slowly: Accents and language barriers can sometimes complicate conversations. Articulate your words carefully and enunciate vowels for better understanding. - Be aware of cultural differences: Spaniards tend to value personal relationships and polite conversation more than Anglo cultures do. Take the time to engage socially before diving into business discussions. Conclusion: By now, you should feel confident in your ability to place a successful call to Spain. With patience, practice, and awareness of cultural nuances, maintaining connections across borders becomes much simpler. Happy calling! [bulges out eyes at pause, meaning huge] Title: How to Apply Bactroban: A Comprehensive Guide Bactroban (mupirocin) is an antibiotic cream used to treat skin infections caused by bacteria. Before using this medication, it's crucial to understand the proper application techniques to ensure its effectiveness and prevent potential side effects or resistance. This article will provide you with a detailed, step-by-step guide on applying Bactrobin, complete with explanations, key tips, and guidelines. **Step 1: Gather Necessary Materials** Before starting, make sure you have everything you need at hand. You'll require: * Prescribed tube(s) of Bactroban ointment or cream * Clean washcloth or gauze pads * Gentle soap * Warm water * Dry towel * Latex gloves (optional but recommended) **Step 2: Wash Your Hands Thoroughly** Washing your hands before handling any medication prevents contamination and reduces the risk of infection spreading. Use warm water and gentle soap, scrubbing all parts of your hands, including under fingernails, for at least 20 seconds. Rinse thoroughly and dry completely with a clean towel. **Step 3: Prepare The Skin Area** Clean the affected area gently with mild soap and warm water. Avoid harsh soaps that can irritate the skin further. Pat dry with a soft cloth or let air dry. Make sure the area is completely dry before proceeding to avoid diluting the medication. **Step 4: Put On Gloves (Optional)** Although optional, wearing latex gloves while applying Bactroban minimizes the chance of transferring bacteria from your hands to other parts of your body. It also maintains hygiene during repeated applications throughout the day. **Step 5: Apply Bactroban Correctly** Squeeze a small amount of Bactroban onto your finger or a cotton swab. Spread a thin layer over the entire infected area once or twice daily, depending on your doctor's instructions. Massage gently until the medicine disappears into the skin. Be careful not to rub too hard as it may cause discomfort or pain. **Key Tips:** * Only apply enough medication to cover the affected area; excess amounts won't increase efficacy and could lead to waste. * Don't cover the treated area unless directed by your healthcare provider, as bandages might trap moisture leading to additional skin issues. **Step 6: Dispose Of Any Unused Medication Properly** After usage, close the cap tightly on the tube. Store it in a cool place away from direct sunlight and out of reach of children and pets. If there are leftovers after finishing treatment, discard them properly according to local regulations. Never reuse old tubes or share them with others due to risks of cross-contamination and development of bacterial resistance. **Step 7: Monitor Progress And Report To Healthcare Professional** Keep track of improvements in the infected site. Notify your physician if symptoms persist or worsen after several days of treatment. Also, report any signs of allergies such as rashes, hives, or difficulty breathing immediately. By following these steps meticulously, you maximize the benefits of Bactroban and promote faster recovery. Remember, always consult your healthcare professional regarding questions about medications and their uses. It looks like we've got a baby hippo. Title: How to Call an Uber Driver on Your iPhone or iPad Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of calling an Uber driver using your iPhone or iPad. By following these steps, you'll be able to request a ride with ease and convenience. This article includes detailed explanations, helpful tips, and best practices to ensure a smooth experience. Step 1: Download and Install the Uber App To start, you need to download the official Uber app from the Apple App Store if you haven't already done so. Open the App Store on your device, search for 'Uber', and tap 'Get'. Confirm the installation by entering your Apple ID password or using Touch ID / Face ID. The Uber app is now installed on your device. Key tip: Make sure your device has enough storage space before installing new apps. Step 2: Create or Log into Your Uber Account Launch the Uber app and either create a new account or log in using your existing credentials. If creating a new account, provide the necessary information such as name, email address, phone number, and payment method. Already having an account? Simply enter your login details and proceed. Guideline: Keep your account details secure and up-to-date. Step 3: Verify Your Location Services Ensure that your location services are enabled for the Uber app. Go to 'Settings' > 'Privacy' > 'Location Services', find the Uber app in the list, and make sure its toggle is set to 'While Using the App'. This allows Uber to accurately detect your pickup location. Key tip: Accurate location data ensures prompt arrival for your Uber driver. Step 4: Request a Ride Tap on the 'Where to?' field at the bottom center of the screen to input your destination. You can type in the address manually or allow Uber access to your contacts to select a contact's address quickly. After confirming your dropoff point, choose the preferred vehicle type based on your needs (e.g., economy, premium, SUV). Finally, hit the 'Request' button. Guideline: Always double-check your entered addresses for accuracy. Step 5: Wait for Your Driver Once you have requested a ride, wait for a nearby Uber driver to accept your request. While waiting, you can view their estimated time of arrival, profile picture, car model, license plate number, and rating within the Uber app. Additionally, track their real-time progress on the map provided. Key tip: Contact your driver directly via the in-app messaging feature if needed during this stage. Step 6: Enter the Vehicle and Begin Your Journey When your Uber driver arrives, check their identity against the information displayed in the Uber app. Once confirmed, greet them politely, and enter the vehicle. During the trip, feel free to ask any questions about the route or local attractions; however, remember that drivers are prohibited from accepting cash tips. Guideline: Maintain good manners throughout your interaction with the driver. Step 7: Rate Your Driver and Complete Payment After reaching your destination, exit the vehicle and say farewell to your driver. To complete the transaction, rate your driver within the Uber app (from 1 to 5 stars) and leave feedback if desired. The appropriate amount will then be automatically charged to your stored payment method. Key tip: Providing accurate ratings and constructive feedback help improve the overall quality of service offered by Uber drivers. The picture on the right shows a shark. This shark's shape is called "fusiform", and it is an ovoid shape where both ends of the fish are pointy. This is the best shape for going through water quickly. Fishes with fusiform shapes can chase prey and escape predators quickly. Many live in the open ocean and swim constantly, like marlins, swordfish, and tuna. Ichthyosaurs, porpoises, dolphins, killer whales all have similar shapes. I shall be delighted to get out of this skyscraper for a few days." Title: How to Handle People Who Are Angry at You Handling someone who is angry at you can be a challenging situation. However, with patience, empathy, and effective communication, you can help defuse their anger and work towards resolving any underlying issues. Here's an in-depth guide on how to handle people who are angry at you: ### Step 1: Stay Calm and Composed When confronted by an angry person, your natural reaction might be to become defensive or upset yourself. Instead, take a deep breath and maintain composure. This will help prevent further escalation of emotions and demonstrate that you are willing to listen and understand their perspective. Keeping calm also allows you to think more clearly so you can respond appropriately. Key Tip: Practice mindfulness techniques such as deep breathing exercises to stay centered during heated conversations. ### Step 2: Listen Actively Give the angry individual your undivided attention. Show them that you value their opinion by listening attentively without interrupting. Allow them to express themselves fully before responding. By actively listening, you convey respect for their feelings and thoughts, which can go a long way toward easing tensions. Guideline: Use nonverbal cues like nodding and maintaining eye contact to show that you are engaged in the conversation. Avoid checking your phone or looking away while they speak. ### Step 3: Empathize With Their Emotions Try to put yourself in their shoes and understand why they are feeling angry. Acknowledge their emotions by saying something like, "I understand why you would feel frustrated in this situation." Validating their feelings does not mean agreeing with their actions but rather shows compassion and understanding. Key Tip: Refrain from using phrases such as "calm down" or "you shouldn't be mad," as these dismiss their emotions and may provoke stronger reactions. ### Step 4: Clarify Misunderstandings If there has been a misunderstanding between you two, clarify it gently and honestly. Ask questions to ensure you have accurate information about what caused their anger. Once you both have a clear understanding of the issue, it becomes easier to address and resolve. Guideline: Phrase your questions carefully to avoid sounding accusatory or defensive. For example, say, "Could you please explain to me how this happened?" instead of "Didn't you check this before submitting it?" ### Step 5: Apologize Sincerely It began about another wretched construe I handed in to Swain. "Is this your own work, Stewart?" asked Swain; and I thought it was so good he could hardly believe I had done it, and I said, quite proudly, "Yes, sir, I've done every word of it." "Then all I can say is, you have no right to be in this division of the school; and I shall talk to the Doctor about it." He was turning over the leaves of the exercise-book while he was talking; and presently, turning up one of the cribs, he said, "Look here, Stewart; who did this?" "I wrote that a month ago, sir," I said. "Yes, I know you wrote it, but who did the construing?" I looked at Swain, and then at the map on the wall, for I didn't know who had done it. (Romantism) Imagine you are on a camping trip and you have filled up a backpack with rocks to different heights. Now, let's say you want to know how much the total weight of the rocks changes when you move to a new spot in the woods. To find out, you could calculate the change in weight by adding up the differences in height (or "gradient") of each rock times its own individual weight (its "density"). This would give you the total change in weight based on how much each individual rock contributes. But instead of doing all that addition, there's another way to figure it out. Instead of thinking about the change in weight caused by each individual rock, imagine wrapping a rubber sheet around your entire backpack. When you move to a new spot, the rubber sheet will stretch or contract to fit the new shape of the bag. By measuring the amount of stretching or contracting (the "flux"), you can determine the overall change in weight! This concept is similar to something called the Divergence Theorem in mathematics. It allows us to convert a volume integral into a surface integral, making calculations easier. In other words, just like how we can measure the change in weight of our backpack by looking at how much the rubber sheet stretches, mathematicians can simplify complex problems by converting one type of calculation into another. In summary, sometimes there are multiple ways to approach a math problem. Just like how we can calculate the change in weight of a backpack by either adding up the contributions of each individual rock or by measuring how much a rubber sheet stretches, mathematicians can use techniques like the Divergence Theorem to make their calculations more straightforward. here's more tools over here too. Choosing a residency program is an important decision that can significantly impact your career as a medical professional. This guide will provide you with in-depth information and actionable steps to help you make an informed choice. Step 1: Self-Assessment Before starting your search for a residency program, conduct a thorough self-assessment. Identify your strengths, weaknesses, interests, and goals. Consider factors such as geographical preferences, lifestyle choices, and family considerations. Understanding yourself better will enable you to find a program that aligns with your personal and professional objectives. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Be honest about your abilities and limitations. * Seek feedback from mentors or colleagues who know you well. * Reflect on past experiences to determine what type of environment suits you best. Step 2: Researching Programs Once you have identified your priorities, begin researching potential programs. Consult resources like FREIDA (Fellowship and Residency Electronic Interactive Database), official specialty society websites, and individual institution pages. Look at features such as curriculum structure, faculty qualifications, patient population, research opportunities, and clinical sites. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Create a list of 'must-haves' and 'nice-to-haves'. * Keep track of application deadlines and requirements. * Reach out to current residents or alumni if possible to gain firsthand insights. Step 3: Applying to Programs After narrowing down your options, prepare and submit your applications through ERAS (Electronic Residency Application Service). Make sure to tailor your personal statement to each specific program, highlighting why you are interested in them and how you could contribute to their community. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Request letters of recommendation early. * Highlight any unique skills or accomplishments. * Proofread all documents carefully before submission. Step 4: Preparing for Interviews If selected, participate in interviews which usually occur between November and January. Prepare answers for common questions, learn about the program, and formulate thoughtful queries of your own. Remember, this is also a chance for you to evaluate whether the program meets your needs. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Dress professionally. * Show enthusiasm and genuine interest. * Send thank-you notes after each interview. Step 5: Ranking Programs Using the rank order list provided by NRMP (National Resident Matching Program), list your preferred programs based on your interview experience and overall fit. The algorithm used in the match process pairs applicants with programs according to these ranks. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Prioritize based on both personal and professional criteria. * Be realistic about chances at top-tier institutions. * Include enough safety choices to ensure matching. Step 6: Awaiting the Match Results In mid-March, match results are released via the NRMP's Registration, Ranking, and Results system. If successful, contact the program directly to accept the offer and start planning for relocation. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Remain calm during the waiting period. * Have a backup plan ready in case of non-match. * Celebrate your achievement once matched! By following these comprehensive steps, you increase your likelihood of finding a residency program that supports your growth as a healthcare provider and sets you up for success in your chosen field. And paint things you're going to paint when you go to school next year aren't ya? I thought you said they're going to the park? How Do Plants Know Which Way Is Up And Which Way Is Down? Originally published on Fri June 22, 2012 4:32 pm Think of a seed buried in a pot. Like this one: It's dark down there in the potting soil. There's no light, no sunshine. So how does it know which way is up and which way is down? It does know. Seeds routinely send shoots up toward the sky, and roots the other way. Darkness doesn't confuse them. Somehow, they get it right... More intriguing, if you turn a seedling (or a whole bunch of seedlings) upside down, as Thomas Andrew Knight of the British Royal Society did around 200 years ago, the tips and roots of the plant will sense, "Hey, I'm upside down," and will wiggle their way to the right direction, doing a double U-turn, like this: How do they know? According to botanist Daniel Chamovitz, Thomas Knight 200 years ago assumed that plants must sense gravity. They feel the pull of the Earth. Knight proved it with a crazy experiment involving a spinning plate. He attached a bunch of plant seedlings onto a disc (think of a 78 rpm record made of wood). The plate was then turned by a water wheel powered by a local stream, "at a nauseating speed of 150 revolutions per minute for several days." If you've ever been at amusement park in a spinning tea cup, you know that because of centrifugal force you get pushed away from the center of the spinning object toward the outside. Knight wondered, would the plants respond to the centrifugal pull of gravity and point their roots to the outside of the spinning plate? When he looked... ...that's what they'd done. Every plant on the disc had responded to the pull of gravity, and pointed its roots to the outside. The roots pointed out, the shoots pointed in. So Thomas Knight proved that plants can and do sense gravitational pull. But he couldn't explain how. We humans have teeny crystalline stones floating in our ear cavities that literally sink in response to gravity, telling us what's up and what's down. What do plants have? Strangely, this is a real puzzle. We still don't know for sure how plants do it. There is a team of botanists, John Kiss and his colleagues at Miami University in Ohio, who have a promising idea, but at the moment it's just a very educated guess. Plants have special cells right down at the tip - the very bottom - of their roots. And if you look closely, inside these cells there are dense, little ball like structures called "statoliths" which comes from the Greek, meaning "stationary stone." You can see them here. I think of them as pebbles inside a jar. If the jar is upright, the pebbles, naturally, fall to the bottom. If I put the jar on its side, the pebbles will roll to the side of the jar, the new bottom, and lie there. If I turn the jar upside down, the pebbles will drop into the cap, which used to be the top but is now the bottom. Basically these little pebbly things respond to gravity. In a plant cell, gravity pulls them to the "bottom," and once they find a resting place, they can send signals to neighboring cells in the plant essentially saying, "OK guys! We now know where Down is. Those of you that need to go down (root cells), go this way! Those of you who need to go up (the shoot on top), go the other way!" This, suggests Professor Kiss, is how plants figure out where "down" is. They use little statolith balls as gravity receptors. His idea got a boost when he sent some seedlings into space (to the space station) where the pull of gravity is close to zero, figuring if the statoliths just float randomly and don't drop to the bottom of their cells, the plants won't know which way is down. And sure enough, he reported that plants growing in space did not send their roots in any specific direction. The roots just went every which way. So the next time you pass a tree, a flower, a grape vine, grasses, bushes, vegetables any plant that seems to be reaching for the sky, that plant may be going up not just because it wants to be kissed by the sun, but also because down at its bottom, in cells rooted in the Earth, it's got itty bitty rocks telling it, "go thattaway!" Daniel Chamovitz's account of how plants tell up from down comes from his new book, What A Plant Knows: A Field Guide to the Senses. The drawings are my own, and are based on Professor Chamovitz's descriptions. If there's something wrong with them, the fault is mine, not his. She knows the form of cheese as in like cheese sticks. Hello young mathematicians! Today, let's learn about a fun concept called "transformations." Imagine you have a magical shape-shifting box. When you put something into this box, it changes according to specific rules. In our case, we will work with graphs (visual representations of mathematical equations), and we want to see how they change when we apply these transformation rules. Our story begins with the simplest possible graph of a quadratic equation, which looks like a parabola. The basic equation is $f(x)=x^{2}$. Now, meet its transformed friend, $h(x)=x^{2}-9$. Can you spot any differences between them? Don't worry; I will guide you through understanding their relationship using our magical shape-shifting box! Firstly, both functions belong to the same family of quadratic equations, but they appear different due to some special adjustments made inside our magical shape-shifting box. To convert $f(x)$ into $h(x)$, follow these steps: Step 1: Place the original graph inside the box. Step 2: Decide on a transformation. This time, we need to move the entire graph down by 9 units, making it closer to the x-axis. Step 3: Perform the transformation inside the box. Voila! You now have the new graph, $h(x)$. As you can see, all the points on the graph moved down by 9 units, just as we wanted! So, there you have it! With a single step, we changed the position of our initial graph and created a brand new one. Transforming functions can be as easy as playing with a magical shape-shifting box. Keep practicing, and soon you'll master creating various graphs using these exciting techniques! Happy mathing! there's a boy and a girl. I guess it requires a lot of thought. Fatty acids and fatty acid derivatives are another group of carboxylic acids that play a significant role in biology. These contain long chains of hydrocarbons and a carboxylic acid group on one end. It tasted so sweet and so refreshing." Grandpa took a deep breath. "Can I have a drink too?" he asked the bird. The bird replied, "Absolutely! I have another bottle nearby." They flew together until they arrived at the spot where the bird got its drink. Grandpa saw a small pond with murky waters. He asked the bird, "Is this the drink?" His voice trembled with worry. The bird replied, "Yes, it is. My friends and I come here every morning to have a drink." Grandpa raised his eyebrows and asked, "Is this drink safe to drink?" Little did the bird know that it was drinking poison - the pond was so murky that it couldn't tell the Let's learn about a fun pattern game! Imagine you have a magical box with a secret rule. This rule tells you how to transform any whole number into another whole number. The magic word for this rule is "f". To perform the trick, follow these steps: 1. If your number is 1, just leave it alone because our rule says f(1) = 1. 2. For any other number (let's call it n), subtract the result of applying the rule to the previous number, f(n-1): f(n) = n - f(f(n-1)). For example, let's see what happens when we apply the rule to the numbers 1 through 5: * f(1) = 1 * f(2) = 2 - f(f(1)) = 2 - f(1) = 1 * f(3) = 3 - f(f(2)) = 3 - f(1) = 2 * f(4) = 4 - f(f(3)) = 4 - f(2) = 3 * f(5) = 5 - f(f(4)) = 5 - f(3) = 4 Great job! You've discovered one of the secrets of this magical box. Now comes the most intriguing part: there's a hidden mystery inside the sequence of transformed numbers. I challenge you to find out if adding the original number to its corresponding transformed value will lead you back to where you started! In mathematical language, we want to figure out whether f(n + f(n)) equals n for every positive integer n. To solve this riddle, we need to dive deeper into the behavior of our mysterious function f. By observing closely, you may notice two interesting patterns (which mathematicians like to call Property A and Property B): **Property A:** Each output of the function f(n) is exactly the same as f(n-1) or f(n-1) plus one. **Property B:** When the output of f(n) doesn't change compared to f(n-1), the next outcome increases by one: f(n+1) becomes f(n) + 1. These clues should guide us towards solving the big question: Is it true that f(n + f(n)) = n for all positive integers n? Well, no one knows yet! But here's an idea-why don't you try investigating this fascinating puzzle yourself? Experiment with different numbers and share your findings with friends. Remember, math is best learned together, having fun along the way! In accounts where Laozi married, he was said to have had a son named Zong. They are separated when Zong is a child. (e.g., have scales, come from eggs, breathe through their gills) - Discover how fish move and eat. - Fish collage/chart from Lesson Two - K-W-L Chart from Lesson One - Index cards - 3-4 goldfish - Live water plant - Clear plastic container - Pictures of fish from magazines, index cards, etc.: 12 pictures total; 6 pairs of matching fish - Colored construction paper in blue, yellow, orange, white, green, red, and black - Chart paper Set Up and Prepare Group your students for rotation through four independent centers. Set up the centers as follows: - Fish Memory Game: Glue the 12 fish pictures onto index cards. - Fish Habits: Set goldfish and a live water plant in a small clear-plastic container. Display the fish diagram you make with the students. They will observe the fish, sketch what they're doing, and label their parts. - Draw and Glue a Fish: Cut half circles of construction paper, at least 10-20 for each student, depending on size. Set out the Fish collage/chart, paper, crayons, glue, paper, scissors, and the construction paper half-circles. Students will draw a fish and then glue the construction paper half-circles for scales. They can use the Fish collage/chart from Lesson One for ideas. - Fish Read-a-Thon: Provide books about fish, both non-fiction and fiction. Students will read various books about fish. Step 1: Gather students together to review the fish song from Lesson One. As you sing the song, display the fish collage/chart. Encourage them to call out any names of the fish they might know. Remind them that all animals have body coverings to help them survive. What do fish have covering their bodies? (scales) Tell the children that most animals use their mouth and nose to breathe. What do fish use to breathe? (gills) Ask them if fish come from their mother's bodies or eggs? (eggs) Step 2: On a large piece of chart paper, make a simple sketch of a fish. Introduce their body parts and model labeling them: eye, mouth, tail, fins, gills, fins. Have children assist you where possible. Step 3: Show students that they'll be going to different centers to explore fish. Explain the tasks for each of the four centers. Step 1: Rotate students through the centers in groups - about 10-15 minutes in each. Step 2: When finished, gather children together to share what they learned. Add to the K-W-L Chart. Use a different color marker when writing student responses to indicate a new entry. Supporting All Learners When creating groups, combine high achievers with low or medium achievers. Have students use crayons to create an underwater scene. Paint over the crayon with blue watercolor paint to show the fish habitat. (The paint won't stick to the crayon.) Ask students to bring in a book or toy that depicts a fish and share them. - Complete a fish sketch with labeled body parts. - Complete a fish drawing with scales. - Did students work well in their groups? - Were they engaged and on task the entire time? - How might you do this lesson differently next time? Title: Understanding Angel Number 747: A Comprehensive Guide ========================================================= Introduction ------------ Angel numbers are believed to be messages from guardian angels or spiritual guides that appear as repeating number sequences in our daily lives. These numbers can hold significant meaning and offer guidance, reassurance, or warnings about various aspects of life. One such powerful angel number is 747. This article will provide an in-depth analysis of angel number 747, its interpretation, significance, and implications in your life. By understanding this divine message, you'll gain valuable insights into personal growth, relationships, career, and spiritual development. ### Step 1: Familiarize Yourself with Angel Numbers Concepts Before diving deep into the specific meaning of 747, familiarize yourself with general concepts related to angel numbers. Here are some essential points to consider: * **Divine Messages**: Guardian angels communicate through signs and symbols; one common way they do so is by showing us repetitive numerical patterns. Recognizing these signals allows you to receive their wisdom and support. * **Personal Meaning**: While there are established interpretations for individual digits (such as 0 to 9), angel numbers take on unique meanings based on the context of the recipient's life situation. Therefore, understanding the overall message requires self-reflection and introspection. * **Positivity and Encouragement**: Most often, angel numbers carry positive affirmation and motivation. They serve as reminders to focus on your strengths, trust the universe, and maintain balance between materialistic goals and spiritual pursuits. Key Tip: Keep an open mind when interpreting angel numbers. Remember that while numerology offers helpful guidelines, only you have access to your inner thoughts and feelings required to fully grasp the intended message. ### Step 2: Break Down Angel Number 747 To understand the overarching theme conveyed by 747, we must first dissect the significance of each digit within the sequence: #### Digit 7 Digit seven represents spiritual awakening, intuition, knowledge seeking, learning, study, education, psychic abilities, and mysticism. When appearing alone or paired with other numbers, it encourages individuals to explore deeper truths and embrace their inherent gifts. The energy associated with 7 also resonates with persistence, determination, inner strength, and inner-wisdom. #### Digit 4 The vibrational essence of digit four relates to stability, order, organization, system, service, patience, practicality, integrity, honesty, endurance, and progress. It inspires people to build strong foundations, establish routines, and work diligently towards their objectives. Furthermore, 4 symbolizes passion, drive, and ambition - qualities necessary for achieving success in both professional and personal spheres. Combining Digits 7 & 4 ------------------------ When combined, digits 7 and 4 create a potent blend of spiritual exploration, intellectual curiosity, and steadfast dedication to accomplishing tasks at hand. Moreover, this union emphasizes the importance of balancing mystical experiences with pragmatic actions. ### Step 3: Interpret the Message Behind Angel Number 747 Now that we've broken down the components of 74 Did you like going to the beach with Linda? Using the slope-intercept form of a line helps in finding the slope of a line from an equation and the intercept directly from the equation. This is great because it shows how to graph a line using y=mx+b and can be quite useful for applications of linear equations. Slope intercept form of a line is one of the first ways we deal with a line equation. And basically what it is is, y=mx+b, m is referring to the slope or the steepness of the graph and b is the y intercept. Now remember how we find intercepts is we plug in 0 for the other things. So to find the y intercept, we'll plug in 0 for x, we plug in 0 for x, this goes away leaving us with just y=b. So it's really not any mystery that this is the y intercept, it's just what happens when we would calculate it out. So appreciate for an equation, let me take a look at one. So y=x-2, okay? And I just want to get a, rough sketch to this graph. Get our coordinate grid. Our y intercept is -2. So basically that means we go down the y axis two units, so 1, 2. There's your starting point and from there we know that our slope is 3. Slope is rise overrun. So that tells us we're going to go up three, 1 up 1, up 2, up 3 then go over one, you get a next point. We could continue on going up 3 over 1, but we really only need two points to get a line. Connect our dots and our line is going to look something like that. It's not exact but it gets an idea of what's going on. So this is the general way of taking a line in slope intercept form, creating a graph. oh, do you know what this is? Find the Magnitude of a Vector: A Comprehensive Step-by-Step Guide Vector mathematics is an essential part of physics, engineering, computer graphics, and many other fields. One fundamental concept in vector math is calculating the magnitude (or length) of a vector. This guide will walk you through the process with clear explanations, helpful tips, and guidelines to ensure accuracy and understanding. Let's get started! **What is a Vector?** A vector is a mathematical object that has both magnitude (length) and direction. It can be represented as an arrow pointing from one point to another in space or as an array of numbers called components. For example, a two-dimensional vector might look like this: (3, 4), where 3 represents its horizontal component and 4 represents its vertical component. **Step 1: Identify the Components of Your Vector** The first step in finding the magnitude of a vector is identifying its components. These could be given directly, such as (2, 5), or they may need to be calculated based on some problem description. Regardless, make sure you have your vector written in the form (x, y) for two dimensions, (x, y, z) for three dimensions, and so forth. Key Tip: Pay close attention to signs (+/-) because they determine whether a component contributes positively or negatively to the overall magnitude. **Step 2: Use Pythagorean Theorem to Calculate Magnitude** Once you've identified the components, you can calculate the magnitude using the Pythagorean theorem if you're working in two or three dimensions. The formula looks like this: Magnitude = sqrt(x^2 + y^2) for two dimensions = sqrt(x^2 + y^2 + z^2) for three dimensions Here, sqrt denotes square root, x, y, and z are the individual components of the vector, and the superscript '2' indicates squaring. Guideline: Make sure to square each component before adding them together. Also, remember that the order of operations requires you to perform calculations inside parentheses first, then exponents, followed by multiplication and division from left to right, and finally addition and subtraction from left to right. **Step 3: Simplify the Equation** After applying the Pythagorean theorem, simplify the equation by performing any necessary arithmetic operations. Remember that when taking the square root of a number under a square, you should always consider whether the result could be either positive or negative. However, since we're dealing with magnitudes here, which are inherently positive, discard any negative results. Key Tip: If you find yourself struggling with complex expressions, break them down into smaller parts. Calculating (5^2) is easier than (3+2)^2, even though they represent the same value. **Step 4: Rounding (if Necessary)** Depending on the context, you might want to round your answer to a certain number of decimal places. Be careful with significant figures rules depending on what kind of numerical data you're working with. And there you have it! You now know how to calculate the magnitude of a vector. Practice regularly to reinforce your skills and expand them to higher dimensional spaces if needed. Happy learning! The first vaccination ever was for smallpox. In 1796 an English doctor, Edward Jenner, noticed something. He saw that people who got cowpox did not get sick from smallpox. He gave a young boy the cowpox virus to protect him from smallpox. People did this so they might get smallpox on "one place" on their bodies. Then they could "pick" which body part got scars from smallpox. Deuteranopia is the most common form of color blindness; between five and ten percent of males suffer from it. It is called Daltonism because John Dalton discovered it. Those affected have trouble telling the difference between red and green. As seen by protanopes (people suffering from protanopia), red is darkened. Most often, this is because they lack the receptors for long (protanopia) or medium (deuteranopia)-length light waves or because these receptors have changed their sensitivity. Title: How to Become a Famous Actor Introduction: Becoming a famous actor is a challenging yet rewarding goal that requires talent, dedication, hard work, and persistence. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the necessary steps to help you achieve your dream of becoming a successful actor or actress. Remember, fame isn't guaranteed; however, by following these steps, you can significantly increase your chances of making it big in the acting world. Step 1: Develop Your Acting Skills Developing strong acting skills should be your top priority as an aspiring actor. Here are some ways to improve your craft: * Take acting classes at local community centers, colleges, or private studios. Learn various techniques such as Meisner, Stanislavski, Method Acting, etc. * Practice regularly with monologues, scenes from plays, or even self-made videos. Consistent practice refines your abilities over time. * Work on building your emotional range by exploring different emotions and learning how to express them authentically. * Improve your vocal control and diction by practicing voice exercises and enunciation drills. * Study other actors' performances to learn from their strengths and weaknesses. Key Tip: The more versatile you become as an actor, the better equipped you'll be to handle diverse roles and impress casting directors. Step 2: Build a Strong Foundation Through Education While formal education isn't strictly required to become an actor, obtaining training from reputable institutions can provide valuable connections and knowledge needed in the industry. Consider pursuing any of the following options: * Attend a four-year university program specializing in theater arts or drama. * Enroll in a conservatory program focused solely on acting. * Participate in workshops led by experienced professionals within the field. Guideline: Always research schools thoroughly before applying to ensure they offer quality instruction and have good relationships with agents, managers, and casting directors. Step 3: Create a Professional Resume and Headshot Your resume showcases your experience, while your headshot gives casting directors a visual representation of who you are. Follow these guidelines when creating both: Resume: * Keep formatting simple and easy-to-read. * List all relevant acting experiences, including school productions, professional gigs, and independent projects. * Highlight notable achievements like awards or accolades. * Include contact information and union affiliation (if applicable). Headshots: * Hire a professional photographer specializing in headshots. * Choose a neutral background free from distractions. * Dress appropriately for the type of roles you wish to pursue. * Present yourself naturally - avoid heavy makeup or exaggerated expressions. Step 4: Gain Experience Through Auditions and Performances To build up your resume and gain exposure, audition for every opportunity available. This includes: * School productions * Community theatre * Local commercials * Independent films * Web series Performing frequently hones your skills and increases visibility among industry professionals. Key tip: Treat every performance as if it were your most important gig yet - word travels fast about talented performers! Step 5: Network Within the Industry They might not have looked just like they do now but there's some there that were around when the din... Title: How to Call the United States from Switzerland Introduction: Making international calls can sometimes be a daunting task due to different phone systems, time zones, and costs involved. This tutorial will provide you with an easy-to-follow guide on how to call the United States (US) from Switzerland using landlines or mobile phones. By following these steps, you'll ensure clear communication with your friends, family, or colleagues without encountering any difficulties. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Familiarize yourself with International Dialing Codes: Before making an international call, understand that every country has its unique dialing code. The US uses +1 as its country calling code while Switzerland uses +41. These codes are necessary when initiating calls between countries. 2. Check Time Zones: Keep track of both Swiss and American time zones since they differ across regions in America. It is essential to avoid disturbing someone during their sleeping hours. 3. Understand Charges: Be aware of potential charges associated with international calls. Some service providers may charge higher rates than others, so consider comparing prices before placing your call. Detailed Steps: Step 1: Identify Your Phone Type Firstly, determine whether you're using a landline or a mobile phone because procedures vary slightly depending on this factor. Step 2: Get Access Number (For Landlines Only): If you're using a landline, contact your local telecom company to obtain an access number that allows direct international dialling. Without this, you won't be able to make international calls directly. Mobile users can skip this step. Step 3: Dial Correctly Whether you're on a landline or mobile, here's how to format your call correctly: - Start by dialing '00', which is the exit code used in Switzerland to place international calls. - Next, input '+1', which is the country code for the USA. - Afterwards, enter the area code followed by the recipient's phone number. For example, if you want to reach a number in New York City (area code 212), you would dial '00' then '+1' then '212' followed by the seven-digit phone number. Step 4: Confirm Connection Listen carefully after dialling to confirm connection success. You should hear ringing tones indicating that your call is going through. If there's silence or error messages, double-check the numbers dialled and try again. Step 5: Cost Consideration Bear in mind that international calls can accumulate significant fees. To minimize cost: - Use VoIP services like Skype or WhatsApp where available. - Make sure you have inclusive minutes packages in your plan if applicable. - Contact your provider about special deals for international calls. By adhering to these simple yet crucial steps, you'll find connecting with people in the US from Switzerland straightforward and hassle-free. Remember always to respect time differences and budget accordingly for any potential charges. Happy calling! They took the dog and suddenly went away. II.A.1 Part Two-Compiling and Graphing the Data During this portion of the activity, you and your class will compile all the ant lion data and plot the results on a graph. Each student will need: - Ant Lion Class Data Worksheet - One piece of graph paper |Instructions for the teacher: - Copy the Class Data Worksheet on the chalkboard or overhead projector. - Collect data from your student teams column by column as they appear on the Class Data Worksheet. Record these on the board or overhead projector. (You may need a team spokesperson.) Not all teams will have information for each column. Students should fill in their Class Data Worksheets along with you as each team calls out its information. - Once all the data have been recorded on the Data Worksheets, guide students through the steps below to make a graph of the data. The graph you make will address the question: Is there a relationship between ant lion size and pit size? - Draw an outline of the graph. (The x-axis, or bottom, horizontal line, should be approximately 16 squares long. The y-axis, or vertical line, should be approximately 16 - Label the x-axis "ant lion size." - Label the y-axis "ant lion pit size." - Put a zero at the point where the y-axis and x-axis come together. - On the y-axis, make a mark every 5mm, starting at the zero. Label these marks .5cm, 1cm, 1.5cm, 2cm, 2.5cm, 3cm, 3.5cm, 4cm, 4.5cm and so on until the largest ant lion pit is - On the x-axis, make a mark every 2mm, starting with the zero. Label these 2mm, 4mm, 6mm, 8mm, and so on until the largest ant lion is accounted for. - Now plot the data from the Data Worksheet onto your graph. Start with information collected by team #1. Find team #1's ant lion pit size on the y-axis. Lightly draw a horizontal line to the right. - Next find team #1's ant lion size on the x-axis. Draw a vertical line up from that point until it intersects with your first line. Make an easy to see dot where the lines intersect. Continue doing this until the data from each team are included in your graph. - To interpret your graph, use the examples below. - If the dots on the graph form an almost straight line or are scattered in an almost straight line as seen in Example A, there is in fact a direct relationship between ant lion size and ant lion pit size. This means that pit size is fairly predictable. From looking at an ant lion, you would know approximately how large a pit it would make. Or, by looking at the pit alone, you could make a good guess about how large the ant lion was that made it. - If the dots are unorganized as in Example B, then no relationship exists between ant lion size and ant lion pit size. This would mean that a big ant lion could make a small pit or a small ant lion could make a big pit, etc. After completing this activity, students should: - Understand predator/prey relationships. - Be able to give examples of adaptations sand-dwelling animals have for digging. - Understand the concept of an ecological niche. - Be able to carefully observe and measure. - Be able to participate in discussion and learn cooperatively. Further Questions and Activities for Motivated Students - Collect as many different kinds of small insects as you can find to feed your captive ant lion. Keep a data sheet to record the time and date, how many and what kind of insects you fed your ant lion, and what was captured and eaten. Continue this for at least one week. Try to answer the following questions: - What is the average number of insects that the ant lion eats in one day? - What kind of insect does the ant lion prefer? - What is the average distance the ant lion can throw the discarded body of its prey? Be sure to properly care for your captive ant lion. Design an experiment to test whether ant lions prefer wet or dry sand. The northern boundary of the state with Massachusetts is marked by the distinctive Southwick Jog/Granby Notch, an approximately 2.5 mile (4.0 km) square detour into Connecticut slightly west of the center of the border. Somewhat surprisingly, the origin of this anomaly is uncertain, with stories ranging from surveyors who were drunk, attempting to avoid hostile Native Americans, or taking a shortcut up the Connecticut River; Massachusetts residents attempting to avoid Massachusetts' high taxes for the low taxes of Connecticut; Massachusetts' interest in the resources represented by the Congamond Lakes which lie on the border of the jog; and the need to compensate Massachusetts for an amount of land given to Connecticut due to inaccurate survey work. Perhaps the only suggested reason which can be safely ruled out is that the jog is necessary to prevent Massachusetts from sliding out into the Atlantic Ocean! In any event, the dispute over the border retarded the development of the region, since neither state would invest in even such basic amenities as schools for the area until the dispute had been settled. where's it going to go in the bank? Does that mean the same thing as "line segments?" A Vector is a line segment where the first endpoint is in the origin (0, 0). This means that a Vector only represent the direction and length of a line. So a and b represents the direction and length of the two line segments. what does "dot" mean? dot stands for dot product, also called scalar product. Is one of two ways you can multiply vectors. Is defined as "the product of two vectors to form a scalar, whose value is the product of the magnitudes of the vectors and the cosine of the angle between them" And what does "|a|" mean? Let's look at the formula: a dot b = |a||b|cos angle we wan't to solve this to get angle: cos(angle) = (a dot b) / (|a| |b|) Normalizing a vector means making the length of the vector equal 1. This makes |a| |b| become 1*1. So if a and b is normalized the formula becomes: cos(angle) = (a dot b)/(1 * 1) cos(angle) = (a dot b) finally to solve the equation: angle = Math.acos(a dot b). (a dot b) returns a single value not a vector. The Vectors are normalized by dividing x and y with the length of the Vector. "Subtract x,y,z?" Hah? Title: How to Change an iPad into Airplane Mode Airplane mode is a feature available on most electronic devices, including iPads, that disables all wireless communication functions. This includes cellular data, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth connections. Enabling airplane mode can help conserve battery life, reduce radiation exposure during flights, or avoid distractions while studying or working. Here's our comprehensive guide on changing your iPad into airplane mode. **Step 1:** Unlock Your iPad --------------------------- Before you begin, ensure your iPad is unlocked and you have access to its home screen. If the device is locked with a passcode or biometric authentication (Touch ID or Face ID), you won't be able to modify any settings until it's properly unlocked. **Step 2:** Locate the Settings App ---------------------------------- Find the Settings app on your iPad's home screen. It has a gray icon featuring gears inside. Tap on this icon to open the Settings application. **Step 3:** Navigate to the Airplane Mode Option ----------------------------------------------- Once within the Settings app, scroll down until you see the option labeled 'Airplane Mode.' You cannot miss it as it features an airplane icon. **Step 4:** Enable Airplane Mode ------------------------------ Tap the switch next to 'Airplane Mode' so that it turns green. When activated, this automatically disables all wireless radios on your iPad, effectively putting it into airplane mode. A small airplane icon will appear at the top right corner of your iPad's status bar indicating that airplane mode is enabled. ### Key Tips & Guidelines: * Remember, enabling airplane mode means no incoming or outgoing calls, messages, emails, or internet usage unless connected to Wi-Fi before turning on airplane mode. * While airplane mode prevents radio transmissions, certain background processes may still consume power. To maximize battery savings, consider manually closing unnecessary applications. **Step 5:** Verify Airplane Mode Status ------------------------------------- After enabling airplane mode, verify its activation by checking the top right corner of your iPad's display. An airplane icon should be visible if successful. Also, try opening any apps requiring network connectivity like Mail or Safari; they should fail to load content due to lack of connection. ### Key Tips & Guidelines: * Some apps might continue functioning even after activating airplane mode if they were opened prior to enabling this feature. Quit these apps and reopen them to confirm their functionality has indeed been disabled. **Step 6:** Disable Airplane Mode (Optional) ------------------------------------------ To disable airplane mode and restore normal operation, simply return to the Setting app and tap the same switch again. The color should change back to white when turned off. Wait for your device to reconnect to networks - this could take several seconds depending on your location and signal strength. Remember, being aware of when to enable or disable airplane mode allows you to manage your digital experience better, saving battery life, avoiding unwanted interruptions, and complying with flight regulations. Vice Admiral Sir Francis Drake (1540 - 27 January 1596) was an English sea captain, privateer, navigator, slaver, and politician of the Elizabethan era. He was born in Tavistock and grew up in Kent, where he learned to be a sailor. Soon he was a shipmaster (captain). Queen Elizabeth I awarded Drake a knighthood in 1581, which he received on the Golden Hind in Deptford. He was second-in-command of the English fleet against the Spanish Armada in 1588. He also carried out the second circumnavigation of the world, from 1577 to 1580 during which he attacked towns of the Spanish Empire. He had always been a powerful enemy to the Spanish. How To Draw a Frog In this drawing lesson we'll show you how to draw a realistic Frog in 7 easy steps. This Free step by step lesson progressively builds upon each previous step until you get to the final rendering of the Frog. This is a simple lesson designed for beginners and kids with real easy to follow steps. Feel free to print this page and use as a drawing tutorial. Here are some fun facts about the Frog you might find interesting. - Frogs are widely known as exceptional jumpers. - There are 5000 species of Frogs. - Frogs range in size from 10 mm to 300 mm. - Certain frogs change colour between night and day. - Camouflage is a common defensive mechanism in frogs. Step 1: Start by drawing the nose and head section. Step 2: Draw the eye brow section. Step 3: Sketch the back and lower jaw area. Step 4: Draw the front feet and hind leg. Step 5: Finish the front legs and rear leg. Step 6: Draw the second rear leg. Step 7: Draw the eyes and add the body stripe to complete the drawing of a Frog.
