We don't go higher than that in many problems, but the second derivative is an important-- the derivative of the derivative is an important thing to know, especially in problems with maximum and minimum, which is the big application of derivatives, to locate a maximum or a minimum, and to decide which one it is. And I can tell you right away, locating a maximum, minimum, is the first derivative's job. The first derivative is 0. If I have a maximum or a minimum, and we'll have pictures, somewhere in the middle of my function I'll recognize by derivative equals 0. Slope equals 0, that the function is leveling off, either bending down or bending up, maximum or minimum. OK, and it's the second derivative that tells me which it is. The second derivative tells me the bending of the graph. OK, so we now will have three generations. The big picture of calculus started with two functions: the distance and the speed. And we discussed in detail the connection between them. How to recover the speed if we know the distance, take the derivative. Now comes the derivative of the speed, which in that language, in the distance-speed-time language, the second derivative is the acceleration, the rate at which your speed is changing, the rate at which you're speeding up or slowing down. And this is the way I would write that. If the speed is the first derivative-- df dt-- this is the way you write the second derivative, and you say d second f dt squared. d second f dt squared. OK, so that's you could say the physics example: distance, speed, acceleration. And I say physics because, of course, acceleration is the a in Newton's Law f equals ma. For a graph, like these graphs here, I won't especially use those physics words. I'll use graph words. So I would say function one would be the height of the graph. And in this case, that height is y equals x squared, so it's a simple parabola. Here would be the slope. I would use the word "slope" for the second function. And the slope of y equals x squared we know is 2x, so we see the slope increasing. And you see on this picture the slope is increasing. As x increases, I'm going up more steeply. Now, it's the second derivative. And what shall I call that? Bending. Bending is the natural word for the second derivative on a graph. And what do I-- the derivative of 2x is 2, a constant, a positive constant, and that positive constant tells me that the slope is going upwards and that the curve is bending upwards. So in this simple case, we connect these three descriptions of our function. It's positive. It's slope is positive. And its second derivative-- bending-- is positive. And that gives us a function that goes like that. Now, let me go to a different function. Let me take a second example now, an example where not everything is positive. But let's make it familiar. We know the derivative of sine x, but before we write it down, look at the graph. The slope is positive, right? But the slope actually starts out at 1. Better make it look a little more realistic. That's a slope of 1 there. So the slope starts at 1 and the slope drops to a slope of 0 up there. So a slope of 1. I see here is a 1. Here I'm graphing y prime. dy dx I sometimes write as y prime, just because it's shorter, and particularly, it'll be shorter for a second derivative. So y prime, we know the derivative of sine x is cos x, which is pretty neat actually, that we start with a familiar function, and then we get its twin, its other half. And the cosine is the slope of the sine curve, and it starts at 1, a slope of 1, and it comes down to 0, as we know the cosine does. So that's a graph of the cosine. And now, of course, we have three generations. I'm going to graph y double prime. Let me put it up here. y double prime, the second derivative, the derivative of the cosine of x is minus sine x. OK, let's just-- from the picture, what am I seeing here? I'm seeing a slope of 0. I'm taking now the slope of the slope. So here it starts at 0. The slope is downwards, so the second derivative is going to be negative. Oh, and it is negative, minus sign x. So the slope starts at 0 and ends at minus 1 because that now comes down at a negative slope. The slope is negative. I'm going downhill, and that's a graph of the second derivative. And which way is our function bending? It's bending down. As I go along, the slope is dropping. And I see that in the slope curve. It's falling. And I see it in the bending curve because I'm below 0 here. This is bending down, where that one was bending up. I could introduce the word convex for something that bends upwards, and bending down, I could introduce the word concave. But those are just words. The graphs are telling us much more than the words do. OK, so do you see that picture bending down, but going up? So the slope is positive here, but the second derivative, the slope is dropping. So the second derivative-- and you have to pay attention to keep them straight. The second derivative is telling us that the original one is bending down. OK, let me continue these graphs just a little beyond 90 degrees, pi over 2, because you'll see something interesting. So what happens in the next part of the graph? The slope down to minus 1, the slope-- do you see here? The slope is negative, so on this slope graph, I'm below 0. And the slope is 0. Let me put a little mark at these points here, at these three points. Those are important points. In fact, that is a maximum, of course. The sine curve hits its maximum at 1. At that point when it hits its maximum, what's its slope? When you hit a maximum, you're not going up anymore. You haven't started down. The slope is 0 right there. What's the second derivative? What's the bending at a maximum? The bending tells you that the slope is going down, so the bending is negative. The bending is negative at a maximum. Good. OK, now I'm going to continue this sine curve for another 90 degrees, the cosine curve, and I'll continue the bending curve, so I have minus sine x, which will go back up. OK, now what? Now what? And then, of course, it would continue along. OK, there's something interesting happening at 180 degrees, at pi. Can I identify that point? So there's 180 degrees. Something's happening there. I don't see-- I don't quite know how to say what yet, but something's happening there. It's got to show up here, and it has to show up here. So whatever is happening is showing up by a point where y double prime, the second derivative, is 0. That's my new little observation, not as big a deal as maximum or minimum. This was a max here. And we identified it as a max because the second derivative was negative. Now I'm interested in this point. Can you see what's happening at this point as far as bending goes? This curve is bending down. But when I continue, the bending changes to up. This is a point where the bending changes. The second derivative changes sign, and we see it here. Up to this square point, the bending is below 0. The bending is downwards as I come to here. But then there's something rather special that-- you see, can I try to blow that point up? Here the bending is down, and there it turns to up, and right in there with the-- this is called-- so this is my final word to introduce-- inflection point. Don't ask me why. An inflection point is a point where the second derivative is 0. And what does that mean? That means at that moment, it stopped bending down, and it's going to start bending up. The second derivative is passing through 0. The sign of bending is changing. It's changing from concave here to convex there. That's a significant point on the graph. Not as big a thing as the max or the min that we had over there. So let me draw one more example and identify all these different points. OK, so here we go. I drew it ahead of time because it's got a few loops, and I wanted to get it in good form. OK, here it is. This is my function: x cubed minus x squared. Well, before I look at the picture, what would be the first calculus thing I do? I take the derivative. y prime is the derivative of x cubed, is three x squared minus the derivative of x squared, which is 2x. And now today, I take the derivative of that. I take the second derivative, y double prime. So the second derivative is the derivative of this. x squared is going to give me 2x, and I have a 3, so it's all together 6x. And minus 2x, the slope of that is minus 2, right? Cubic, quadratic, linear, and if I cared about y triple prime, which I don't, constant. And then the fourth derivatives and all the rest would be 0 for this case. OK, now somehow, those derivatives, those formulas for y, y prime, y double prime should tell me details about this graph. And the first thing I'm interested in and the most important thing is max and min. So let me set y prime to be-- which is 3x squared minus 2x. I'll set it to be 0 because I want to look for max, or min. And I look for both at the same time by setting y prime equals 0, and then I find out which I've got by looking at y double prime. So let me set y prime to be 0. What are the solutions? Where are the points on the curve where it's stationary? It's not climbing and it's not dropping? Well, I see them on the curve here. That is a point where the slope is 0. And I see one down here. There is a point where the slope is 0, but I can find them with algebra. I solve 3x squared equals to 2x, and I see it's a quadratic equation. I expect to find two roots. One of them is x equals 0, and the other one is what? If I cancel those x's to find a non-zero, canceling those x's leaves me with 3x equals 2 or x equals 2/3. Yeah, and that's what our graph shows. OK, now we can see on the graph which is a max and which is a min. And by the way, let me just notice, of course, this is the max. But let me just notice that it's what I would call a local maximum. It's not the absolute top of the function because the function later on is climbing off to infinity. This would be way a maximum in its neighborhood, so a maximum, and it's only a local max. And what do I expect to see at a maximum at x equals 0? I expect to see the slope 0 at x equals 0, which it is. Check. And at a maximum, I need to know the second derivative. OK, here's my formula. At x equals 0, I see y double prime if x is 0 is minus 2. Good. Negative second derivative tells me I'm bending down, as the graph confirms, and the place where the slope is 0 is a maximum and not a minimum. What about the other one? What about at x equals 2/3? At that point, y double prime, looking at my formula here for y double prime, is what? 6 times 2/3 is 4 minus 2 is plus 2. 4 minus 2 is 2. So this will be-- this is positive, so I'm expecting a min. At x equals 2/3, I'm expecting a min. And, of course, it is. And again, it's only a local minimum. The derivative can only tell you what's happening very, very close to that point. The derivative doesn't know that over here the function is going further down. So this is a min, and again, a local min. OK, those are maximum and minimum when we know the function. Oh yeah, I better do the inflection point. Do you remember what the inflection point is? The inflection point is when the bending changes from-- up to here I see that bending down. From here, I see it bending up. So I will not be surprised if that's the point where the bending is changing, and 1/3 is the inflection point. And now how do we find an inflection point? How do we identify this point? Well, y double prime was negative. y double prime was positive. At that point, y double prime is 0. This is an inflection point. And it is. At x equals to 1/3, I do have 6 times 1/3. 2 subtract 2, I have 0. So that is truly an inflection point. And now I know all the essential points about the curve. And these are the quantities-- oh! Say you're an economist. You're looking now at the statistics for the US economy or the world economy. OK, I suppose we're in a-- we had a local maximum there, a happy time a little while ago, but it went downhill, right? If y is, say, the gross product for the world or gross national product, it started down. The slope of that curve was negative. The bending was even negative. It was going down faster all the time. Now, at a certain moment, the economy kept going down, but you could see some sign of hope. And what was the sign of hope? It was the fact that it started bending up. And probably that's where we are as I'm making this video. I suspect we're still going down, but we're bending up. And at some point, hopefully tomorrow, we'll hit minimum and start really up. So I don't know. I would guess we're somewhere in there, and I don't know where. If I knew where, mathematics would be even more useful than it is, which would be hard to do. OK, so that's an example of how the second derivative comes in. Now, I started by giving this lecture the title Max and Min and saying those are the biggest applications of the derivative. Title: How to Butter a Brick Buttering a brick might seem like an unusual or even unnecessary task, but there are situations where this technique can be useful. For example, when you're trying to remove sticky substances from a brick surface, applying butter can help loosen the adhesive. This guide will provide clear and detailed steps on how to effectively butter a brick. **Things You'll Need:** 1. A brick 2. Unsalted butter, at room temperature 3. Paper towels or cloth 4. Gloves (optional) 5. Utensil for spreading butter (e.g., knife, spatula) **Step 1: Choose Your Brick** Select the brick that needs buttering. Make sure it is free of any debris or loose particles before proceeding. **Step 2: Soften the Butter** Remove the unsalted butter from the refrigerator and allow it to reach room temperature. Softened butter is easier to spread than cold butter. Using unsalted butter ensures that no additional residue or taste is added to the brick. **Step 3: Put On Gloves (Optional)** While gloves aren't necessary, they may make the process less messy. If you choose to wear gloves, put them on now. **Step 4: Apply Butter to the Brick** Using your fingers or a utensil such as a knife or spatula, scoop out some softened butter and begin applying it evenly onto the brick's surface. Be generous with the amount of butter used; covering the entire surface area will ensure better results. Key Tips for Applying Butter: - Use enough pressure to press the butter into the brick's pores but avoid causing damage to the brick. - Spread the butter smoothly without leaving clumps or gaps. - Pay extra attention to areas with stubborn stains or grime. **Step 5: Let the Butter Sit** Allow the butter to sit on the brick for several hours or overnight if possible. Giving the butter time to penetrate deeply into the brick's pores will maximize its effectiveness in lifting off unwanted materials. **Step 6: Remove Excess Butter** After waiting, gently wipe away excess butter using paper towels or a clean cloth. The remaining butter should have seeped into the brick's pores. **Step 7: Clean the Brick** Now it's time to scrub the brick thoroughly. Depending on what substance you are removing, different cleaning methods may be required. Generally speaking, warm soapy water and a stiff brush work well for most situations. Scrub the brick vigorously yet carefully, focusing on areas previously covered in gunk or dirt. Rinse off any soap residue and let the brick dry completely. **Step 8: Assess the Results** Inspect the brick once it has dried. Ideally, the majority of the unwanted material should have been removed during the cleaning process. However, if some remains, repeat the buttering and cleaning procedure until satisfactory results are achieved. By following these eight steps, you will successfully butter a brick and increase its chances of returning to its original condition. Remember, patience and thoroughness are crucial throughout this process. We can XXX yeah we can show you XXX. What is C three P O doing? And this is his main meal of the day. Now I wonder how can you make dose steps. Hello young learners! Today, we are going to talk about controlling things and how we can organize information to make sure we have control. Have you ever played a video game where you had to move a character around on the screen? Think about how you can make the character go left, right, up, or down - all those movements are ways of controlling the character. Now, let's imagine that we want to build our own remote-controlled car. To do this, we need to know how to make the car go forward, backward, turn left, and turn right. We can represent these four possible moves with arrows: , , , . In our case, we will use a special kind of arrow called a vector, which looks like this: v = [a, b], where a and b tell us how fast we should go in the horizontal and vertical directions, respectively. So, when we press a button on our controller, it sends a signal to the car telling it which vector (which set of speeds) to follow. The car then adjusts its wheels to move in the desired direction based on that vector. But here comes a challenge: sometimes, we want our car to be able to reach any point on a playground, no matter where it starts. This means that our car needs to be controllable - capable of moving to any position and orientation we desire through appropriate choice of signals. To ensure controllability, we need to create a special list of vectors that allows us to reach any point in the space surrounding our car. Imagine having different size Lego blocks; by combining them in various ways, we can build anything we want. Similarly, by using different combinations of vectors from our list, we can generate motion in any direction. In mathematics, this concept is similar to organizing objects in rows and columns to form matrices (you may think of matrices as tables). These matrices can help us determine whether our collection of vectors is sufficient to achieve controllability. Let's say we have a matrix M made up of our vectors: M = [v1 | Av1 | A v1 | ... | A^(n-1)v1], where v1 represents our initial vector, A is a matrix containing information about how each new vector is generated based on the previous one, and n is the number of dimensions in our space (for example, n = 2 for a flat surface or n = 3 for a room). The rank of M tells us if our collection of vectors is enough to reach every point in the space. If the rank of M is equal to n, then our collection of vectors guarantees controllability! However, if the rank is less than n, then there are some points in the space that we cannot reach, meaning our car isn't fully controllable. By understanding these concepts, we can build better remote-controlled cars and other exciting systems! Keep exploring and learning, and who knows what incredible inventions you'll come up with! [D puts highchair away; S wanders off mumbling; tape off/on at 9.56] To work with data on a worksheet, you first have to enter that data in the cells on the worksheet. Then, you might want to adjust the data so that it is visible, and display it just the way that you want. Watch the video Enter the data - Click a cell, and then type data in that cell. - Press ENTER or TAB to move to the next cell. Tip To enter data on a new line in a cell, enter a line break by pressing ALT+ENTER. - To enter a series of data, such as days, months, or progressive numbers, type the starting value in a cell, and then in the next cell type a value to establish a pattern. For example, if you want the series 1, 2, 3, 4, 5..., type 1 and 2 in the first two cells. Select the cells that contain the starting values, and then drag the fill handle (fill handle: The small black square in the lower-right corner of the selection. When you point to the fill handle, the pointer changes to a black cross.) across the range that you want to fill. Tip To fill in increasing order, drag down or to the right. To fill in decreasing order, drag up or to the left. - To wrap text in a cell, select the cells that you want to format, and then on the Home tab, in the Alignment group, click Wrap Text. - To adjust column width and row height to automatically fit the contents of a cell, select the columns or rows that you want to change, and then on the Home tab, in the Cells group, click Format. Under Cell Size, click AutoFit Column Width or AutoFit Row Height. Tip To quickly autofit all columns or rows in the worksheet, click the Select All button, and then double-click any boundary between two column or row headings. Format the data - To apply number formatting, click the cell that contains the numbers that you want to format, and then on the Home tab, in the Number group, click the arrow next to General, and then click the format that you want. - To change the font, select the cells that contain the data that you want to format, and then on the Home tab, in the Font group, click the format that you want. Why don't you mm make a dinosaur? Drawing fancy letters can be a fun and creative way to add some flair to your handwriting or artwork. With a little practice, anyone can learn to draw elegant, decorative letters that will stand out on greeting cards, certificates, posters, and more. This tutorial will guide you through the process of creating beautiful, swirling script using just paper and pencil. **Things You'll Need:** * Paper * Pencil * Eraser (optional) ## Step 1: Understand Basic Letter Construction Before diving into drawing fancy letters, it is important to have a solid understanding of basic letter construction. Each letter consists of three main parts: the upstroke, downstroke, and baseline. The upstroke is the part of the letter that goes from bottom to top, such as the vertical line on an uppercase 'L'. The downstroke is the opposite-it travels from top to bottom, like the middle bar of an uppercase 'H'. Finally, the baseline is the invisible line upon which all lowercase letters rest; think of it as the ground level for your writing. Keep these components in mind as you begin practicing your fancy letters. ![Basic Letter Anatomy](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/ad/Write-Fancy-Lettering-Step-1-Version-2.jpg/aid453067-v4- I'm going to read that then give it back to you. If you are sorting content into an order, one of the most simple techniques that exists is the bubble sort technique. In essence you start at one end of the list, move one by one to the other end of the list, and if you ever reach a situation where two items are out of order, you swap them. This is one of the most simple sort techniques that exists, that is taught in any basic programming course. Let's say you have an array of Grades(5). You want to sort them so that the highest grade is at the beginning of the list, and that the lowest grade is at the end of the list. Note that this is NOT REAL CODE. This is an example of the concept, that you can apply to any language. So you would fill Grades(5) with the values. Then you would say - for ctr = 1 to 4 .for ctr2 = ctr + 1 to 5 ..if Grades(ctr) < Grades(ctr2) then ...Temp = Grades(ctr) ...Grades(ctr) = Grades(ctr2) ...Grades(ctr2) = Temp So in essence you have the outer loop stepping through each item but the very last one. The inner loop steps through every untried item from whereever you are in the outer loop, going forward. The two are compared and if the higher number is not "on top", the are swapped. Let's say your array is 90 70 80 100 60 On the first time through the loop, you begin with 90 (value 1) and compare it with the others, in order. Is 90 < 70? No. Nothing happens. Is 90 < 80? No. Is 90 < 100? Yes. The 100 takes spot 1, and the 90 takes spot 4. Is 100 < 60? No. Now we have guarantee that spot #1 is definitely the largest number in the entire array. Now we work on the second largest number. We move on to stop 2. Is 70 < 80? YES, they swap spots. Is 80 < 90? YES, they swap spots. Is 90 < 60? No, so the 90 stays in spot 2. And so it goes, until the entire array is settled in proper order. You can of course arrange the array in ascending or descending order just by switching the < to a > ! Title: How to Canter Without Hesitation Cantering without hesitation is an essential skill for any rider, as it promotes balance, rhythm, and confidence in both horse and rider. This guide will walk you through the steps necessary to canter smoothly and confidently with your horse. **Step 1: Establish Basic Control at Walk and Trot** Before attempting to canter, ensure that you have established basic control over your horse's speed and direction while walking and trotting. You should be able to maintain a steady pace, stop when asked, and change directions without issue. Mastering these foundational skills sets the stage for successful canter transitions. *Key Tip:* Regular practice sessions help strengthen this bond between you and your horse. Spend time working on improving communication and understanding each other better. **Step 2: Prepare Your Horse for Transition** Begin preparing your horse mentally by riding them along a large circle or serpentine pattern. This encourages relaxation and focus. As you approach the part of the circle where you plan to ask for the canter, make sure your seat is deep and secure, your legs are relaxed but engaged, and your hands are soft yet firm. *Guideline:* Maintain a consistent contact with the reins; too much slack may cause confusion, whereas pulling excessively could create tension or resistance. **Step 3: Use Leg Aids Correctly** To signal the transition into canter, squeeze your inside leg gently against the girth while applying pressure with your outside leg slightly behind the girth. The purpose of using two different leg pressures is to indicate which lead (left or right) you want your horse to take. *Key Tip:* Timing is crucial here. Apply your leg aid just before the point where your horse naturally wants to break into canter due to the curvature of the circle. **Step 4: Utilize Seat and Weight Aids** As you apply your leg aids, shift your weight slightly onto your inside seat bone and allow your upper body to tilt forward slightly. This subtle movement signals to your horse that it's time to pick up the canter. *Guideline:* Be careful not to tip too far forward or lean back, as either position can disrupt your balance and confuse your horse. **Step 5: Anticipate and Smooth Out the Stride** Once your horse begins cantering, sit deeply and evenly in the saddle, keeping your lower leg still and following the motion with your upper body. Stay loose in your hips and knees to absorb the increased momentum. *Key Tip:* Focus on maintaining a constant rhythm rather than trying to correct every small mistake immediately. Consistency fosters confidence and improves overall performance. **Step 6: Practice Regularly** Like any new skill, practicing regularly will greatly improve your ability to canter without hesitation. Gradually increase the difficulty of exercises once you feel comfortable, such as asking for canters during straight lines or transitions from halt directly into canter. *Guideline:* Remember to always end your training session on a positive note. Even if things didn't go perfectly, acknowledging progress made goes a long way towards building trust and motivation. Remember, patience and persistence are vital components of horsemanship. With dedication and proper technique, you'll soon find yourself cantering effortlessly with your horse. You're not going to let Mommy live with you? All penguins have a white belly and a dark, mostly black, back. This is a type of camouflage to keep them safe when they swim, because it makes them blend in with their background. The white and black colors make an effect called countershading. When a predator looking from below sees the white belly and wings of a swimming penguin, they can not see the penguin well because the light is coming from above. However, when seen from above, the penguin's black back blends in with the dark water below, so they are hard to see. I thought they were grapes at first. And her mother was going to buy a salad. Title: How to Perform Drop Spins with a Rifle for Color or Winter Guard Performing drop spins with a rifle is an impressive skill that requires practice, precision, and control. This tutorial will guide you through the process of executing this technique flawlessly, whether you're part of a color guard or winter guard team. Before starting, ensure your rifle is in good condition and suitable for spinning. Step 1: Adopt the Proper Stance Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and body weight evenly distributed. Keep your back straight, chest up, and chin parallel to the ground. Your non-dominant hand should rest gently on your hip while holding the rifle securely in your dominant hand. Maintaining proper posture throughout the spin ensures balance and stability. Key Tip: Relax your shoulders and arms; tension can hinder fluid movements. Step 2: Grip the Rifle Correctly Place your thumb along the top edge of the rifle butt (the end farthest from the barrel), just below where the stock meets the trigger mechanism. Curl your fingers around the shaft, ensuring a firm but comfortable grip. The rifle's center of gravity should be close to your hand for better control during rotation. Guideline: Avoid gripping too tightly as it may cause fatigue and restrict movement. Step 3: Initiate the Spin Raise the rifle vertically above your head using your dominant arm. Swiftly move your wrist downward, releasing the rifle at waist level. As the rifle descends, allow its momentum to carry it behind your back before catching it again. Timing is crucial here - release the rifle when it reaches eye level for optimal spin speed and distance traveled. Key Tip: Practice smooth transitions between raising, releasing, and catching the rifle to master the technique. Step 4: Control the Rotation Once released, maintain visual contact with the rifle throughout the spin. Anticipate when it will complete one full circle by observing its trajectory and adjusting accordingly. Use subtle finger movements to steer the rifle if necessary, but avoid excessive force which could disrupt the spin. Guideline: Patience and anticipation are vital here. Allow the rifle to rotate naturally without rushing or forcing the motion. Step 5: Catch the Rifle As the rifle completes a rotation and approaches your waiting hand, prepare to catch it securely. Extend your arm fully, keeping your elbow soft yet stable. Close your fingers firmly around the rifle's handle as soon as it makes contact with your palm. Key Tip: Always watch the rifle until it's safely caught. Never look away prematurely! Practice regularly to enhance coordination, timing, and overall proficiency. Remember, consistency is key when learning any new skill. Over time, performing drop spins with a rifle will become second nature, enabling you to add flair and excitement to your color or winter guard performances. Title: Carving a Watermelon Swan: A Step-by-Step Guide Introduction: Creating a watermelon swan is an impressive way to elevate any gathering or celebration. This guide will walk you through the process with clear instructions and helpful tips along the way. With patience and practice, your guests will be amazed by your carving skills! Materials Needed: * Fresh watermelon (preferably oblong shaped) * Melon baller or ice cream scoop * Sharp knife (a small paring knife works best) * Cutting board * Large spoon * Kitchen towel * Dry erase marker or toothpicks Steps to Carve a Watermelon Swan: 1. Preparation: Clean and dry your workspace thoroughly before beginning. Place the watermelon on the cutting board and ensure that it's stable while carving. Use a kitchen towel underneath the melon if necessary. 2. Draw Guidelines: Using a dry erase marker, lightly draw the outline of your swan onto the surface of the watermelon. You can find templates online to help you create a symmetrical design. Make sure to include wings, tail feathers, neck, head, and beak in your drawing. 3. Create Holes for Eyes: Carefully insert the tip of the sharp knife into where you marked the eyes and make two shallow cuts forming small ovals. Then, gently push one end of a toothpick into each eye hole until they are securely held in place. These will serve as supports for the eyeballs later. 4. Scoop Out Flesh: Using the melon baller or ice cream scoop, remove the watermelon flesh from both sides of the body, creating enough space for carving details. Be careful not to dig too deep into the rind; aim for about inch thick. Set aside the carved-out pieces for snacking or garnishing. 5. Begin Carving Body Details: Start by shaping the wing area using gentle sawing motions with the knife. Gradually curve the edge upwards to form the appearance of feathers. Repeat this process on the opposite side. Next, move on to carving out the tail feathers, making sure they resemble the shape drawn earlier. 6. Form Neck and Head: Following the guideline, slowly carve away excess rind around what will become the neck and head areas. Curve the neck slightly downward towards the body, then taper it further to meet the location of the beak. For the beak, carefully carve a triangular shape at the end of the neck. 7. Add Eyeballs: Soak two blueberries (or other small round fruits) in water for about five minutes to give them a wet look. Gently press one soaked blueberry onto each toothpick support, creating the illusion of realistic eyeballs. It's a little packet of chicken noodle soup. [pointed to the other one outside his crib] Title: How to Eject a Disc from Your Xbox One The Xbox One is a popular gaming console developed by Microsoft. It features a disc drive that allows you to play games, movies, and music from physical discs. However, there may be instances when you need to eject a disc from your Xbox One. This could be due to various reasons such as switching to a different game or simply finishing watching a movie. Regardless of the reason, this tutorial will guide you through the process of safely ejecting a disc from your Xbox One. **Step 1:** Turn On Your Xbox One Before you can eject a disc, ensure that your Xbox One is turned on. Press the Xbox button on your controller or directly on the console to turn it on. A white light indicates that the system is powered up and ready for use. Turning on your Xbox One initiates the operating system, making all functionalities available, including the ability to eject discs. **Key Tip**: If your controller isn't responding, try changing batteries or using a different controller. Also, check if the console is properly connected to power and the TV. **Step 2:** Locate the Disc Eject Button On the front panel of the Xbox One, just below the disc tray, you'll find the eject button. It's a small, circular indentation with a single raised dot in the middle. Locating the eject button saves time and eliminates confusion about where to press to remove the disc. **Key Guideline**: Don't confuse the eject button with other similar-looking buttons like the disc slot itself or the power button which are located nearby. **Step 3:** Gently Push the Eject Button Using your thumb or index finger, gently push the eject button. You don't need much force; just enough to activate the mechanism. Applying gentle pressure prevents damage to the eject button and ensures smooth operation of the disc tray. **Step 4:** Wait for the Tray to Open After pressing the eject button, wait for the disc tray to slide open automatically. This usually takes less than five seconds. Waiting for the tray to fully open reduces the risk of damaging either the disc or the drive. **Key Tip**: If the tray doesn't open, make sure nothing is obstructing its path. Check also if the console isn't frozen or overheated. Restarting the console might help resolve this issue. **Step 5:** Remove the Disc Carefully Once the tray is fully opened, lift the edge of the disc slightly and pull it straight out. Avoid tilting or twisting the disc while removing it. Handling the disc carefully minimizes scratches and potential damage, ensuring it remains usable. **Step 6:** Close the Disc Tray After successfully ejecting and removing the disc, close the tray by lightly pushing it back into place until it clicks shut. Closing the tray preserves the integrity of the drive and prepares the console for the next disc insertion. By following these steps meticulously, you can confidently eject a disc from your Xbox One without causing any harm to the device or the disc itself. Happy gaming! Do you know what else is green? Working With Text This section will cover the basics of inserting, moving, and editing text. Typing and Inserting Text To enter text, simply start typing! Text will appear on the screen where the blinking cursor is located. To move the cursor, use the arrow buttons on your keyboard or select a location with the mouse. Below are some useful buttons and key combinations that will help you effectively manage the text of your document. |Movement Action||Key Combination| |Move cursor to the beginning of the line|| |Move cursor to the end of the line|| |Move cursor to the top of the document|| <Ctrl> + <Home> |Move cursor to the end of the document|| <Ctrl> + <End> In order to change the attributes of your text, the text must first be selected. To select text, simply click and drag the mouse over the desired text. The following table contains techniques for selecting specific portions of your text: |Whole word||Double-click within the word| |Whole paragraph||Triple-click within the paragraph| |Several words or lines||Click and drag the mouse over the words| |Entire document||Choose Edit > Select All from the menu bar, or press <Ctrl> + <A>| To deselect text, do one of the following: - Click anywhere outside of the selection on the page. - Press an arrow key on your keyboard. Deleting TextTo delete a small portion of text, use one of the following keyboard options: - Backspace button - deletes text to the left of the cursor. - Delete button - deletes text to the right of the cursor. To delete a large portion of text, follow these steps: - Select the portion of text you want to delete. - Press the Delete key on your keyboard. To move text from one area of a document to another, you must first cut or copy the text, and then paste the text to its new location. When you cut text, it is removed from the document and placed on a clipboard. When you copy text, it is copied from the document and placed on a clipboard. To cut text, do one of the following: - Select the text you want to cut. Click the Cut button in the Clipboard group on the Home tab. - Select the text you want to cut, and then press <Ctrl> + X on your keyboard. To copy text to the clipboard, do one of the following: - Select the text you want to copy. Click the Copy button in the Clipboard group on the Home tab. - Select the text you want to copy, and then press <Ctrl> + C on your keyboard. To move a small amount of text a short distance, the drag-and-drop method may be quicker. - Select the text you want to move. - Click the selection with the mouse, and drag the selection to its new location. To paste cut or copied text, move the cursor to the location where you want the text to be placed, and then do one of the following: - Click the Paste button in the Clipboard group on the Home tab. - Press <Ctrl> + V on your keyboard. The last 24 items that you cut or copied are placed onto Word's clipboard. To add a clipboard item back into your document, follow these steps: - Click the Home tab. - Click the arrow on the bottom-right of the Clipboard group. The items that have been placed on the clipboard will open in the Clipboard pane on the left of the screen. - Click the cursor where you would like the item to appear in your document. - Click the item in the Clipboard pane. To add all the items to the document at once, click the Paste All button on the Clipboard pane. To clear the contents of the Clipboard, click the Clear All button on the Clipboard pane. The Home tab on the left of the ribbon contains the most common tools you will need to edit text. For example, it has tools for editing text font, type, size, color, and style. Format Paint Button A useful feature for formatting text is the Format Painter button, located in the Clipboard group on the Home tab. For example, if you have already formatted your text with a certain font size and style, and you want to format other portions of text the same way, you can use the Format Painter tool instead of manually adding the text attributes each time. - Click the cursor within the text that contains the formatting you want to copy. - Click the Home tab. - Click the Format Painter button from the Clipboard group. A paintbrush will appear beside the cursor. - Select the text you want to format, and release the mouse button. To add the formatting to multiple selections of text, double-click the Format Painter button instead of clicking once. The Format Painter will stay active until you press the <Esc> key to turn it off. Undo an Action If you make a mistake while formatting text, or if you are unhappy with your formatting choices, you can undo your last action by doing one of the following: - Click the Undo icon in the top-left corner of the screen. - Press <CTRL> + <Z>. The lungs are located in the chest on either side of the heart. They are surrounded and protected by the ribcage. The left lung is a little smaller than the right lung because it shares space in the left side of the chest with the heart. Each lung is divided into sections (lobes). The right lung has three sections or lobes, including the: - Right upper lobe, which takes up the top third of the right lung - Right middle lobe, which is the smallest of the three lobes and shaped like a triangle - Right lower lobe, which is the largest of the three The left lung is shaped slightly differently than the right. It has only two lobes - the left upper lobe and the left lower lobe. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Mawhinney/020817c.cha = = = In total, the "DS" family has sold 154.01 million units as of July 14, 2015, making it the best-selling handheld of all time, the best-selling Nintendo console, and overall, the second best-selling system behind the PlayStation 2. Find an Episcopal Church: A Comprehensive Guide =============================================== The Episcopal Church is a mainline Christian denomination that traces its roots back to the Church of England. With over 2 million members across the United States, Canada, and several other countries, finding an Episcopal church can be both exciting and fulfilling. This comprehensive guide will provide you with in-depth information on locating an Episcopal church near you, understanding their beliefs and practices, and getting involved in congregational life. Step 1: Determine Your Location ------------------------------- To begin your search for an Episcopal church, first determine your location by using online mapping tools or GPS devices. Knowing your precise address or zip code enables more accurate results when searching for nearby churches. Key Tips & Guidelines * Utilize popular map services such as Google Maps, MapQuest, or Bing Maps to find your current location quickly. * If privacy is a concern, manually enter addresses instead of relying on automatic geolocation features. Step 2: Visit The Episcopal Church Website ------------------------------------------ Head to <https://www.episcopalchurch.org/>, the official website of the Episcopal Church. Here, you'll discover valuable resources about the denomination, including its history, teachings, worship styles, and social justice initiatives. Familiarizing yourself with these aspects can help ensure compatibility between your personal values and those upheld by local parishes. Key Tips & Guidelines * Explore various sections of the site, paying particular attention to the 'About Us,' 'Beliefs & Practices,' and 'Worship' pages. * Consider jotting down notes regarding specific points of interest or questions that arise during this research phase. Step 3: Locate Nearby Churches Using Online Directories ------------------------------------------------------- Use one of the following reputable directories to locate Episcopal churches within your area: * The Episcopal Church Official Website (<https://www.episcopalchurch.org/parishfinder>) - Provides a user-friendly parish finder tool where users can input their city, state, or zip code to generate a list of nearby Episcopal churches along with contact information and websites. * Episcopal Asset Map (<http://assetmap.episcopalchurch.org/>) - An interactive platform displaying locations, photos, descriptions, and service times of Episcopal institutions like schools, hospitals, camps, and social outreach centers alongside churches. * You're going out to the farm to get some eggs? Letter X Crafts and Activities X marks the spot * craft sticks Have the children make Xs with two craft sticks and some glue. Then have them mark certain spots with the Xs throughout the house and have someone else guess where all the Xs are. You can also tie in activities from other letters - make a map of your house and have the child lead clue to the Xs. * Black construction paper * white crayons or chalk Have the children trace their hands or feet onto the black construction paper with the white crayon or chalk. Then have them fill in the "bones" on their hands and feet to make it look like an xray. This i a great way to learn the bones of the body! * crayons or makers Draw Xs in various sizes on your paper. Show the children how to make an X into a butterfly and then color the butterflies all over the paper. This is a great rainy day activity! Betting on the 2014 World Cup can be an exciting way to add extra excitement to one of the world's most popular sporting events. However, it is important to approach betting with caution and knowledge, as it involves real money and risks. This detailed tutorial will guide you through the process of betting on the 2014 World Cup, from understanding the basics to making informed decisions and avoiding common pitfalls. **Step 1: Choose a Reputable Betting Site** The first step in betting on the 2014 World Cup is to choose a reputable and trustworthy online betting site. There are many options available, but not all of them are created equal. Here are some things to look for when choosing a betting site: - Licensing and regulation: Make sure the site is licensed and regulated by a recognized authority, such as the UK Gambling Commission or the Malta Gaming Authority. - Security and privacy: Look for sites that use encryption technology to protect your personal and financial information, and have clear policies about data protection and confidentiality. - Customer support: Check if the site offers reliable and responsive customer service, preferably via live chat, email, and phone. - Odds and markets: Compare the odds and markets offered by different sites, and make sure they cover all the games and markets you are interested in. - Bonuses and promotions: Consider the welcome bonuses, free bets, and other incentives offered by the site, but also read the terms and conditions carefully before accepting any offer. Key tip: Stick to well-known and established brands, and avoid obscure or unregulated sites. Research user reviews and ratings, and seek recommendations from trusted sources. In Russian, it applies to nouns, pronouns, adjectives, numerals and participles with a set of word endings (sounds/letters attached to the ends of words) that show the grammatical roles of words in a sentence. Because the grammatical roles are shown by the endings, word order is freer in than in English. When William the Conqueror took over England in 1066 AD, he brought his nobles, who spoke Norman, a language closely related to French. English changed a lot because it was mostly being spoken instead of written for about 300 years, because all official documents were written in Norman French. English borrowed many words from Norman at that time, and also began to drop the old word endings. English of this time is called Middle English. Title: How to Deal with a Friend Who Always Wants to Fight Introduction: Having a friend who always wants to fight can be draining and challenging. It's important to approach this situation with care and patience, as you value your friendship but also want to maintain peace and respect. This wikiHow will guide you through dealing with such a friend while preserving your relationship and mental well-being. Step 1: Identify the Cause Analyze why your friend enjoys fighting or arguing. Is it due to their personality? Are they going through personal issues? Understanding the cause is crucial when deciding how best to address the problem. By knowing what drives them, you can tailor your responses accordingly and find more effective solutions. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Be empathetic - try to put yourself in their shoes. * Don't jump to conclusions; gather enough information before making assumptions. Step 2: Stay Calm During Arguments When faced with an argumentative friend, keep your cool and don't let emotions take over. Speak slowly and clearly, maintaining a steady tone of voice. Avoid raising your voice or getting defensive, which may escalate the conflict further. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Practice deep breathing exercises to stay calm during heated discussions. * Use "I" statements instead of blaming language (e.g., say "I feel upset," rather than "You make me angry.") Step 3: Set Boundaries Make it clear that certain behaviors are unacceptable by setting firm boundaries. Let your friend know what topics or actions trigger arguments between you two. Politely yet assertively communicate these limits without sounding aggressive. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Choose specific examples to illustrate your points. * Reiterate that boundary-setting aims to improve communication and preserve the friendship. Step 4: Encourage Open Dialogue Promote open dialogue where both parties express themselves honestly and listen actively. Show genuine interest in understanding their perspective, even if it differs from yours. When people feel heard, they tend to argue less frequently. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Paraphrase what your friend says to ensure comprehension. * Ask questions to clarify any misunderstandings. Step 5: Seek Professional Help (Optional) If conflicts persist despite consistent efforts to resolve them amicably, consider seeking help from a mediator or therapist trained in dispute resolution. A neutral third party can facilitate constructive conversations and provide guidance on managing disagreements. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Research local therapists specializing in interpersonal relationships. * Remember that asking for professional assistance doesn't mean failure; it shows maturity and commitment to resolving the issue. Step 6: Evaluate Your Relationship Consider whether continuing the friendship benefits both of you. If the constant fighting outweighs its positives, reevaluating the relationship might be necessary. Keep in mind that ending a toxic friendship could lead to healthier connections in the future. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Reflect on past experiences and assess patterns within the friendship. * Consult trusted friends or family members about their observations regarding your relationship dynamics. Now well you get to pick a toy to take home 'fore I forget. Title: How to Get Gems in Animal Jam Animal Jam is a popular online game that allows players to create and customize their own animal characters, explore a virtual world, play games, and make friends. One of the main currencies used in the game is called gems, which can be used to buy various items such as clothing, furniture, and rare accessories for your animal character. Here's an in-depth guide on how you can get gems in Animal Jam. **Step 1: Complete Daily Challenges** One of the easiest ways to earn free gems in Animal Jama is by completing daily challenges. These tasks are simple activities that change every day, like feeding other animals or collecting specific items from around the game map. Completing these challenges will reward you with a small number of gems, but they add up over time. Plus, finishing all five challenges in one day gives you a bonus prize! This method encourages consistent playing while providing incremental gem rewards. It may take some patience, but this approach ensures steady progress without spending real money. **Step 2: Participate in Diamond Mines** Diamond Mines are mini-games within Animal Jam where you dig for treasure chests containing different types of jewels, including sapphires, rubies, emeralds, and diamonds. While digging, there's also a chance of finding gems. The deeper you go into the mine, the higher value treasures you find. However, keep in mind that going too deep might cause your pickaxe to break, so manage your tools wisely. Participating in diamond mines frequently increases your chances of obtaining more gems. **Step 3: Trade With Other Players** Trading is another way to acquire gems in Animal Jam. You can trade unwanted items (like duplicate rares) for gems with other players. To ensure fair trades, always check the current market price before agreeing to any deal. Remember, never give away personal information during trading and report suspicious behavior immediately. Trading offers opportunities for acquiring gems through negotiation and smart bartering strategies. **Step 4: Purchase Gems With Real Money** If you want to obtain gems quickly, consider purchasing them using real money. Visit the Parent Dashboard, select 'Add Gems,' then choose from several payment options available. Prices vary depending on the quantity purchased; larger quantities offer better value per gem. Although buying gems requires financial investment, it guarantees immediate access to resources needed for premium content. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always prioritize essential purchases over impulsive ones when buying gems. Plan out what you need most urgently - whether it's a new den, special outfit, or limited edition item - and stick to that plan. * Be cautious about scams or fraudulent activities related to selling or buying gems outside the official platform. Stick to secure methods provided within the game itself. * Keep track of ongoing events or promotions offering free gems as participation prizes or completion rewards. Stay updated by following Animal Jam social media channels or newsletters. * Consider joining membership programs if regular gem acquisition becomes important. Membership often includes additional benefits such as monthly allowances of free gems, early access to exclusive sales, and discounted pricing on certain products. * Lastly, remember to enjoy the process of earning gems instead of rushing it. Engaging in various aspects of the game, interacting with fellow gamers, and exploring new areas contribute significantly to building connections and enhancing overall gaming experience beyond just accumulating gems. Title: How to Get Zorua in Pokemon White ## Step 1: Understand the Basics Before we dive into the process of getting Zorua in Pokemon White, it's important to understand some basics about this elusive creature. Zorua is a Dark-type Pokemon that was introduced in Generation V. It has the ability Illusion, which allows it to take on the form of the Pokemon in the lead position of your party when it enters battle. However, its disguise will be revealed once it takes damage or uses a move. Zorua cannot be found just anywhere; you need to meet specific requirements and go through particular steps to obtain one. " Fill a bag with many objects of various shapes. Have the children take turns picking out an object from the bag. They decide if the object is square or not. If the object isn't a square, see if they can identify what shape it is. Put different colored shapes made out of construction paper on the floor. Have the children name the shapes as they step on them. SHAPE STICK PUPPETS Have children cut out circle, square, triangle, and rectangle. Have them draw faces on each one. Tape the shapes to popsicle sticks. Write the names of each shape on the back. HIDE AND SEEK SHAPES Cut various shapes out of paper. Hide the shapes around the room. Let the children search for them. As they find them, see if they can identify the shape of each one they find. FOOD EXPERIENCES WITH SHAPES EGG IN A CIRCLE Tear a hole in the center of a piece of bread. Place in a skillet that has been coated with butter. Put a egg in the hole and fry. Have the children look at the bread and tell you what shape they see. Give each child a piece of toast. Let them identify the shape. Have them each take a bite. Ask them if all the shapes now look the same. TRIANGLE CHEESE AND CRACKERS Serve the children triangle shape crackers and cheese. Have them identify the shapes before they eat them. RECTANGLE GRAHAM CRACKERS Give each child a rectangle graham cracker. Let the children spread peanut butter on them. ASSORTED SHAPED CRACKERS Serve crackers in various shapes. See if the children can sort them into circle, squares, rectangles and triangles before eating them. Every know and then make plans to have a shape day with your children. Ask the children to bring in circular objects to display on a round table. Or ask them to wear clothes that contain circular designs. Cut various shapes out of different colors and patterns of contact paper. Let the children peel the backs off the shapes and use them to create designs on pieces of paper. Fill a laundry basket with familiar items that have defininte shapes, such as a ball, softball, wooden block, a book, a plastic egg, a cookie cutter, a box, a postcard, a scarf, and a cake pan. Let the children look through the items and sort them by their shapes. Fill the pages of a magnetic page photo album with a variety of construction paper shapes in different colors and sizes. Set out the book along with a washable felt tip marker. Show the children how to use the marker on the plastic pages to trace the shapes. Clean the pages with a damp cloth after each child's turn. SHAPE PEG BOARD Set a piece of peg board on a table. Use a felt tip marker to draw lines between the holes in the board to make large shape outlines. Let the children follow the outlines and insert golf tees in the holes to make the shapes. SPENCE MAKES CIRCLES BY CHRISTA CHEVALIER BOXES! BOXES! The earliest animal-pollinated flowers were pollinated by insects such as big beetles, long before bees first appeared. Bees are different because they are "specialized" as pollination agents, with behavioral and physical modifications that make pollination easier. Bees are generally better at the task than other pollinating insects such as beetles, flies, butterflies and pollen wasps. The appearance of such floral specialists is believed to have driven the adaptive radiation of the angiosperms, and, in turn, the bees themselves. " "Ah, well," said Balin, drawing a long breath, "is that indeed the man? You want to see what else is in here? Hello youngsters! Today, let's talk around sets and subsets. You may think of a set together a group of things, like a team of pets or a deck of cards. A subset is just a special type of set - it's a set where every one of its elements also belong to another set. Allow me illustrate through an example. Imagine us having a bag filled with apples ($X$) and another bag empty ($ oldphi$). The bag full of apples has many apples, say apple $a$, apple $b$, etc. However, our 2nd bag, the empty one, doesn't contain any apples at all. So, does the empty bag qualify as a subset the the first bag containing apples? Surprisingly, yes! This happens due to some unique rule that applies once we talking about the empty set. But before diving into that, allow's understand the meaning of "subset." We express "$ oldphi$ is a subset of $X$," written as "$ oldphi X$", if everything in $ oldphi$ is likewise present in $X$. In our case, $ oldphi$ has no points or objects within it; therefore, there's nothing inside $ oldphi$ that isn't already component of $X$. That makes $ oldphi$ a legitimate subset the $X$. Seems counterintuitive, but that's exactly how mathematicians defined it! Now, why execute mathematicians define it this way? Well, they usage a statement called a "vacuous truth": For every point y in $ oldphi$, i beg your pardon is empty, y is additionally part of $X$. Since there room NO points in $ oldphi$, this declaration holds real because you can't find ANY points in $ oldphi$ that aren't currently in $X$. It's thus considered a vacuous fact. In short, the empty set $( oldphi)$ is a subset of any kind of other set $(X)$, given that it has zero components, which automatically fulfill the condition that each aspect of $ oldphi$ should also exist in $X$. Isn't mathematics fascinating when we explore these quirks and rules? Happy exploring! You could probably tell me this story yourself, huh? What did you get at the store yesterday? A COCK, being perched among the branches of a lofty tree, crowed aloud, so that the shrillness of his voice echoed through the wood and invited a Fox to the place, who was prowling in that neighbourhood in quest of his prey. But Reynard, finding the Cock was inaccessible by reason of the height of his situation, had recourse to stratagem in order to decoy him down. So, approaching the tree, "Cousin," says he, "I am heartily glad to see you; but at the same time I cannot forbear expressing my uneasiness at the inconvenience of the place, which will not let me pay my respects to you in a handsomer manner; though I suppose you will come down presently, and thus the difficulty will be easily removed. " "Indeed, cousin," says the Cock, "to tell you the truth, I do not think it safe to venture upon the ground; for though I am convinced how much you are my friend, yet I may have the misfortune to fall into the clutches of some other beasts, and what will become of me then?" Title: How to Add a Card to the Apple Wallet on an iPhone Introduction: Apple Wallet is a convenient digital wallet feature available on iPhones that allows you to store various cards such as credit cards, debit cards, loyalty cards, and more. This tutorial will guide you through adding a card to your Apple Wallet on an iPhone with clear explanations, helpful tips, and best practices. Step 1: Open the Wallet App The first step to add a card to the Apple Wallet is to open the pre-installed Wallet app on your iPhone's home screen or app library. The icon consists of a white stylized 'W' against a black background. Launching this app gives access to all your added passes, tickets, rewards cards, and payment methods. Step 2: Tap on the '+' Button To initiate the process of adding a new card, locate the '+' button at the top right corner of the Wallet app interface. By tapping on this button, you are signaling the intention to include additional cards to those already present within your digital wallet. Step 3: Select the Type of Card You Want to Add Upon clicking the '+' button, users can choose from several options depending upon their requirements. These may include Credit or Debit Cards, Loyalty Cards, Rewards Cards, Store Cards, or Boarding Passes among others. Choose the category most applicable to the card you intend to add by scrolling down until you find it. If you wish to add multiple types of cards, repeat these steps accordingly. Key Tip: Make sure you have your physical card handy during this entire procedure since certain details must be entered manually even if they appear automatically via barcode scanning. Step 4 (For Payment Methods): Enter Card Details Manually If you select Credit or Debit Cards, further prompts will ask for necessary information like cardholder name, card number, expiration date, security code (CVV), etc. Some fields might auto-fill based on data previously saved in Safari browser autofill settings; however, crosscheck them carefully before proceeding. For accurate results, input any remaining manual entries precisely. Alternatively, some banks offer direct integration features enabling seamless transfer of relevant banking info directly into the Wallet app. Check whether your bank supports such services. Step 5 (For Payment Methods): Agree to Terms & Conditions After filling out the required fields accurately, agree to terms and conditions presented by either Apple Pay or your respective bank institution. Note that accepting these agreements permits transactions using said card within participating stores, apps, websites, and public transportation systems where contactless payments are accepted. Step 6 (For Other Types of Cards): Follow Specific Instructions Based on Category Depending on what type of card you chose earlier (loyalty, rewards, etc.), specific procedures need following. They generally involve snapping pictures of QR codes or barcodes found on membership cards or coupons. Scan these codes when prompted after selecting the correct category. Once captured correctly, Wallet should recognize and save the encoded information. Step 7: Verify Your Identity (Optional) Some institutions require identity verification before finalizing card addition. Expect text messages containing unique verification codes sent to registered mobile numbers linked to your account. Inputting these codes completes the authentication process. Conclusion: By completing these steps, you now know how to add different kinds of cards to your Apple Wallet on an iPhone. Remember always to double-check every detail while entering sensitive financial information to avoid potential errors. Enjoy reaping the benefits offered by digitally streamlined organization! Jerusalem's architecture is a mixture of old and new. The Old City contains architectural examples from each major period in the city's history. Many ancient historical sites and places of worship stand near modern shopping centers and industrial zones. Architecture from the late 1800s and early 1900s shows European influences. Usefulness rather than style characterizes new apartment buildings constructed by the government as housing for immigrants. Many buildings, old and new, have matching exteriors because all construction is required to be faced with a cream-colored limestone called Jerusalem stone, produced by nearby quarries. Choosing the right wet cat food can be a daunting task with so many brands and types available on the market. However, by following this comprehensive guide, you will learn how to make an informed decision that ensures your feline friend's nutritional needs are met while also catering to their taste preferences. ### Step 1: Understand Your Cat's Nutritional Needs Cats require specific nutrients in their diet to maintain optimal health. These include proteins (mostly from animal sources), amino acids such as taurine and arginine, fatty acids like Omega-3 and -6, vitamins, minerals, and water. A high-quality wet cat food should provide these essential components. Always consult your vet if unsure about what nutrition is best for your cat. ### Step 2: Consider Age & Life Stage Different life stages have different nutritional requirements. Kittens need more calories and certain nutrient ratios than adult cats. Senior cats may benefit from foods formulated for older animals which might contain fewer calories or added supplements for joint health. Look for labels indicating appropriateness for age when purchasing wet cat food. ### Step 3: Check Ingredient List Ingredients are listed according to weight before cooking. High-quality ingredients mean real meat (chicken, fish, turkey) appears at the top of the list. Avoid fillers such as cornmeal, wheat flour, or soybean meal. Also watch out for artificial preservatives, colorings, or flavor enhancers. ### Step 4: Analyze Guaranteed Analysis This section provides percentage content of crude protein, fat, fiber, moisture, etc. While comparing products, ensure they meet Association of American Feed Control Officials (AAFCO) standards. Note that percentages given here are 'as fed', meaning after adding water during manufacturing; thus, dry matter basis comparison could give a clearer picture. ### Step 5: Examine Texture and Consistency Some cats prefer chunky textures while others like pate. Some brands offer varieties within single product lines to accommodate varying texture preferences. It's important because palatability largely depends on texture. ### Step 6: Review Recall History Manufacturers sometimes recall pet foods due to contamination issues or other problems. You can check FDA's website for recalls related to pet foods. Brands with multiple recalls might raise concerns regarding quality control measures. ### Step 7: Compare Prices Price shouldn't be the only deciding factor but it plays a role nonetheless. More expensive doesn't always mean better. Many mid-priced options offer excellent value without compromising on quality. Remember, cost per serving decreases when buying larger cans or cases. ### Key Tips and Guidelines: - Gradually transition new food into your cat's diet over several days to avoid digestive upset. - Canned food has higher water content compared to kibble, helping keep cats hydrated. - Variety is key to preventing pickiness and ensuring balanced nutrition. Rotating between flavors and even brands can help. - Consult your vet regularly to monitor overall health and adjust diet accordingly. - Don't forget dental care! Even though wet food promotes oral health better than dry food, regular teeth cleanings are still necessary. I might not be able to, huh? You can think of this spinning motion as its "spin," just like electrons in atoms have their own spins. Now, suppose we place our toy top on a table with a giant invisible fan blowing air upwards (this represents the magnetic field). Depending on which way the top was initially spun (pointing up or down), it will start to wobble differently when the fan is turned on due to the force acting on its spin. In the example provided, our electron starts with its spin pointing up along the z-axis ($|+ \rangle\_z$). The magnetic field is applied in the y-direction, causing the electron's spin to precess around the magnetic field, similar to how a spinning top wobbles while staying on the table. To predict how the electron's spin changes over time, scientists use something called the Hamiltonian ($\hat{H}$). It describes the total energy of the system, including interactions with other elements like our magnetic field. In this case, the Hamiltonian depends on the strength of the magnetic field and the spin component in the y-direction ($S\_y$). Finally, using some math magic (which looks complicated but is based on basic algebraic principles!), we apply the time-evolution operator to determine the state of the system at any given point in time, represented by $|\psi(t)\rangle$. This gives us information about the probabilities of measuring different spin states as time passes. Once upon a time, there was a man who lived beside the sea. Every day, he went to the beach and watched the original waves roll in. One day, the man noticed a strange thing. He saw a turtle start to escape from the sea. The man went closer and saw that the turtle had a small wagon on its back. The man asked the turtle, "Where are you going?" The turtle replied, "I am escaping from the sea. I want to explore other places, but I can't do it without my wagon." The man thought it was a nice idea. "If you leave, I will miss you," he said. But he also thought it was brave of the turtle to go on an adventure. The next day, the man said goodbye to the turtle. Then, the turtle went on its way. The man waved goodbye and watched the turtle make its escape. From that day on, the man thought of the turtle fondly. He remembered the original explorer who taught him the rewards of taking risks. Anatomy Of A Frog by e-Tutor Staff In this lesson module you will learn about the different parts of a frog. You will also virtually dissect a frog. What is the anatomy of a frog? Frogs are amphibians, living both on land and in water. Their anatomy is very unique. Their bodies are similar to humans in that they have skin, bones, muscles, and organs. The body of a frog can be divided into a head, a short neck, and a trunk. The head contains the brain, mouth, eyes, ears and nose. The frog's head movement is limited due to the short, almost rigid neck. The trunk of a frog forms walls for a single body cavity known as the coelom. The coelom holds all of the frog's internal organs. Frogs have the same kinds of organs as humans and the same organ systems. For example, frogs have a long, sticky tongue which they use to capture food. They also have teeth, which unfortunately are very weak and rather useless. Humans have tongues and teeth as well (and a mouth of course). A frog uses its tongue for grabbing prey. The vomarine and maxillary teeth are used for holding the prey. The internal nostrils are used by the frog for breathing. The tympanic membrane is the eardrum. It is located behind the frog's eyes. The eustachian tubes equalize the pressure in the frog's inner ear. The glottis is a tube, which leads to the lungs, while the esophagus is a tube which leads to the frog's stomach. The stomach helps the frog break down food and the liver also helps with digestion (it makes bile). Bile (also known as gall) is a fluid secreted by hepatocytes from the liver of most vertebrates (humans and frogs are vertebrates). Hepatocytes are cells present in the liver, and they initiate the formation and secretion of bile. In many species, bile is stored in the gall bladder between meals. When eating, the bile is discharged into the duodenum. Bile, therefore helps with digestion. The duodenum, which is the first and shortest part of the small intestine, is responsible for the breakdown of food in the small intestine. Most chemical digestion takes place in the duodenum. The small intestine absorbs nutrients from food. The large intestine absorbs water. It also collects waste. You can also think of the cloaca as storing waste, as this part of the frog collects eggs, sperm, urine and feces. The cloaca (opening) is also where sperm, eggs, urine, and feces exit the frog's body. The spleen stores blood, while the kidneys filter the blood. The ureters carry urine from the kidneys to the bladder. The (urinary) bladder stores urine. The testes make sperm, while the ovaries makes eggs and the eggs travel through the oviducts. A frog's skin is always moist. It is made up of two layers, an outer epidermis and an inner dermis. In addition to protecting the frog, the skin also helps the frog breathe. A frog will take in oxygen from the water through their skin. The oxygen in the water passes through their skin and goes directly to their blood. Frogs also have a pair of lungs which allows them to breathe when on land. A frog has very few bones. They make up the skeleton of the frog. The skull (head bone) is large and flat. The legs are long for jumping. In addition to being specialized for jumping, the bones in their upper and hind legs are also specialized for leaping. The muscles move the skeleton of the frog. The muscles help the frog jump and swim. Frogs have other systems similar to humans that are a part of their bodies. For example, a frog has a nervous system (all of the nerves and the spine), a circulatory system (the heart and blood going through the body) and a digestive system (the food going through the mouth to the intestines). The heart (which is part of the circulatory system) is three-chambered. There are two atria and one ventricle. There is a valve within the frog's heart known as the spiral valve. It directs the flow of blood to prevent oxygenated and de-oxygenated blood from mixing. A frog's sense of hearing (which is part of the nervous system) is highly developed. Frogs can detect high-pitched sounds with their ears. In addition to breathing oxygen through their skin when in water, frogs can also detect low-pitched sounds through their skin. Another highly developed system is a frog's sense of sight and smell (which again are both part of the nervous system). Frogs detect predators and prey using their large eyes. Their eyes however have poorly developed eyelids, which do not close. In order to close its eye, a frog has to draw the eye into its socket. There is a third eyelid, known as the nictitating membrane. It can be drawn over the pulled in eye (eyeball). Frogs also use their sense of smell to detect chemical signals. These signals help them to identify potential food. Now that we know the basics of frog anatomy, let's move onto the Activities and Extended Learning. Write some of the things you have learned about frogs from the Study Guide. Your answer should be in short essay format, 3-5 paragraphs. As you write the essay, answer the following questions in complete sentences, using your own words: 1. How are frogs adapted for aquatic life? 2. How are frogs adapted for terrestrial life? 3. If frogs have lungs, why do they also breathe through their skin? Get a book or search the web for the human body. See if you can find the similarities and differences between a frog's anatomy and a human's anatomy. You should be able find some of the information in the Study Guide. You can also use the links provided in the Resources. How are frogs and humans alike? How are frogs and humans different? Once there was a beggar, who went from door to door. But there was no food for him, the people were too poor. Then one day, he had a plan to feed the whole town. He would make them Stone Soup, a pot was soon found. Next, he filled the pot up, with water and a stone. The villagers were grateful, one shared a meaty bone. One ran home and got some carrots to add to the pot. Another shared potatoes, as the soup was getting hot. 'Round and 'round the beggar stirred, while others joined the group. One brought onions, one brought beans, they added to the soup. When the soup was all done and everyone was fed. The villagers took their bowls and went home to their beds. The next day they decided, to all go as a group, To thank the town beggar, for his magic soup. But when they went to find him, they found that he was gone. He stuck his hand in the cake, huh? Nouns are words for things, and since things can be possessed, nouns can also change to show possession in grammar. In English, we usually add an apostrophe and an "s" to nouns to make them "possessive", or sometimes just an apostrophe when there is already an "s" at the end, like this: The Universe is all of time and space and its contents. It is made of many millions of billions of stars and planets and enormous clouds of gas separated by a big space. [looks at the object she is holding] They're not going to be able to read it!
