[Sarah considers tape recording sessions one of her schools] Parts of Speech Verbs: All the Right Moves Verbs are words that name an action or describe a state of being. Verbs are seriously important, because there's no way to have a sentence without them. While we're on the topic, every sentence must have two parts: a subject and a predicate. - A subject tells who or what the sentence is about. The subject is a noun or a pronoun. - A predicate tells what the subject is or does. The verb is found in the predicate. There are four basic types of verbs: action verbs, linking verbs, helping verbs, verb phrases. You Could Look It Up Verbs are words that name an action or describe a state of being. The action of an action verb can be a visible action (such as gamble, walk, kvetch) or a mental action (such as think, learn, cogitate). Quoth the Maven To determine if a verb is transitive, ask yourself, "Who?" or "What?" after the verb. If you can find an answer in the sentence, the verb is transitive. Action Verbs: Jumping Jack Flash Action verbs tell what the subject does. For example: jump, kiss, laugh. - The mobsters broke Irving's kneecaps. - Some people worry about the smallest things. An action verb can be transitive or intransitive. Transitive verbs need a direct object. - The boss dropped the ball. - The workers picked it up. Intransitive verbs do not need a direct object. - Who called? - Icicles dripped from his voice. Chain Gang: Linking Verbs Linking verbs join the subject and the predicate. Linking verbs do not show action. Instead, they help the words at the end of the sentence name and describe the subject. Here are the most common linking verbs: be, feel, grow, seem, smell, remain, appear, sound, stay, look, taste, turn, become. Although small in size as well as number, linking verbs are used a great deal. Here are two typical examples: Quoth the Maven To determine whether a verb is being used as a linking verb or an action verb, use am, are, or is for the verb. If the sentence makes sense with the substitution, the original verb is a linking verb. - The manager was happy about the job change. - He is a fool. Many linking verbs can also be used as action verbs. For example: - Linking: The kids looked sad. - Action: I looked for the dog in the pouring rain. Mother's Little Helper: Helping Verbs Helping verbs are added to another verb to make the meaning clearer. Helping verbs include any form of to be. Here are some examples: do, does, did, have, has, had, shall, should, will, would, can, could, may, might, must. Verb phrases are made of one main verb and one or more helping verbs. - They will run before dawn. - They do have a serious problem. Identify each of the verbs in the following sentences. Remember to look for action verbs, linking verbs, and helping verbs. - A group of chess enthusiasts had checked into a hotel. - They were standing in the lobby discussing their recent tournament victories. - After about an hour, the manager came out of the office and asked them to disperse. - "But why?" they asked as they moved off. - "Because," he said, "I can't stand chess nuts boasting in an open foyer." - had checked - were standing, discussing - came, asked, disperse - asked, moved - said, can't stand, boasting One more time, with gusto! Underline the verbs in each of these sentences. - I can please only one person per day. Today is not your day. - I love deadlines. I especially like the whooshing sound they make as they fly by. - Tell me what you need, and I'll tell you how to get along without it. - Accept that some days you are the pigeon and some days the statue. - Everybody is somebody else's weirdo. - I don't have an attitude problem; you have a perception problem. - Last night I lay in bed looking up at the stars in the sky, and I thought to myself, where the heck is the ceiling? - My reality check bounced. - On the keyboard of life, always keep one finger on the escape key. - Never argue with an idiot. They drag you down to their level, then beat you with experience. - can please, is - love, like, make, fly - tell, tell, get - accept, are - don't have, have - lay, looking, thought, is - argue, drag, beat Excerpted from The Complete Idiot's Guide to Grammar and Style 2003 by Laurie E. So either walk around the furniture or we have a gated off area. Becoming a teacher in Colorado is an exciting journey that involves several steps. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process, providing in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines to help you achieve your goal with ease and confidence. Step 1: Earn a Bachelor's Degree To become a licensed teacher in Colorado, you must hold at least a bachelor's degree from a regionally accredited college or university. Choose an academic major related to the subject area(s) you wish to teach. Completing coursework in education foundational areas like child development, instructional strategies, and classroom management is also recommended. Key Tip: Consider enrolling in a state-approved educator preparation program as part of your undergraduate studies. These programs provide essential teaching skills and often include student teaching experiences. Step 2: Pass Required Examinations Colorado requires aspiring teachers to pass specific content examinations depending on their chosen endorsement area(s). The Praxis Subject Assessments are used to evaluate content knowledge. Additionally, candidates seeking initial licensure must take the PLACE (Program for Licensing Assessment for Colorado Educators) assessment in Professional Knowledge. Guideline: Register for these tests early and allow ample time to study using available resources such as test prep materials and practice exams. Step 3: Obtain Fingerprinting and Background Check All applicants for Colorado educator licenses must submit fingerprints for both a federal and state criminal history check. You can do this by scheduling an appointment with MorphoTrust USA, a company contracted by CDE for fingerprint services. Key Tips: Make sure to bring valid photo identification when getting fingerprinted. Also, keep in mind that processing times may vary, so plan accordingly. Step 4: Apply for Initial Teaching License Once all prerequisites have been met, complete the online application for an Initial Teacher License via the Colorado Department of Education's eLicensing system. Provide official transcripts showing proof of bachelor's degree completion and scores from required assessments. Pay necessary fees associated with the license application. Guidelines: Double-check all information before submitting the application. Errors or omissions could delay the review process. Step 5: Complete Induction Program Within three years of obtaining your initial teaching license, you need to participate in a performance-based induction program approved by the Colorado State Board of Education. During this period, work closely with a mentor teacher who provides guidance, support, and feedback throughout your first few years of teaching. Key Tips: Actively engage in professional development opportunities offered during the induction program. Use this time to hone your craft and build strong relationships with students, colleagues, and administrators. Step 6: Upgrade Your License After successfully completing the induction program, you can upgrade your Initial Teacher License to a Professional Teacher License. To do so, maintain current employment as a teacher, fulfill ongoing professional development requirements, and meet any additional endorsement area needs. Guidelines: Keep track of completed professional development hours and ensure they align with state standards. Submit renewal applications biennially through the eLicensing system. By following these steps, you'll be well on your way to becoming a successful teacher in Colorado. Remember that patience, persistence, and passion for learning are crucial traits shared among effective educators. Good luck! If you open your mouth XXX the water in breathe in you'll choke. Six weeks later, he scratched smallpox into the boy's skin. The boy did not get sick from smallpox. This boy was the first person ever to get a vaccination. Color Theory Basics What is Color? There are two types of color, first is the color that comes from a light source like TVs and monitors, and the second is the color that is reflected. Printed documents and every other object absorb some colors and reflect others. If an object absorbs every color other than red, than we see that object as a red colored object. Additive Colors and Subtractive Colors Additive color mixing is the method of creating colors by mixing red, green and blue lights. It is the method that is used in TVs and monitors. If these colors are mixed 100 % white light will be produced. Subtractive color mixing is the method of creating colors by mixing pigments. In this method, when cyan and yellow mixed, green is produced. Red is produced by mixing yellow and magenta, and blue is produced by mixing cyan and magenta. RGB Color Model RGB is the color model that is used in monitors, scanners etc. RGB stands for "Red Green Blue". Each has a range from 0 to 255. If all the values are 255, we see white. If all values are zero, than we see black. If red is 255 but green and blue are zero, we see pure red. All (16,777,216) colors can be generated by mixing red, green and blue. These are the primary colors of light: RGB(255,0,0) means Red = 255, Green = 0 and Blue = 0. It is red. RGB(0,255,0) means Red = 0, Green = 255 and Blue = 0. It is green. RGB(0,0,255) means Red = 0, Green = 0 and Blue = 255. It is blue. These are the secondary colors: RGB(255,255,0) means Red = 255, Green = 255 and Blue = 0. It is yellow (red+green). RGB(255,0,255) means Red = 255, Green = 0 and Blue = 255. It is magenta (red+blue). RGB(0,255,255) means Red = 0, Green = 255 and Blue = 255. It is cyan (green+blue). HSL Color Model HSL stands for "Hue Saturation Luminosity". Hue is the purest form of a color. It has a range from 0 to 360. If saturation and luminosity are in their default values, and we set the hue to 0, then we see pure red. If we set it to 120, then we see pure green. If we set it to 240, then we see pure blue. Saturation is the purity of a color. More saturated colors are less mixed with gray. Less saturated colors are more grayish. It has a range from 0 to 100. The default value for saturation is 100. Luminosity is the brightness of a color. It has a range from 0 to 100. The default value for luminosity is 50. Color wheel is a circle which harmonious colors can be derived. It is created according to the color theory. Primary colors of light are red, green and blue. They are the colors which can not be formed by mixing other colors. Secondary colors of light are cyan magenta and yellow. They are the colors which are formed by mixing two primary colors. Tertiary colors are the colors which are formed by mixing a secondary with a primary. Harmonious colors are the colors that are selected from color wheel according to color theory. They are the "Matching Colors" which looks fine together. There are different harmonious color schemes. For example, in analogous scheme, the harmonious colors are the directly beside the selected color. In the complementary scheme, the harmonious colors are directly opposite in the color wheel. Split Complementary Colors Color colors are blues and greens; they are the colors of snow and ice. They appear to recede into space. Warm Colors are reds and yellows; they are the colors of sun and fire. Warm colors appear closer than cool colors. They give a sense of warmth by increasing the blood pressure. Warm colors also give a sense of energy, comfort and happiness. Shades are dark values of a color. They can be formed by mixing the color with black. Tints are light values of color a color. They can be formed by mixing the color with white. You had a stop and think, huh? In snow time you don't see the sun and can make a snowman. Phases of matter can be changed by a number of things. The most common ones are temperature and pressure. Substances usually become a gas at warm temperatures and/or low pressures, become a solid at cool temperatures, and become a plasma at extremely hot temperatures. Substances often become liquid in between solid and gas, but when the pressure is very low (such as the vacuum of outer space) many substances skip the liquid phase, going directly from solid to gas or vice versa. Scientists have created graphs called phase change diagrams to show the relationship between pressure, temperature, and phase of many substances. Look at your pretty shirt that Grandma bought you! Title: How to Get Charmander in Pok mon Yellow Pok mon Yellow is a popular role-playing game developed by Game Freak and published by Nintendo for the Game Boy system. The objective of the game is to become a Master Trainer by capturing and training various species of creatures called Pok mon. One such desirable starter Pok mon is Charmander, a Fire-type lizard Pok mon. This article will guide you through obtaining Charmander in your Pok mon Yellow adventure. **Step 1:** Start Your Journey ------------------------------ To begin, turn on your Game Boy or emulator and load up your Pok mon Yellow cartridge. Once you reach the title screen, select 'New Game' using either the A button (to confirm) or B button (to cancel). After selecting New Game, choose your name and proceed with customizing your character. You are now ready to start your journey! ### Explanation By starting a new game, you create a unique save file that stores all of your progress throughout the game. Selecting your name personalizes your experience as other trainers within the game will address you by this moniker during battles and interactions. Customizing your character provides an opportunity to express yourself visually before diving into the world of Kanto. **Step 2:** Choose Your Starter Pok mon ------------------------------------- Professor Oak, a renowned Pok mon researcher, invites you to his lab after your introduction to Pallet Town. Upon arriving at the lab, he asks you to choose one of three available starter Pok mon-Bulbasaur, Squirtle, or Pikachu. However, instead of offering Charmander directly, Professor Oak presents him to your rival, Gary. ![Image of Bulbasaur, Charmander, Squirtle, and Pikachu]( He waited. He went on waiting. People in Maine produce agricultural goods like poultry, eggs, dairy products, cattle, wild blueberries (the state makes 25% of all blueberries in North America, making it the largest blueberry maker in the world), apples, maple syrup and maple sugar. Aroostook County is known for its potato crops. Commercial fishing, once a mainstay of the state's economy, maintains a presence, mostly lobstering and groundfishing. Western Maine and springs are a big source of bottled water. Maine's company outputs make paper, lumber and wood products, electronic stuff, leather things, food products, textiles, and bio-technology. Naval shipbuilding and building remain key as well, with Bath Iron Works in Bath and Portsmouth Naval Shipyard in Kittery. Naval Air Station Brunswick is also in Maine, and serves as a large support base for the U.S. Navy. A CERTAIN Husbandman, lying at the point of death, and being desirous his sons should pursue that innocent, entertaining course of agriculture in which he himself had been engaged all his life, made use of this expedient to induce them to it. He called them to his bed-side and spoke to this effect: "All the patrimony I have to bequeath you, Sons, is my farm and my vineyard, of which I make you joint heirs. But I charge you not to let it go out of your own occupation; for if I have any treasure besides, it lies buried somewhere in the ground, within a foot of the surface. " This made the Sons conclude that he talked of money which he had hid there; so, after their father's death, with unwearied diligence and application, they carefully dug up every inch, both of the farm and vineyard; from which it came to pass that, though they missed the treasure which they expected, the ground, by being so well stirred and loosened, produced so plentiful a crop of all that was sowed in it as proved a real, and no inconsiderable treasure. Basket makers use a wide variety of materials to create a basket, such as bark, willow rods, leaves, wire, plastic, paper, and rope. There are three basic kinds of baskets-coiled, twined, or woven. A woven basket is made of spokes and weavers: the spokes run up and down and the weavers go over and under the spokes. A coiled basket is made by sewing rings of a fibrous material to the previous ring. Twined baskets have flexible weavers that are twined around the spokes in a variety of patterns. Title: How to Edit the Category of a wikiHow Forum Discussion You Started Introduction: As a member of the vibrant wikiHow community, you may have started discussions on the wikiHow forum. Sometimes, due to various reasons, you might need to edit the category of your discussion. This tutorial will guide you through the process with clear, concise steps, along with helpful tips and guidelines. By following these instructions, you can ensure that your discussion reaches the right audience and remains organized within the appropriate category. Step 1: Log into Your Account Visit the wikiHow homepage (<https://www.wikihow.com/>) and click on 'Sign In' at the top-right corner. Enter your email address or username and password associated with your account, then click 'Log In.' Being logged in allows you to access the necessary functions required to edit categories for your forum discussions. Key tip: Bookmarking the login page can save time during future logins. Step 2: Access the wikiHow Community Dashboard Once signed in, hover over your profile picture located at the upper-right corner. A dropdown menu should appear; click on 'Community Dashboard.' The dashboard is where all user interactions, including recent posts and threads, are displayed. It provides an overview of ongoing conversations across different categories. Guideline: Make sure you familiarize yourself with other features available in the dashboard, such as bookmarks, contributions, and settings. These options can enhance your overall experience on the platform. Step 3: Navigate to Your Discussions On the left sidebar of the Community Dashboard, locate the section named 'Your Interactions.' Under this heading, there should be several subcategories - one being 'Discussions.' Click on 'Discussions,' which filters the content shown exclusively to those initiated by you. Step 4: Select the Targeted Discussion From the list of discussions generated in Step 3, identify the specific thread whose category needs editing. Once selected, proceed by clicking on its title to open the full post. Step 5: Initiate Editing Mode Within the opened discussion, look towards the bottom-left corner below the last comment. There should be two buttons labeled 'Follow' and 'Improve'; choose the latter. Pressing 'Improve' reveals additional options, among them 'Change Topic Category.' Step 6: Choose New Category Clicking on 'Change Topic Category' opens up a dialog box containing multiple categories to select from. Browse through the options carefully until finding the most suitable choice. Keep in mind the nature of your discussion when determining the proper classification. Key Tip: If no relevant category exists, consider contacting a moderator via private message requesting assistance. They can help create new categories if deemed necessary. Step 7: Save Changes After selecting the ideal category, press the blue 'Apply Change' button found near the bottom-right corner of the dialog box. Upon confirmation, the system saves the changes made and automatically reassigns the discussion under the chosen category. Guideline: Double-check the updated category before saving any modifications. Ensuring correct categorization promotes better organization and visibility for fellow users. Conclusion: Editing the category of a wikiHow forum discussion requires patience and attention to detail. Following this tutorial ensures accurate categorization while enhancing the usability of the site for both you and others. They forgot to make ears for this poor dolly. - slide 1 of 3 This lesson plan introduces preschoolers to the pumpkin plant and how it grows. They will see how a pumpkin plant develops from a seed, how it flowers and produces a large round orange fruit. - The objective is to acquaint the children with how vegetables, pumpkins in this case, grow. - They will learn about how pumpkins are good to eat. - In discussing what they have learned, they will improve their comprehension and speaking skills. - Pumpkin seeds - A garden patch or pots containing soil - Flash cards showing different stages of pumpkin growth - Posters showing different types of pumpkins - Photographs of Halloween pumpkins - Chart paper - Drawing Paper - slide 2 of 3 Talk to the preschoolers about pumpkins. Ask them if they have seen a pumpkin or eaten it. Ask them to tell you what it looks like. Talk about the types of food that can be made using a pumpkin. Show the preschoolers a real pumpkin and compare it to their earlier observations. Point out the stalk from which the pumpkin is attached to the vine. Tell the children that pumpkins grow on vines. Ask them to imagine what would happen if big ripe pumpkins grew on trees. Hand out pumpkin seeds and either form groups or let each child grow his or her own pumpkin plant. Show the children how to plant and water the seeds in a garden patch if one is available, or in pots filled with garden soil. The children can observe how the pumpkin plant develops from the seeds, produces leaves, flowers, and then pumpkins. They can see how the pumpkin ripens, and if not picked, how it starts to decay. The seeds from its pulp will return to the soil to grow anew. Together with the children, prepare a chart noting the pumpkin growth and its eventual decay as it happens. Along with the practical gardening, or if practical gardening is not possible, show the preschoolers flashcards and/or videos detailing the pumpkin life cycle. - slide 3 of 3 Encourage each child to talk about his or her experience in gardening and watching the pumpkins grow. Ask them to tell you what the pumpkin seeds look like, how they are sowed in the soil, how and when the pumpkin plant appears, how it grows, what the pumpkin flower looks like, how the pumpkins look when they first appear and how they change as they grow, how to tell when a pumpkin is ripe and when it is decaying. Distribute drawing sheets and colors, and ask the children to make colored drawings of the pumpkin life cycle. They can also make creative, colored drawings of pumpkins and pumpkin vines. What Else to Do Read out aloud stories and poems featuring pumpkins. Bake and salt pumpkin seeds and offer them to the children to eat. If possible, also serve pumpkin pies. Talk about how pumpkins are carved on Halloween. Show the preschoolers some photographs of carved Halloween pumpkins, or ask them to bring photos of pumpkins their families carved. The Golden Gate Bridge is a suspension bridge that crosses over the San Francisco Bay, going from San Francisco to Marin County, in the U.S. state of California. It was opened for use in 1937. When the bridge was finished, its length of 9,266 ft (2824 m) made it the longest bridge in the world until 1964. It carries U.S. Route 101 and California State Route 1. "Sir William should have more faith in us," returned Singleton, with his agreeable smile. The Earth has several poles, not just two. It has geographic north and south poles, which are the points that mark the Earth's axis of rotation. It also has magnetic north and south poles, based on the planet's magnetic field. When you use a compass, it points to the magnetic north pole, not the geographic North Pole. The Earth's magnetic poles move. The magnetic North Pole moves in loops of up to 50 miles (80 km) per day. But its actual location, an average of all these loops, is also moving at around 25 miles a year [ref]. In the last 150 years, the pole has wandered a total of about 685 miles (1102 kilometers). The magnetic South Pole moves in a similar fashion. The poles can also switch places. Scientists can study when this has happened by examining rocks on the ocean floor that retain traces of the field, similar to a recording on a magnetic tape. The last time the poles switched was 780,000 years ago, and it's happened about 400 times in 330 million years. Each reversal takes a thousand years or so to complete, and it takes longer for the shift to take effect at the equator than at the poles. The field has weakened about 10% in the last 150 years. Some scientists think this is a sign of a flip in progress. The Earth's physical structure is behind all this magnetic shifting. The planet's inner core is made of solid iron. Surrounding the inner core is a molten outer core. The next layer out, the mantle, is solid but malleable, like plastic. Finally, the layer we see every day is called the crust. Image courtesy USGS The Earth itself spins on its axis. The inner core spins as well, and it spins at a different rate than the outer core. This creates a dynamo effect, or convections and currents within the core. This is what creates the Earth's magnetic field -- it's like a giant electromagnet. Exactly how the dynamo effect changes the field isn't widely understood. Shifts in the core's rate of spin and the currents within the molten material most likely affect the planet's field and the location of the poles. In other words, the poles move because the convection in the core changes. These changes might also cause the poles to switch places. Irregularities where the core and mantle meet and changes to the Earth's crust, like large earthquakes, can also change the magnetic field. He makes loud and happy sounds. He likes to pretend he is a mighty king with a big army. One day, Tommy's trumpet breaks. He tries to blow, but no sound comes out. He feels sad and angry. He cries and throws his trumpet on the floor. "Tommy, what's wrong?" his mom asks. She sees the broken trumpet and the tears on his face. "My trumpet is broken! I can't play anymore!" Tommy says. "Don't worry, Tommy. I can help you fix it. Let me see what's wrong." his mom says. She picks up the trumpet and looks inside. She sees a small hole in the metal. "Look, Tommy. There is a hole here. That's why you can't make any sound. But we can fix it with some tape. Do you want to help me?" his mom says. "Yes, please!" Tommy says. He wipes his tears and smiles. He gets some tape from the drawer and gives it to his mom. His mom covers the hole with the tape and presses it hard. She gives the trumpet back to Tommy. "Here you go, Tommy. Try it now." his mom says. Tommy puts the trumpet to his mouth and blows. He hears a loud and happy sound. He jumps and claps. "It works! It works! Thank you, mom! You are the best!" Tommy says. How to Find Your Personal Video Style ===================================== Are you looking to create videos that truly reflect your unique perspective and personality? Finding your personal video style can help set you apart from other content creators, engage your audience, and bring consistency to your work. This guide will walk you through the process of discovering and developing your own distinct video style. ### Understanding the Importance of Video Style Your video style is the combination of visual and auditory elements that give your content its signature look and feel. It includes aspects such as: * Color palette * Visual composition * Sound design * Editing techniques * Pacing * Storytelling approach Having a well-defined video style allows you to: 1. Create a cohesive body of work 2. Build brand recognition 3. Connect with your target audience 4. Express yourself more effectively 5. Stand out among competitors Have you been XXX plate of it? Title: How to Create Bullet Points on Reddit Using an iPhone or iPad Step 1: Open the Reddit App Find the Reddit app on your home screen or in the app library and tap on it to open. If you don't have the Reddit app installed, download it from the App Store first. Step 2: Log in to Your Account If you aren't already logged in, enter your credentials to access your Reddit account. Creating bullet points is only possible when you are logged in and ready to post a comment or submit a new post. Step 3: Navigate to the Desired Thread or Subreddit Use the search bar at the top of the screen to find a specific subreddit or thread where you would like to make a comment or create a new post with bullet points. Tap on the thread or subreddit to open it. Step 4: Prepare to Comment or Post New Content To add bullet points to existing content, locate the comment box under any post within the selected thread. To create a new post with bullet points, tap on the 'Create post' button (usually located at the bottom right corner) and select the type of post you want to make (text, image, link, etc.). Step 5: Access the Keyboard Settings Tap inside the text input area (comment box or new post composition field). This will bring up the keyboard. Look for the 'Globe' icon next to the spacebar; this is used to switch between different keyboard layouts. Step 6: Enable the Numeric Keypad Layout Tap on the 'Globe' icon until you see the numeric keypad layout appear. The numeric keypad layout may look slightly different based on which third-party keyboard apps you have installed, but generally, it should include numbers and symbols along with a few special characters. Step 7: Insert Bullet Points Manually Unfortunately, there isn't a dedicated shortcut for inserting bullet points directly into Reddit comments using iPhones or iPads. However, you can still manually add them by following these steps: a. Long press the asterisk (*) symbol on the numeric keypad until a pop-up appears with alternative characters. b. Slide your finger over to the left arrow key (<-) to get back to the previous page of available characters. c. Locate and tap on the black small circle ( ), which represents a bullet point character. Repeat these steps as needed to create multiple bullet points. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Always save time for proofreading before submitting your comment or post. Double-check that all intended bullet points were correctly added and formatted accordingly. * Remember that typing speed might be slower while adding manual bullet points compared to desktop computers due to limited options on mobile devices. * Consider practicing these steps outside of high-pressure situations so that they become second nature when needed during active discussions or debates. Title: How to Have Fun in Your Classroom at Lunchtime Introduction: Lunchtime is often seen as a break from learning, but it doesn't have to be just that. Transforming your classroom into a fun space can help create a positive atmosphere, strengthen student relationships, and even provide additional educational opportunities. This guide will walk you through the steps of creating an enjoyable lunchtime experience in your classroom. Step 1: Obtain Necessary Approvals Before making any changes to your classroom or planning activities, ensure you have obtained the necessary approvals from school administration. Explain your goals and plans, emphasizing benefits such as promoting a sense of community and providing a safe, supervised environment during lunchtime. Key Tips: * Be prepared to address concerns about noise levels and potential damage to school property. * Offer to collaborate with other teachers and staff members to share ideas and resources. Why It Helps: Gaining approval beforehand ensures smooth implementation and reduces potential obstacles down the line. Step 2: Design a Comfortable Space Rearrange desks and furniture to create comfortable seating areas where students can relax and socialize. Add cushions, bean bags, or area rugs to make the space more inviting. If possible, bring in some plants or decorations to brighten up the room. Key Guidelines: * Maintain clear pathways for safety and easy access to exits. * Consider lighting conditions when choosing seating arrangements; avoid placing students in dimly lit corners. Why It Helps: A well-designed space encourages students to feel at ease and engage in conversations, fostering stronger connections between classmates. Step 3: Plan Engaging Activities Organize various low-key games, puzzles, or art supplies that promote collaboration and creativity. Examples include board games, card games, jigsaw puzzles, coloring books, and origami paper. Rotate these items regularly to keep things fresh and exciting. Key Tips: * Choose activities suitable for different age groups and skill levels. * Set ground rules for behavior and proper usage of materials. The one you kept going on and on and on and on. Title: How to Access the FM Radio on an iPod Nano The iPod Nano, particularly the 6th generation model, comes with a built-in FM radio tuner that allows you to listen to your favorite local stations without needing internet connectivity. This feature can be quite handy when you want to save battery life or if you're in an area with poor cell service. Here is a comprehensive guide on how to access and use this feature. **Things You'll Need:** 1. An iPod Nano (6th Generation) 2. A pair of wired headphones (the radio function uses the headphone cord as an antenna) **Step 1: Check Your iPod Nano Model** Firstly, ensure that you have the correct model of iPod Nano. The FM radio feature is only available on the 6th generation model. To check this, look at the back of your device for the model number which will start with 'N'. If it ends with '0', then it's a 6th generation iPod Nano. **Step 2: Connect Your Headphones** Plug one end of your wired headphones into the headphone jack located at the top of your iPod Nano. The other end should be inserted into your ear or looped around so that it doesn't dangle and potentially damage the cable. Remember, the headphones serve as the antenna for receiving FM signals. **Step 3: Navigate to the Radio Icon** Turn on your iPod Nano by pressing any button. Once awake, press the Home button (the middle button below the screen) to go to the main menu. Scroll left or right until you see the Radio icon, represented by sound waves. Tap it to open the FM radio application. **Step 4: Select a Band** After opening the radio app, you'll notice two bands - one labeled 'FM' and another 'AM'. Since we are focusing on accessing the FM radio, leave it set to 'FM'. AM radios require more power and generally provide lower audio quality than FM radios. **Step 5: Start Scanning** Tap on 'Scan' located at the bottom right corner of the screen. Your iPod Nano will automatically search for available frequencies within the FM band. This process may take several seconds depending upon the number of strong signals nearby. **Step 6: Save Preferred Stations** As the scan progresses, some clear station frequencies will appear briefly before being replaced by others. When you find a station you like, quickly double tap its name to bookmark it for easy future access. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - Moving the position of the headphone cord changes the reception slightly, so try adjusting it if you're having trouble finding a clear signal. - Keep your body away from large metal objects while using the FM radio since they could interfere with the signal. - Maintain volume control at about halfway to reduce potential static interference. - Note that the iPod Nano does not support RDS (Radio Data System), meaning it won't display song names or artists from broadcasting stations. By following these steps and guidelines, you can enjoy free local radio programming anywhere, anytime, straight from your iPod Nano! In England, in Anglo-Saxon times, "Shires" were units for getting taxes. They usually had a town at their centre. People called these towns the "shire town". The shires had the same name as their shire town (for example Bedford"shire"). Later people called these towns the "county town". However, after solving the problem, we also ask ourselves if our answer is correct i.e. if our answer is justified. How do we justify your solution? Here are the steps we can use to countercheck our answers: - Did you understand the problem? - What are the given data in the problem? - What does the problem ask for? - Is your procedure correct? - Did you answer the question? - There are 4 bananas in the plate. How long would it take to get from Jenny's house to the university? Henry to Jenny's house is equal to 1 hour. And from Henry's house to the university is 2 hours. We wanted to know how long it will take to get from Jenny's house to the university. In this case, we add up the number of hours from Jenny's to Henry's and from Henry's to the university. Therefore, the number of hours from Jenny's house to the university is 3 hours. - There are 2 dozens of strawberry in the fridge. Mother bought another dozen. Title: How to Have an Indoor Yard Sale An indoor yard sale, also known as a garage sale or rummage sale, is an excellent way to declutter your home, make some extra cash, and meet neighbors within your community. By organizing an indoor yard sale, you're providing an opportunity for people to purchase gently used items at reduced prices while keeping reusable goods out of landfills. This wikiHow guide will walk you through the process of hosting a successful indoor yard sale, ensuring that both sellers and buyers have a positive experience. ### Part 1: Preparation #### Step 1: Choose a Suitable Location Select a spacious area inside your house where you can comfortably display all the items you wish to sell. Common locations include garages, basements, living rooms, or recreation areas. Adequate space allows customers to browse with ease without bumping into one another or feeling cramped. Having enough room also ensures proper organization and presentation of merchandise, which may lead to increased sales. #### Step 2: Determine Dates and Times Schedule your indoor yard sale during weekends when most individuals are off work and available to shop. Saturdays tend to be more popular than Sundays; however, consider holding your event over two consecutive days (Saturday and Sunday) to maximize exposure and potential sales. Plan for a full day of selling from morning till late afternoon, giving yourself ample time to accommodate various schedules and customer preferences. TIP: Check local events, festivals, or farmer's markets occurring simultaneously in your region. Avoid scheduling conflicts by choosing dates outside these busy periods to ensure maximum foot traffic and visibility for your indoor yard sale. #### Step 3: Gather Items to Sell It is used to make special glasses and ceramics, including the Mount Palomar telescope's 200 inch mirror. Lithium is the lightest known metal and can be alloyed with aluminium, copper, manganese, and cadmium to make strong, lightweight metals for aircraft. No we haven't told him or anything no. Every time you put " (double-quotes) around a piece of text you have been making a string. A string is how you make something that your program might give to a human. You print them, save them to files, send them to web servers, all sorts of things. Strings are really handy, so in this exercise you will learn how to make strings that have variables embedded in them. You embed variables inside a string by using specialized format sequences and then putting the variables at the end with a special syntax that tells Python, "Hey, this is a format string, put these variables in there." As usual, just type this in even if you do not understand it and make it exactly the same. my_name = 'Zed A. Shaw' my_age = 35 # not a lie my_height = 74 # inches my_weight = 180 # lbs my_eyes = 'Blue' my_teeth = 'White' my_hair = 'Brown' print "Let's talk about %s." % my_name print "He's %d inches tall." % my_height print "He's %d pounds heavy." % my_weight print "Actually that's not too heavy." print "He's got %s eyes and %s hair." % (my_eyes, my_hair) print "His teeth are usually %s depending on the coffee." % my_teeth # this line is tricky, try to get it exactly right print "If I add %d, %d, and %d I get %d." % ( my_age, my_height, my_weight, my_age + my_height + my_weight) Remember to put # -- coding: utf-8 -- at the top if you use non-ASCII characters and get an encoding error. They need to start with a character, so a1 would work, but 1 will not. - What does %s, %r, and %d do again? - You'll learn more about this as you continue, but they are "formatters". They tell Python to take the variable on the right, and put it in to replace the %s with its value. - I don't get it, what is a "formatter"? [silence TV has been on all along, but volume not too high] [Nina gives her mother a big hug] Creating a custom desktop color in Mac OS X Lion is an excellent way to personalize your computing experience and make your workspace truly your own. This guide will walk you through the process step-by-step, providing clear explanations and useful tips along the way. **Step 1: Prepare Your Workspace** Before diving into customization, ensure that your workspace is tidy and ready for changes. Close unnecessary applications, remove any clutter from your desktop, and save all open documents. This preparation allows you to focus solely on changing the desktop color without distractions or potential complications. **Step 2: Access System Preferences** Click on the Apple icon located at the top left corner of your screen. From the dropdown menu, select 'System Preferences.' Alternatively, press 'Command + Space' keys simultaneously to activate Spotlight search, then type 'System Preferences' and hit Enter. **Step 3: Open Desktop & Screen Saver Settings** In the System Preferences window, locate and click on the 'Desktop & Screen Saver' icon (it resembles a wallpaper with two arrows). This action opens the settings where you can modify your desktop background and screensaver behavior. **Step 4: Choose Solid Colors Option** On the left side of the Desktop & Screen Saver window, there are several categories listed under 'Desktop Pictures'. Click on the 'Solid Colors' option situated towards the bottom. Selecting this category reveals various colored rectangles representing different solid colors available as backgrounds. **Step 5: Pick Your Desired Color** Browse through the array of colors until you find one that suits your taste. If none of the provided options match what you have in mind, don't worry! You can create a custom color instead. To do so, click on the small square button displaying a rainbow gradient next to the word 'Color,' which opens the Colors panel. **Key Tips:** - Use the sliders below the spectrum bar to adjust hue, saturation, and brightness according to your preference. - The checkbox labeled 'Transparency and Opacity' enables you to control the intensity of the chosen color if you wish to maintain some visibility of your files on the desktop. **Step 6: Save Your Custom Color** Once satisfied with your selection, click on the 'OK' button at the bottom right corner of the Colors panel. The new custom color now appears among the other presets in the Solid Colors section. **Step 7: Apply the New Desktop Color** Simply click on your selected color - whether it's one of the defaults or your newly created custom color - to apply it as your new desktop background. The change takes effect immediately. **Step 8: Confirm and Enjoy** To confirm the successful application of your new desktop color, look at your desktop. It should be adorned with the shade you chose. Now that you've completed these steps, take a moment to appreciate your uniquely personalized workspace. By following this comprehensive tutorial, you've successfully created a custom desktop color in Mac OS X Lion. Feel free to explore more ways to further tailor your system to reflect your individual style and preferences. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV2/BR/morbr2.cha = = = One was two fifteen to three fifteen. Title: How to Become Cute Introduction: Becoming cute is all about embracing your unique qualities and presenting yourself in a way that exudes charm, appeal, and attractiveness. It involves both physical appearance and personal attitude. This guide will provide you with comprehensive steps to help you become cuter, complete with detailed explanations, key tips, and guidelines. Step 1: Maintain Good Personal Hygiene * Explanation: Cleanliness is crucial when striving to be cute. A well-groomed individual radiates freshness and vitality, which are essential components of cuteness. * Key Tips: + Take regular showers or baths (preferably daily) using mild soap or body wash. + Brush and floss your teeth at least twice a day, and consider using mouthwash for extra freshness. + Wear deodorant or antiperspirant to prevent body odor. + Keep your nails clean and trimmed. + Style your hair regularly, ensuring it's free from tangles and grease. Step 2: Dress Appropriately * Explanation: Clothing plays a significant role in projecting an adorable image. Select outfits that complement your body type, personality, and occasion. * Key Tips: + Opt for clothes made of soft, lightweight materials such as cotton or silk. + Choose pastel colors and delicate patterns like flowers or polka dots. + Accessorize with items like headbands, scarves, or bows. + Avoid overly revealing clothing; instead, aim for modest yet stylish attire. Step 3: Adopt a Cheerful Attitude * Explanation: Your mindset significantly influences your overall cuteness. By maintaining a positive and cheerful disposition, you can effortlessly captivate those around you. * Key Tips: + Smile genuinely and often. + Compliment others and express gratitude frequently. + Practice active listening when conversing with someone. One popular aspect of Animal Jam is collecting rare items. However, some people resort to scamming others to obtain these rarities. This guide will show you how to get rare items honestly and build your collection fairly. Step 1: Play Games and Earn Gems Time Needed: Ongoing Importance: The primary way to earn gems (the main currency) in Animal Jam is by playing mini-games found throughout Jamaa. By consistently doing well in these games, you'll accumulate more gems that can be used to buy rare items from the Diamond Shop or other players. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Focus on mastering specific games to maximize gem earnings. * Log in daily for bonus gems. * Complete Daily Challenges for additional rewards. Step 2: Save Your Gems Time Needed: Ongoing Importance: Saving your hard-earned gems allows you to make larger purchases at once instead of spending them impulsively. This strategy increases your chances of acquiring better and rarer items over time. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Create a savings goal for yourself. * Avoid buying every item that catches your eye; think about its value before purchasing. * Keep track of sales events when prices might drop temporarily. Step 3: Participate in Events and Sales Time Needed: Occasional Importance: Special events like parties and holidays often feature exclusive limited-time items, which may become highly sought after later. Taking advantage of seasonal sales also gives you access to discounted rare items. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Stay updated on upcoming events through official Animal Jam social media channels. * Prioritize high-value event items based on their potential desirability. * Consider selling unwanted event items during future events to acquire new ones. Step 4: Trade Thoughtfully and Fairly Time Needed: As needed Importance: Trading is essential for expanding your inventory while getting rid of duplicate or less valuable items. Be sure always to trade fairly and avoid taking part in any scams. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Research common trading values using reputable sources such as Animal Jam guides or forums. * Never agree to trades involving personal information, real money, or third-party websites outside of Animal Jam. * Always double-check the item being traded before confirming the exchange. Step 5: Engage with the Community Time Needed: Regularly Importance: Building relationships within the community can open up opportunities for finding rare items via trades or gifts. Additionally, joining groups dedicated to collecting certain types of rares lets you learn from experienced collectors. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Join chat rooms related to rare items or collections. * Attend player-hosted gatherings featuring giveaways or trades. * Network with fellow collectors who share similar interests. By following this comprehensive guide, you're now equipped with the knowledge and skills necessary to grow your rare item collection ethically and responsibly. Remember, patience and persistence are crucial elements of success in building a valuable inventory in Animal Jam! You can ask me when I come and talk with you, okay? Ann invested a certain sum of money in a bank that paid simple interest. The amount grew to $240 at the end of 2 years. She waited for another 3 years and got a final amount of $300. What was the principal amount that she invested at the beginning? - $ 200 - $ 150 - $ 210 - $ 175 - Insufficient data The correct choice is (A) and the correct answer is $ 200 The sum grew to $240 at the end of 2 years. At the end of another 3 years, the sum grew to $ 300. i.e. in 3 years, the sum grew by $ 60. Therefore, each year, it grew by $ 20. Sum at the end of 2 years = $ 240 Sum grew by $ 20 each year. Hence, in the first 2 years, sum grew by 2 * 20 = $ 40. Therefore, sum at the beginning of the period = Sum at the end of 2 years - $40 = $ 240 - $ 40 = $ 200. Ever wanted to know how to multiply other than using a calculator? Well we have put together a brilliant trick that is simple to learn and works every time! We are going to use lines to calculate 21 X 32 First we look at the left part of the sum, 21. The first digit, 2 needs to be represented in line format. How? Well we draw two lines diagonally, like so: If it was 31 then there would have been three lines. Ok, so now we need to draw a line for the 1 of 21: So now we have the 21 part of the sum sorted out. Next we concentrate on the 32 part of the sum. Not surprisingly we need to draw lines in the opposite direction to the way 21 was drawn. Let's look at the 3 of 31: And finally we add the lines for the 2 of 32: Right, so we have all the work done that is necessary to calculate the answer to 21 X 32. But how do we know what to do next? Easy. Break the diagram above into three sections, the first section is the left part of the diagram, the 2nd section is the top and bottom intersection and the third is the right intersection. The diagram below should clarify this for you: Looking at part one circled above, you can see 6 red dots. These dots are there when one line crosses over another one. You can see that there are 6 red dots, this makes up the first part of the answer. Looking at section 2 there are four red dots at the top section and three red dots at the bottom section. Add them together totalling 7. This makes up the second part of our answer. Finally, part three. There are two red dots cutting the intersection. The third part of the answer is two. Putting all these answers together to get 672. So our answer 21 X 32 = 672 So how did you find it? This method will also work for larger numbers too. Try 123 X 321 and see how you fair. So what about when the sum gets bigger? How do we tackle it? Well let's take an example of 42 X 37, and having drawn in all the lines we should have an image something like this: Looking at part one on the left, how many red dots are there? I count 12. Part two, how many red lines do you see? I count 34 (don't worry, this won't make it any more difficult). And finally, part three. How many red dots? 14. so, put the numbers together, in order and you get 12, 34, 14 But, this isn't the answer yet. We need to have only single digits in each part and to do this you add the tens part to the left hand side. The tens part, for example would be the three of 34 or the 1 of 14. So to make these numbers be on their own its easier to work from the right to left. Move the 1 of the 14 to the left, adding the 1 to 34 giving 35. In this movement we have just got our last digit in the answer, 4. So now our sum looks like this: 12, 35, 4 Next, move the three of 35 across to the left, and adding it to the 12, giving: 15, 5, 4 And finally, notice that we cannot add the 1 of 15 to anything on the left hand side, so we know we have reached the end of the calculation. All we need to do now is remove the commas and we have our answer. So 42 X 37 = 1554 And that's how you multiply numbers using lines! When Archbishop of Canterbury Hubert Walter died on 13 July 1205, John became involved in a dispute with Pope Innocent III. The Canterbury Cathedral Chapter claimed the sole right to elect Hubert's successor, and favoured Reginald, a candidate out of their midst. However, both the English bishops and the King wanted someone else to have this powerful office. The king wanted John de Gray, one of his own men. When their dispute could not be settled, the Chapter secretly elected one of their members as Archbishop. A second election imposed by John resulted in another nominee. When they both appeared in the Vatican, Innocent disavowed (rejected) both elections, and his candidate, Stephen Langton, was elected despite the objections of John's observers. John was supported in his position by the English barons and many of the English bishops, and refused to accept Langton. What kind of cheese does that look like? Why he put sharp things on him? Who Lives Here? In this activity, children will discuss the different kinds of housing that different pets require. Then they will create Who Lives Here? riddle cards. WHAT YOU NEED - Blank riddle cards (print and copy) - Construction paper - Crayons, colored pencils, or magic markers WHAT TO DO - Ask children what pets they have at home. List their responses in a column on chart paper. (Children without pets may suggest a pet they would like to have.) Then discuss with children the special housing needs of the pets on the list. Do they need a cage, a tank, a small house of their own? Why? List each pet's "house" alongside its name on the chart paper. - Next, tell children they are going to make riddle cards. Pass out the blank riddle cards and have children fold them in half (so the question appears on the front of the card at the top). Then read aloud with children the question on the front of the card. Explain that they are going to draw a picture of a pet "house" on the front of the card and a picture of the pet that lives in that house on the inside of the card. Encourage them not to tell what pet they are drawing, since they want people to guess the answer to the question, Who lives here? - When children are done drawing, they can exchange cards with one another and try to guess what pet is inside. - Post the finished riddle cards on a hallway bulletin board and invite other classes to guess the answers. - Have children make a set of Who Lives Here? flash cards with a pet house on the front and the pet on the back. Then divide the class into two teams and play a game of Who Lives here?, giving each team a point for a correct answer. - If you have children who are able to write, have them label their pictures on the inside of the card. Some elements only have a single isotope that is stable. For example, fluorine-19 (F) is the only stable isotope of fluorine. The other isotopes of fluorine decay rapidly so they are not found in nature. Other elements have many stable isotopes. For example, xenon has seven stable isotopes. It also has two isotopes that decay very slowly that are found in nature. The largest number of stable isotopes for any element is ten, for the element tin. Some elements do not have any stable isotopes, such as curium. These only exist on Earth because they are created in nuclear reactors, nuclear explosions, or particle accelerators. [D murmurs as S speaks; 5 sec silence] Creating a Healthy Work from Home Area Step 1: Choose a Dedicated Space Designate a specific area in your home as your workspace. This separation between work and personal life will help you focus better during working hours and relax when off duty. A spare room or quiet corner can serve this purpose well. If possible, avoid areas with high foot traffic, noise, or distractions like televisions or gaming consoles. Step 2: Invest in an Ergonomic Chair Sitting for prolonged periods can lead to back pain and poor posture. An ergonomic chair provides adequate lumbar support, adjustable height, and armrests that promote good sitting habits. By investing in such a chair, you're taking preventive measures against potential health issues related to improper seating. Key Tip: Regularly stand up, stretch, and move around every half hour to keep blood circulating and muscles active. Step 3: Adjust Your Desk Height Your desk should be at a comfortable height where your arms are parallel to the floor while typing. Ideally, your eyes should align with the top third of your computer screen to reduce strain on your neck. If necessary, use books or boxes to raise your monitor or laptop until it reaches the correct position. Guideline: The recommended distance between your face and the screen is about an arm's length away. Step 4: Procure Necessary Equipment Equip yourself with essential office supplies including a mouse, keyboard, printer, etc., based on your profession. Using these devices instead of relying solely on laptops can minimize awkward body positions and thus decrease chances of discomfort or injury. Step 5: Optimize Lighting Conditions Natural light is ideal but ensure there's no glare on your screen which could cause eye strain. Use blinds if direct sunlight becomes too intense. For artificial lighting, opt for LED bulbs that mimic natural daylight. Position lights so they illuminate your workspace evenly without casting harsh shadows. Step 6: Manage Cables & Wires Tangled cords pose tripping hazards and create visual clutter. Organize them using cable ties, cord covers, or wire organizers. Keep power strips off the floor to prevent accidents. Less clutter leads to less stress! Step 7: Personalize Your Space Add plants, artwork, or other items that make you feel happy and inspired. Psychologically, having a pleasant environment increases productivity and job satisfaction. Just remember not to overdo it; maintain enough space for comfort and ease of movement. Step 8: Maintain Cleanliness Regularly clean your workspace to remove dust, crumbs, and germs. Disinfect surfaces regularly, especially during flu season. Not only does cleanliness improve hygiene, but also reduces allergens contributing to respiratory problems. Step 9: Set Boundaries Communicate clearly with family members or housemates about your work schedule and need for privacy during certain hours. Establishing boundaries prevents interruptions helping you stay focused and productive. In conclusion, creating a healthy work-from-home area involves careful planning and consideration. Prioritizing ergonomics, lighting, organization, personalization, and cleanliness fosters both physical and mental wellbeing, ultimately leading to increased efficiency and enjoyment of remote work. Calculating Relative Standard Deviation (RSD) in Microsoft Excel is an efficient way to determine the precision and reproducibility of a set of data. This value represents the ratio of standard deviation to the mean, expressed as a percentage. By following these steps, you can accurately calculate RSD using Excel's built-in functions: 1. **Organize your dataset**: Before starting any calculations, ensure that your data is well organized with all values pertaining to one variable placed together in a column or row. Proper organization will help prevent errors during calculation and make subsequent analysis easier. *Key tip*: Use headings for each category to clearly identify what type of data they represent. 2. **Calculate Mean**: The first step towards calculating RSD is determining the arithmetic mean of your dataset. You can do this by selecting the cell where you want the result displayed, then typing `=AVERAGE(` followed by the range containing your data. For instance, if your data spans from cells A2 through A50, enter `=AVERAGE(A2:A50)`. Press Enter to get the average. 3. **Compute Standard Deviation**: Next, compute the standard deviation of your dataset. Click on the cell where you wish to display the SD value, input `=STDEV.S(` into the formula bar, and insert the same range used earlier. Complete the function with a closing parenthesis, so it looks like this: `=STDEV.S(A2:A50)` . *Guideline*: If dealing with a sample size less than 30, replace `STDEV.S()` with `STDEV.P()`. 4. **Find Relative Standard Deviation (RSD)**: Now we need to express the standard deviation relative to the mean. To accomplish this, select the cell for the final output, start a new equation with `=(`, click on the cell holding the SD value, add a division sign `/`, click on the cell containing the mean, close off the bracket `)`, then multiply by 100%: `=(B2/B1)*100%`. Remember to adjust ranges accordingly based on their locations within your spreadsheet. 5. **Format Output**: Format the resulting percentage as required - either decimal or fractional representation according to your needs. Right click on the cell -> 'Format Cells', choose Percentage under Category tab, and specify number of decimals. 6. **Review & Interpret Results**: Review your calculated RSD; lower percentages indicate higher reliability while larger ones suggest greater variability among measurements. Always compare results against acceptable standards or previous sets of similar experiments. Remembering these procedures enables quick computation of RSD whenever needed, providing valuable insights about the consistency and dependability of experimental outcomes! [Chi is trying to put another block on top of the arch] For this exercise you'll need your picture frame and a washable (non-permanent) marker. This time we'll be tracing a picture in the frame. These pictures show a foreshortened hand, which many people have trouble drawing. And why do people have trouble drawing foreshortened objects? Say it with me - "Your logical mind says it doesn't know how." Because the object seems to get distorted in the foreshortening, your logical mind freaks out and sees that the object you want to draw doesn't conform to the "symbol" that it knows. Tracing is something that your logical mind will tolerate for some reason, so let's do it. Tracing is basically copying. You have the thing you want to trace and you put a piece of paper right on top of it and copy it line for line. The difference here is that we'll be tracing a three dimensional object rather than a two start this exercise, print out one of the photos of hands on this page. Now put the print in your frame (cut it to size). Now, using your washable marker, trace the photo of the hand right on the glass of your frame. Trace the contour of the hand and trace the folds of skin where the fingers are bending. Trace every line you see. When you're done, take the hand photo out of the frame and look at your drawing. You could put a blank sheet of paper in the frame to better see your work. Did you get all the foreshortened fingers? Looks good, huh? So now you know you can trace. Put your hand under the frame and trace your hand onto the glass. my traced hand. this part is tougher, and the real "meat" of this exercise. CAREFULLY!!! wash the drawing off of the glass of your frame and secure the glass back in the frame without a picture or the backing so you just have the frame and the glass. Place the frame on top of your non-drawing hand. Curl your fingers so you get a little bit of foreshortening but keep your hand flat enough so the frame won't slide around. Now, using your drawing hand, trace your own hand on the glass just like you did with the photo of the hand. Again, trace all the lines you see. It's a bit more difficult because you're now dealing with a truly three-dimensional object and not a photo, but I know you can do it. You're going to have to keep your point of view the same all the time and you might want to close one eye. Try to remember how you drew the foreshortened fingers of the photo and draw your fingers in the same way, with the same types of lines and curves. you do? Pretty fun and challenging, huh? If the drawing of your own hand came out anything like the photo of the hand, you really have got a handle on drawing. Now, all you need to do is draw a subject when it's not right under your drawing surface and draw rather than trace. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Parent Child/SLI/PC-g/gal30.cha = = = Yah he definitely knew most of them. Title: How to Connect a Samsung Galaxy S4 to a Personal Computer Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through connecting your Samsung Galaxy S4 to a personal computer (PC). By following these steps, you'll be able to transfer files, sync data, and charge your phone using your PC. This process can help you manage your media, back up important information, and free up space on your device. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Make sure both devices have enough battery life before starting the connection process. * Keep your USB cable handy; this is the primary tool needed to establish the connection between your phone and PC. * Familiarize yourself with your phone and PC settings related to connections and file transfers. 1. Prepare Your Devices: Gather necessary items - your Samsung Galaxy S4, USB cable, and an available USB port or USB hub on your PC. Charge your devices if their batteries are low. 2. Enable Developer Options: On your Galaxy S4, navigate to Settings > About Phone > Software Information. Tap 'Build Number' seven times until you see the message "You are now a developer!" Return to the previous menu, where 'Developer options' should now appear as an option below 'About Phone'. 3. Turn On USB Debugging: In the newly-revealed 'Developer options', scroll down and toggle ON 'USB debugging'. Confirm any prompts that may appear by tapping 'OK'. Enabling this feature allows advanced functionality when connecting via USB. 4. Check Connection Mode: Connect your Galaxy S4 to your PC using the provided USB cable. A notification will appear at the top of the screen labeled 'Connected as a ' Selecting this will reveal various connectivity modes like MTP (Media Transfer Protocol) or PTP (Picture Transfer Protocol). Choose either one depending on your needs - we recommend selecting MTP for general file management purposes. 5. Install Necessary Drivers: Ensure your PC has installed the appropriate drivers for your Galaxy S4 model. If prompted during the connection process, follow the installation wizard steps. Otherwise, download them manually from Samsung's official website based on your specific model number and operating system type. 6. Access Device Storage: After successful installation, open 'My Computer' or 'This PC' on Windows, or double-click on your device under 'Devices' section in macOS Finder. You should now see your connected phone listed along with its internal storage and external SD card slot if applicable. 7. Manage Files: Now you can freely move, copy, paste, delete, rename, or organize files just like working with other folders on your computer. Remember to safely eject or disconnect your phone once done transferring files to prevent potential data corruption issues. 8. Disconnect Safely: When finished managing files, right-click on your phone icon within 'My Computer' or 'Finder' window and select 'Eject' or 'Safely Remove Hardware', respectively. Wait for confirmation messages then unplug the USB cable from both ends. 9. Revert Changes: After completing all tasks, disable USB debugging mode on your phone by navigating back to Settings > Developer options and toggling off 'USB debugging'. Also consider hiding Developer options again by going back to Settings > About Phone > Software Information and tapping 'Build number' five times quickly. Conclusion: By connecting your Samsung Galaxy S4 to a PC, you gain enhanced control over managing multimedia content, apps, documents, and backup procedures. Understanding these steps ensures smooth integration between mobile and desktop environments while maximizing productivity. Or Joseph, and I'll help you, okay? You can help your child learn to connect to others. Take time to: Give your child your full attention sometime each day. Follow her lead in play. This tells her that her ideas are valuable. Notice what she's doing. "You have already put together half of that puzzle." This tells her you find her projects important. Deal with her emotions. Talk with her about them-"I can see you're angry right now. How can we get these angry feelings out?" Work through changes together. Preschool children like routine-it makes them feel safe and comfortable. You can't always predict a big change. But when you can-if you're expecting a new baby, or planning a trip-prepare your child. Read her books about the big event or act it out with puppets or dolls. Let her know you will still love her and be there for her. Arrange for her to play with other children. At first, you may have to be right next to the children to help sharing and taking turns. Later, you can back off as they learn to work things out on their own. Title: "Exploring Linear Algebra through Everyday Examples" Have you ever played with building blocks or arranged books on a shelf? Then you have already started learning some basic concepts of linear algebra! At its core, linear algebra deals with organizing information into tables (matrices) and understanding relationships between different pieces of data. Let's explore this exciting field together using easy-to-understand examples. **Linear Systems of Equations:** Imagine solving a puzzle where you need to find the values of x, y, and z that satisfy three equations simultaneously. Using elimination methods like adding or subtracting one equation from another, we can simplify these equations step by step until we get a unique solution or discover there are multiple solutions or no solution at all. This concept relates to real-life problems like balancing chemical reactions or allocating resources efficiently. **Vectors and Vector Spaces:** Picture yourself moving along two perpendicular axes - left and right, forward and backward. Each movement can be represented as a vector, which has both magnitude (how far you move) and direction (which way). A set of such compatible vectors form a vector space. Understanding their properties helps us analyze complex systems more easily, just like finding shortcuts while navigating city streets. **Matrix Representations:** Now let's take our previous example further. Instead of dealing with individual variables, imagine working with groups of them organized neatly into matrices. These rectangular arrays allow us to perform operations on entire collections of numbers at once, saving time when tackling larger problems, similar to using spreadsheets for data analysis. **Transformations:** Think about flipping open a book. You change your perspective but keep reading without any confusion because words don't jumble up during this transformation. Similarly, linear transformations involve changing the position, size, or orientation of geometric shapes while preserving certain properties. They play crucial roles in computer graphics, physics simulations, and even video games! **Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors:** Sometimes when we apply a transformation, specific directions remain unchanged. Those special directions are called eigenvectors, and their scaling factors under transformation are known as eigenvalues. Try spinning a top; it rotates around its vertical axis (eigenvector), maintaining stability despite the spin (eigenvalue). Identifying these pairs makes mathematical models more manageable across various fields, including engineering dynamics and quantum mechanics. So here you go! With these fun examples, you now understand key principles behind applied linear algebra. By practicing problem-solving skills through engaging activities, who knows-maybe one day YOU could become a pioneer applying linear algebra to make groundbreaking advancements! Some teeth have cracks that are too small to show up on X-rays. Sometimes the cracks are under the gum. These small cracks are known as "cracked tooth syndrome." Some people grind or clench their teeth. These people may be more likely to have cracked tooth syndrome. Sometimes, the way a person's teeth come together can put too much pressure on one tooth. This can cause the teeth to crack. Teeth with large fillings may be more likely to crack. Teeth that have undergone root canal treatment are weaker than other teeth and also may be more likely to crack. People with one cracked tooth are more likely to have others, either at the same time or in the future. The tooth may hurt sometimes when you bite or chew. The sensitivity or pain can be mild or intense. It may last a brief time or a long time. It may be painful only when you eat certain foods or when you bite in a specific way. You will not feel a constant ache, as you would if you had a cavity or abscess. The tooth may be more sensitive to cold temperatures. If the crack gets bigger, a piece of the tooth may break off. You may also develop an infection. This can happen in the gum around the fractured tooth. You may notice a pimple-like bump on the gum near the tooth. Pus may drain from the pimple. This is known as a fistula. Many people with cracked tooth syndrome have symptoms for months. Cracked tooth syndrome is one of the most difficult dental problems to diagnose because the pain is not predictable. Your dentist may refer you to an endodontist (root canal specialist). Your dentist will examine your mouth and teeth, focusing on the tooth in question. He or she may use a sharp instrument called an explorer to feel for cracks in the tooth. Your dentist also will look at the gums around the tooth. You may have X-rays taken. But X-rays often do not show the crack. Your dentist may use special tools to test the tooth. One tool looks like a toothbrush without bristles. It fits over one part of the tooth at a time as you bite down. If you feel pain, the part of the tooth being tested most likely has a crack in it. Your dentist may shine a very bright light on the tooth. He or she may stain it with a special dye. If the tooth already has a filling or crown, your dentist may remove it in order to see the tooth better. How long symptoms last depends on how quickly a cracked tooth can be diagnosed. Even after a crack is found, treatment may not completely relieve the symptoms. If you grind or clench your teeth, talk to your dentist about treatment. Grinding can increase your risk of cracked tooth syndrome. Treatments for cracked tooth syndrome do not always relieve the symptoms. Treatment depends on: Sometimes a crack affects one or more cusps of a tooth. These are the highest points of the tooth. In this case, the tooth may be fixed with a crown, also known as a cap. Some cracks affect the pulp: the center of the tooth, where the nerves and blood vessels are. In that case, the tooth will need root canal treatment. About 20% of teeth with cracked tooth syndrome will need root canals. After a root canal, the tooth will no longer be sensitive to temperature, but it still will respond to pressure. If you felt pain before the root canal, you may still feel some pain afterward. It probably will not be as intense or as frequent, but it may still occur. In some cases, the tooth may need to be removed. Some cracks affect the root of the tooth in the jaw. There's no way to fix this type of crack. If your tooth is removed, you can have it replaced with an implant or a bridge. Your dentist can make a night guard (a plastic bite piece) to prevent you from grinding your teeth. This will relieve the pain from grinding. For some people it can stop tooth sensitivity. The night guard can be worn during sleep. It also can be worn at other times if clenching or grinding happens during waking hours. When To Call a Professional If you feel pain when you bite or chew, contact your dental office. Treatment of cracked tooth syndrome is not always successful. Your dentist should talk with you about what might happen. In some people, a crown will fix the problem. In others, root canal treatment solves the problem. Some people continue to have occasional symptoms after treatment. They may need to have the tooth taken out. 2002-2013 Aetna, Inc.
