Floor Press: A Comprehensive Step-by-Step Guide The floor press is an upper body exercise that targets your chest, shoulders, and triceps muscles. It's a great alternative to bench pressing if you don't have access to a bench or weights. This guide will provide you with an in-depth explanation of how to perform the floor press correctly and safely, as well as some key tips and guidelines. **Things You'll Need:** * A flat surface (e.g., gym mat, carpeted area) * A barbell or dumbbells (optional) **Step 1: Preparation** Find a comfortable spot on the floor where you can lay down without any obstructions. If using weights, ensure they are within easy reach. Clear away any objects that might interfere with your movement during the exercise. **Why this step matters:** Proper preparation prevents injuries by ensuring there are no distractions or hazards while performing the exercise. **Step 2: Set Up Your Position** Lie flat on your back with your legs extended straight out in front of you. Keep your feet together or hip-width apart for stability. Bend your knees slightly if needed for comfort. Place your hands shoulder-width apart on the ground or hold onto the barbell/dumbbells if using them. **Why this step matters:** Correct positioning ensures proper alignment of your body, engaging the targeted muscle groups effectively. **Step 3: Engage Core Muscles** Before lifting, engage your core muscles by drawing your belly button towards your spine. This action stabilizes your lower back and protects it from injury. **Why this step matters:** Engaging your core maintains spinal integrity throughout the exercise, reducing risk of injury. **Step 4: Lift the Weight(s)** Press the weight(s) upwards until your arms are fully extended but not locked at the elbows. Inhale deeply as you lift. Make sure to keep your wrists neutral and avoid letting them bend backwards or sideways. **Why this step matters:** Proper form when lifting reduces strain on joints and maximizes muscle engagement. **Step 5: Lower the Weight(s)** Slowly bring the weight(s) back down towards your chest, exhaling as you do so. Stop just before the weights touch your chest to maintain tension in the muscles. **Why this step matters:** Controlled descent builds strength and endurance, and minimizes impact on joints. **Step 6: Repeat** Perform multiple repetitions according to your fitness level and goals. Remember to breathe naturally - inhale during the upward motion and exhale during the downward motion. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Start light: If you're new to floor pressing, start with lighter weights or even bodyweight only. Gradually increase the load once you feel confident and strong enough. * Focus on form: Always prioritize correct form over heavier loads. Poor form can lead to injuries and hinder progress. * Vary width: Changing the distance between your hands alters which part of the chest is worked. Narrower grips target more inner chest, wider grips hit outer chest. * Use a spotter: Whenever possible, especially when using heavy weights, have someone nearby who can assist if necessary. * Progress gradually: Increase weight, reps, or sets incrementally to allow your body time to adapt and grow stronger. By following these steps and guidelines, you'll be able to incorporate effective and safe floor presses into your workout routine. Happy training! Many polymer molecules are like "chains" where the monomer units are the links. Polymer molecules can be straight-chain, have "branching" from the main chain, or "cross-linking" between chains. As an example of cross-linking, sulfhydryl (-S-H) groups in two cysteine amino acid units in polypeptide chains can bond together to make a disulfide bridge (-S-S-) joining the chains together. Read the full transcript Learn about Ordering Fractions Now, let's learn about ordering fractions in an order. So we're supposed to order fractions 3/7, 3/4, and 1/4, from the least to the greatest. So, let's write these down; 3/7, 3/4 and 1/4. We need to figure out which one is the lowest and in value and which one is the highest in value. As we can see, we have different denominators. So they are unlike fractions. So the first thing I've got to do is convert them to like fractions. So if I want to convert them to like fractions, what do I do? I've got to calculate the lowest common multiple of seven, four, and four. So seven, four, and four are all the prime factorization of seven is seven, four is two times two, and four is two times two. So the LCM becomes 7x2x2=28. So the LCM is 28. Now that I have that, we need to rewrite these three fractions so that the denominator is 28. So 3/7 will become, multiplied both sides by 4, will become 12/28. The goal is to get the denominator to 28, right. 3/4 will become, I'll have to multiply it by 7 on both sides to get 21/28. And the last one, 1/4 will become, multiply it by 7 again, 7/28. So the three fractions are 12/28, 21/28, and 7/28. So it becomes relatively easy to compare them. This is the least in value because the numerator is all what I'm looking at. This is the greatest in value and this is the middle. So the correct order is 3, 1, and 2. So the correct order will be, if I look at the rational one, 1/4, 3/7, and 3/4. So the final answer is 1/4, 3/7, and 3/4. 1/4 is less than 3/7 which is less than 3/4. 1/4 is less than 3/7, and less than 3/4. So quickly recapping what we've learned, if we're given three fractions and we have to order them, we compute their LCM, make them all like fractions, and then compare the numerators. We get the values, 1/4 is less than 3/7 is less than 3/4. Ya got the wrong end of the pencil. Do you want to look at this at the beach? So that would be well over about just was two years ago now. Later, Schoenberg invented a new system for writing music called twelve-tone system. Music written with the twelve-tone system sounds strange to some, but is mathematical in nature, often making sense only after careful study. Pure twelve-tone music was popular among academics in the fifties and sixties, but some composers such as Benjamin Britten use it today, when it is necessary to get a certain feel. You can make your own compass to answer these questions. - Modeling clay - Drinking glass - 2 bamboo skewers - Rubber band - 3 small bar magnets* or 1 long bar magnet - Red nail polish - Thread, about 15-cm piece - Graph paper - Black, red, and green markers - Map or compass (optional) - Put a lump of modeling clay in a drinking glass. Stick a bamboo skewer upright in it. - Put another skewer across the top of the glass. Loop the rubber band around both skewers several times so they stay together, but the one resting on the drinking glass edge can still turn freely. - Put the 3 bar magnets together so they make one long magnet (or use one long bar magnet). Put a dot of red nail polish at one end so you know which end is which. - Tie one end of the thread around the middle of the magnet. Tie the other end to the skewer. The magnet should hang level and balanced, while still able to swing freely, like this: - Tape a sheet of graph paper to the table. - Set the glass on the paper, with its base centered at the left edge of the paper, like this: - With the black marker, draw around the base of the glass. Keep the glass in this circle throughout the experiment. - Turn the skewer so it points in any direction over the paper. With the black marker held straight down beside the skewer, make two marks on the paper: one near the glass and one near the tip of the skewer. - With the ruler, connect the two black dots. This line shows where you pointed the skewer. - Let the magnet swing freely. When it stops, make a red mark on the graph paper under the red-dotted end of the magnet. Make a green mark directly beneath the other end. Use the ruler and the red marker to connect the points with a straight line. Make an arrow point on the red dot end of the line, like this: - Repeat steps 8-10 several times more, pointing the skewer in a different direction each time.You should end up with a black line and a red arrow for every trial of the experiment. - Try to figure out where your red arrows are pointing. Use a map or compass for help if you need to. Nothing here is dangerous, but a magnet can harm your compass. Don't keep them close together for long. Perform steps 1-4 of the "Go" procedure. Move the skewer to different places. Notice where the reddotted end of the magnet points with each trial. Modify the "Go" procedure to build a portable, build-your-own compass that will work anywhere and read direction to within a 10 ( 5 at any point on the 360 in a circle) margin of accuracy. Show Your Results For "Go Easy," display your apparatus and present a poster that shows how and why it works as a compass. For "Go," display your graph paper records, showing the orientation of the skewer and the direction the magnet pointed in each of your trials. For "Go Far," display your homemade compass and present experimental evidence to demonstrate that you achieved the 10 accuracy required. Tips and Tricks - If your magnet is taking a long time to settle, it's okay to slow it with your hand. Just let go before you determine where it is pointing. - If your magnet swings back and forth slightly, draw the line in the middle that best represents its direction. - Keep your setup at a distance from iron and steel objects. Title: How to Foster Kittens: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Fostering kittens can be an incredibly rewarding experience. Not only do you get to help young cats in need, but you also contribute to their development and socialization before they find their forever homes. This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about fostering kittens, from preparation to care and eventual adoption or return to a shelter. **Section 1: Preparation** Step 1: Contact Your Local Shelter or Rescue Group Before getting started with foster parenting, reach out to local shelters or rescue groups that have established foster programs. Express your interest in becoming a foster parent, and ask what steps are necessary to begin the process. They may require background checks, home visits, or other precautions for the safety of both humans and animals involved. Key Tip: Make sure you understand if the organization covers all expenses related to caring for the kitten(s), such as food, litter, medical costs, etc., or if there's any financial responsibility on your part. Step 2: Create a Suitable Living Space Set up a safe area where the kittens can stay while they adjust to their new environment. This could be a spare room, bathroom, or even a large dog crate depending on available space. The area should be cat-proofed and include essential items like bedding, toys, food dishes, water bowls, and a litter box. Guideline: Keep this space warm (around 70 F - 80 F) since young kittens cannot regulate body temperature well. Also, ensure easy accessibility to the area for feeding, cleaning, and monitoring the kittens' health. **Section 2: Caring for Newborn Kittens** Step 3: Feeding Newborn Kittens If the mother cat is unavailable or unable to nurse her offspring, you might need to bottle feed them using kitten milk replacer (KMR). Follow these steps when bottle-feeding: 1. Hold the kitten upright against your body, supporting its head and neck gently. 2. Place the nipple of the bottle into the kitten's mouth at a slight angle so that it sucks naturally. Give the dinosaur the tea www. The first stop we make is a rack with um... [mother starts to look through the box of doll house furnishings] Title: How to Boot Your Mac From a CD or DVD (Mac OS X) Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of booting your Macintosh computer from a CD or DVD using the built-in startup manager feature. This can be useful when troubleshooting issues with your system software, installing new operating systems, running diagnostics, or accessing utilities that require starting up from an optical disc. By following these steps carefully, you'll ensure a smooth boot process without encountering any difficulties. Key Tips & Guidelines: 1. Use high-quality, officially licensed discs to avoid compatibility issues and potential damage to your hardware. 2. Keep track of your data before attempting this procedure since starting up from another device may temporarily change some settings or configurations. 3. Be patient during the boot process; allow your Mac sufficient time to recognize and load the contents of the disc. 4. If you face any problems, consult Apple Support or visit trusted online forums for assistance tailored to your specific model and situation. 5. Make sure your Mac supports starting up from an optical drive by checking its technical specifications. Most models released after 2006 should have no trouble doing so. Detailed Steps: Step 1: Preparation Gather all necessary materials, including the Mac computer, the CD or DVD you wish to start up from, and the original installation media if you plan on reinstalling the operating system. Confirm that the disc is free from dust, fingerprints, or scratches which might interfere with proper reading. Step 2: Insert Disc Insert the chosen CD or DVD into your Mac's optical drive tray slowly and gently until you hear a soft click indicating successful insertion. The drive mechanism should pull the disc inside automatically. Step 3: Restart Computer Choose "Restart" from the Apple menu located at the top left corner of your screen. Wait for your Mac to shut down completely, then proceed once it begins powering back on. Step 4: Access Startup Manager Immediately upon hearing the startup chime sound, press and hold the "Option" key on your keyboard. Continue holding it until you see the Startup Manager appear onscreen, displaying available devices to boot from as icons. Step 5: Select Optical Drive Locate the icon representing your CD/DVD drive within the Startup Manager interface. It usually appears as a gray hard disk labeled with the name of the inserted disc. Use the arrow keys on your keyboard or mouse cursor to navigate towards this icon. Step 6: Initiate Boot Process Press "Enter" or double-click the selected icon to commence booting from the CD/DVD. A progress bar will appear momentarily while your Mac reads the disc's content and prepares to launch the contained application. Step 7: Follow Onscreen Instructions Once loaded, adhere to any prompts provided by the software on the disc. These could include selecting language preferences, agreeing to license agreements, choosing installation locations, or setting up user accounts depending on what the disc contains. Conclusion: Congratulations! You've successfully booted your Mac from a CD or DVD. Now you can utilize the benefits offered by starting up from an alternative source, whether that means diagnosing issues, performing maintenance tasks, or simply enjoying multimedia entertainment. Always remember to eject the disc safely once finished by dragging its icon to the Trash bin or pressing the appropriate button on your keyboard or machine. The vast majority of dog bites are from a dog known to the child-his or her own pet, a neighbor's or friend's. You can help prevent this from happening to your child. Please discuss with him or her the appropriate way to behave around dogs. The following activity will help you and your child understand the difference between safe and potentially dangerous interactions with dogs. The following is a list of pledges that you can recite with your child: 1. I will not stare into a dog's eyes. 2. I will not tease dogs behind fences. 3. I will not go near dogs chained up in yards. 4. I will not touch a dog I see loose (off-leash) outside. 5. If I see a loose dog, I will tell an adult immediately. 6. I will not run and scream if a loose dog comes near me. 7. I will stand very still (like a tree), and will be very quiet if a dog comes near me. 8. I will not touch or play with a dog while he or she is eating. 9. I will not touch a dog when he or she is sleeping. 10. I will only pet a dog if I have received permission from the dog's owner. 11. Then I will ask permission of the dog by letting him sniff my closed hand. May I Pet the Dog? Help your child understand the difference between safe and potentially dangerous interactions with dogs. Creating a Colorful Spiral Image in Python This tutorial will guide you through creating a visually appealing spiral image using Python. We'll be utilizing the power of mathematical concepts like sine and cosine functions to generate our spiraling pattern. The final result will showcase an array of vibrant colors that blend seamlessly together. This project is suitable for both beginners looking to understand basic programming principles as well as more experienced programmers interested in diving into visual arts with code. **Step 1: Setting Up Your Environment** Firstly, ensure you have Python installed on your computer. If not, download it from the official website (<https://www.python.org/>). After installation, verify the successful setup by typing `python --version` or `python3 --version` in your terminal or command prompt. Next, install NumPy and Matplotlib libraries if they are not already present. These packages can be added via pip, which comes bundled with recent Python distributions. Now take the plate and the spoon on down to Mom. Creating custom greeting cards with Qoop.com is an excellent way to add a personal touch to any occasion. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to create your own unique greeting cards using this online platform. **Step 1: Visit Qoop.com** First, open your web browser and navigate to <https://www.qoop.com/>. By visiting their website, you can explore various products they offer, including photo books, calendars, phone cases, and more. However, our focus today is creating custom greeting cards. **Step 2: Select Greeting Cards** On the homepage, hover over the 'Products' tab at the top of the page and click on 'Greeting Cards'. You'll be redirected to a new page displaying different types of greeting cards such as birthday cards, thank-you cards, holiday cards, etc. Choose the category that suits your needs. **Step 3: Create Your Design** After selecting the card type, click on 'Create Now'. It will prompt you to either upload a design or use one of their pre-made templates. If you have designed something already, choose 'Upload Photo(s)' otherwise select 'Design Online' to access template options. *Key Tip*: Make sure your image resolution is high enough (at least 300 dpi) so that the printed version looks clear and crisp. Low-resolution images may appear pixelated when printed. **Step 4: Personalize Your Template** If you opted for designing online, pick a template according to your theme or event. Then personalize it by adding text, changing colors, moving elements around, etc., until it matches your vision. Remember to include all necessary information like names, dates, special messages, etc. *Guideline*: Keep readability in mind while typing out your message. Avoid using too many font styles or sizes within the same card; two complementary fonts are usually sufficient. Also, ensure there's enough contrast between your text color and background color for easy reading. **Step 5: Preview Your Card** Once satisfied with your creation, preview the card before proceeding to checkout. Most platforms allow you to see what the final product will look like both inside and outside. Check carefully for typos, alignment issues, and other potential problems. *Key Tip*: Don't forget to review both sides of the card since sometimes important details get overlooked, especially if you added content on the backside. **Step 6: Add Quantity & Details** Decide how many copies you want and fill in the required fields - recipient name, address, gift message, etc. Some services let you schedule delivery dates, which could be handy during busy seasons. **Step 7: Proceed to Checkout** When everything is set, go ahead and complete the purchase process. Enter shipping and payment info, then review your order summary before submitting. **Step 8: Wait Patiently** Now comes the waiting part! Depending on where you live, processing times, and shipping methods, receiving your custom greeting cards might take anywhere from a few days up to two weeks. *Key Tip*: Order early to avoid last-minute rushes and stress. Plus, ordering well in advance gives you time to redo anything should changes be needed. By following these steps meticulously, you're now equipped to create beautiful, professional-looking custom greeting cards perfect for gifting or sending heartfelt wishes! Title: How to Clean Dirty Coins: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Coins are subjected to daily wear and tear as they change hands or get buried in soil. Over time, coins can become dirty, tarnished, or even corroded. While some collectors prefer the natural patina that builds up on old coins, others might want to clean their coins to restore them to their former glory. However, cleaning coins is a delicate process; if done incorrectly, you could damage your coin's surface, lowering its value. This guide will walk you through various methods to clean dirty coins safely and effectively. Materials Needed: 1. Mild dish soap (Dawn, Ivory) 2. Lukewarm water 3. Soft-bristle toothbrush 4. Distilled water 5. White vinegar 6. Baking soda 7. Olive oil 8. Cotton swabs 9. Latex gloves 10. The bridge, which takes an hour to walk across, is guarded in the middle by a guard who wakes up every half hour and carries back anybody crossing the bridge back to where he or she came from. How long does it take for me to get to the other side? - What are the 10 types of people in the world? We welcome your feedback, comments and questions about this site or page. Please submit your feedback or enquiries via our Feedback page. 1. Baleen whales are some of the largest animals. a. Large size allows for protection from predators and is an advantage in species that compete for mates. b. As an animal increases in size, its ability to retain body heat also increases. This is due to a large area (body core) to produce metabolic heat surrounded by a relatively smaller surface area (the skin) to conduct that heat to the environment. Thus, large size is a metabolic advantage. c. On land, animals must support their weight. In the aquatic environment, water helps support an animal's body weight, allowing for the potential for greater size. d. Large size allows whales to take advantage of high food productivity and store that energy in the form of blubber. 2. The largest baleen whale species is the blue whale. Blue whales may be larger than any other living or extinct animal. a. Blue whales reach average lengths of 21 m (70 ft.), and the largest individuals alive today probably measure about 26 m (85 ft.). The record size for a blue whale--for a specimen taken during the whaling years--is 34 m (110 ft.). b. An average weight for an adult blue whale is about 64,348 kg (142,000 lb.). A record-size individual weighed 172,330 kg (380,000 lb.). 3. Pygmy right whales are among the smallest baleen whale species. Adult females reach lengths of 6.5 m (21 ft.), and adult males may reach 6.1 m (20 ft.). This species is rarely recorded, so details of its physical size are poorly documented. 4. In general, female baleen whales are about 5% longer than males of the same species. 5. Some species exhibit differences in sizes depending on the stock. a. Sei whales reach 20 m (65 ft.) in length in the southern hemisphere, 18.6 m (61 ft.) in the North Pacific, and 17.3 m (57 ft.) in the North Atlantic. b. In the northern hemisphere, baleen whales are usually slightly smaller than their southern hemisphere counterparts. All baleen whales show some variation of a characteristic fusiform (torpedo-shaped) body shape. This body shape reduces drag and is energy efficient for swimming. 1. Most baleen whales exhibit skin color variations of black and gray. 2. Some species exhibit some extent of countershading; the dorsal (top) surface is darker than the ventral (bottom) surface. This coloration, typical of many marine animals, provide camouflage. The result is that predators or prey do not see a contrast between the countershaded animal and the environment. a. Blue whales sometimes exhibit a yellowish ventral surface, a result of diatom growth. (Diatoms are one-celled algae.) b. Barnacles and whale lice give a gray whale its characteristic light gray color. 4. Some species, such as gray whales and sei whales, have white or faintly colored markings or scars. Scarring can be caused by barnacle growth, shark bites, killer whale teeth marks, or from natural pigmentation differences. 5. Blue whales are named for a steel blue-gray skin color. 6. Minke, humpback, bowhead, and fin whales are distinctively colored. a. Northern hemisphere Minke whales have a band of white across the otherwise black flipper. Individuals in the southern hemisphere may or may not have the flipper band. b. Humpback whales are black or gray except for their flippers and the undersides of their flukes, which are white. c. Bowhead whales are dark gray to black except for a white chin. d. A fin whale's head is asymmetrically colored. The right lower lip is white, and the rest of the head is black or gray. Fin whales also have a grayish white chevron-shaped mark on their backs. 1. The forelimbs of baleen whales are pectoral flippers. Pectoral flippers have all the skeletal elements of the forelimbs of terrestrial mammals, but they're foreshortened and modified into paddle-shaped appendages. In rorquals and gray whales, there are four digits instead of five; the thumb bones are not present. Pectoral flippers have all the skeletal elements of the forelimbs of terrestrial mammals, but they're foreshortened and modified into paddle-shaped appendages. 2. The skeletal elements are rigidly supported by connective tissue. Thick cartilage pads lie lengthwise between the bones. 3. Baleen whales use their pectoral flippers mainly to steer and, with the help of the flukes, to stop. 4. Humpback whales have pectoral flippers that are up to one third of their body length and are a contrasting white color. 1. Each lobe of the tail is called a fluke. 2. Flukes are flattened pads of tough, fibrous connective tissue, completely without bone or muscle. 3. Flukes are oriented horizontally. Longitudinal muscles of the back and caudal peduncle move the flukes up and down. 1. In baleen whales all traces of hind limbs have disappeared except for two reduced, rod-shaped pelvic bones that are buried deep in body muscle. 2. These reduced hind limbs are not connected to the vertebral column. 1. Rorqual whales and the pygmy right whale have a fin on top of the back called a dorsal fin. a. Humpback whales have a small dorsal fin located on a hump. The shape of the dorsal fin varies among the humpback whales. b. Other rorqual species have a falcate (backward-curving) dorsal fin, located about two-thirds of the way down the back. c. Dorsal fin size ranges from about 25 to 60 cm (1-2 ft.), depending on the species. 2. Right, bowhead, and gray whales have no dorsal fin. Gray whales have a dorsal hump followed by a series of bumps. 3. Like the flukes, the dorsal fin is made of dense, fibrous connective tissue, with no bones. 4. Dorsal fins are often scarred or marked. Photos of baleen whale dorsal fins can be used for photo-identification. 1. Right whales have huge heads-one-forth to one-third of the entire body length in adults. a. Their narrow elongated upper jaw bones arch upwards to house extremely long baleen. The lower jawline is deeply curved upwards in profile. b. Encrusting growths, called callosities, on the head, jaws, and blowhole areas of right whales are yellow-white. Accumulations of natural markings such as these aid researchers in photo-identification studies. 2. Rorquals have a broad, flat rostrum and a slightly curved jawline. Some species have ridges on the rostrum and a V-or U-shape to the tip of the snout. Humpback whales have several knobs on their heads. 3. Gray whales have a narrow head with a slight curve to the jawline. 4. The neck vertebrae are unfused in rorquals and gray whales, which allows for some flexibility at the head and neck. In right whales, all seven vertebrae are fused, and right whales are incapable of side-to-side head movement. a. Baleen whales have two blowholes (modified nasal openings) located on top of the head. b. Blowholes are covered by muscular flaps. These flaps provide a water-tight seal. There is a "splash guard" in front of the blowhole region in some species. This prevents water from entering the blowholes upon surfacing to breathe. c. Each blowhole leads to an air passage that leads to a baleen whale's trachea and then to its lungs. 1. Baleen is an adaptation for filter-feeding. In baleen whales, stiff plates of baleen grow down from the gums of the upper jaw. The baleen plates are arranged in rows that extend down each side of the mouth. a. Baleen is made of keratin (a protein that also composes hair and fingernails). It is strong, yet elastic. b. Baleen plates arise in the fetus as thickenings of skin on the upper jaw. At birth, baleen is soft and short, but it soon stiffens. c. Baleen grows throughout the whale's lifetime; the terminal end continually wears off. 2. The outer (facing outside) edge of each plate is smooth while the inner edge is frayed. The frayed edge of each plate intertwines to form a mat which functions as a strainer during filter feeding. 3. Gray whales have about 130 to 180 baleen plates on each side. Their baleen plates are about 5 to 25 cm (2-10 in.) long. 4. The right whale family, notably the bowhead whales, have the longest baleen. In bowheads, between about 230 and 360 plates on each side of the jaw reach lengths of 4 m (13 ft.). 5. Rorqual baleen arrangements range from the blue whale, with 260 to 400 plates that reach 91 cm (3 ft.) on each side, to the Minke whale, which has 230 to 360, 12 to 20 cm (5 to 8 in.) plates on each side. 6. Baleen plates range in color from black to yellow or white, depending on the species. 7. Baleen is sometimes referred to as "whalebone," and baleen whales are referred to as "whalebone whales." 8. Baleen whales retain tooth buds during the embryonic stage. These tooth buds disappear before birth. 1. Baleen whales have a smooth skin surface with no oil glands or pores. The epidermis is about 5 to 7 mm (.02-.03 in.) thick. 2. Many species have several hairs located on the snout, jaws, and chin. The lack of a fur coat is an important advantage to swimming: as an animal swims, smooth skin creates less drag than fur does. 3. Samples of skin and blubber have been collected by shooting a hollow dart into a whale's body. Such samples can provide vital genetic information. This sampling technique, called biopsy darting, does not harm the whale. 1. Rorqual, gray, and pygmy right whales have ventral throat grooves that extend from the throat to the flipper area or farther. These folds of skin and blubber expand the capacity of the mouth area during feeding. 2. Because excess skin folds into grooves when the whales isn't feeding, the overall body shape remains streamlined. 3. Members of the rorqual family have between 25 and 100 throat grooves, depending on the species. 4. The pygmy right whale has just two throat grooves, and the gray whale has between two and seven throat grooves. 5. The right whales and bowhead whales do not have throat grooves. Since the coronations in 1066 of both King Harold and William the Conqueror, coronations of English and British monarchs were held in the Abbey. Henry III was unable to be crowned in London when he first came to the throne because the French prince Louis (later Louis VIII) had taken control of the city. So the king was crowned in Gloucester Cathedral, but this coronation was deemed by the Pope to be improper, and a further coronation was held in the Abbey on 17 May 1220. The Archbishop of Canterbury is the cleric in the coronation ceremony. An orbit is a regular, repeating path that an object in space takes around another one. An object in an orbit is called a satellite. A satellite can be natural, like the moon, or human (or extraterrestrial?) -made. (This drawing is not a scale drawing) In our solar system, the Earth orbits the Sun, as do the other eight planets. They all travel on or near the orbital plane, an imaginary disk-shaped surface in space. All of the orbits are circular or elliptical in their shape. In addition to the planets' orbits, many planets have moons which are in orbit around them. What causes an orbit to happen? What is a satellite? What travels in an orbit? How do we put a spacecraft into orbit? Once a ship is in orbit, do we have to do anything to keep it there? How is an ellipse different from a circle? Are there orbits within orbits? What is the orbital plane? What are some kinds of orbits? What are the orbital lengths and distances of objects in our solar system? What could cause an orbit to fail? Title: How to Get Over a Fish's Death Introduction: Losing a pet fish can be an emotional experience, even if it might not seem as significant as losing other types of pets. Fish are often overlooked when it comes to grief, but they can still have a special place in our hearts. This guide will help you navigate through this difficult time with understanding, compassion, and acceptance. Step 1: Acknowledge Your Grief Recognize that your feelings are valid and important. It is okay to grieve over the loss of any living being, regardless of its size or species. Allow yourself to feel sadness, anger, denial, or whatever emotions come up during this process. Key Tip: Don't compare your pain to others; everyone processes loss differently. Give yourself permission to take all the time you need to work through your emotions. Step 2: Seek Support from Friends and Family Talk about your feelings with people who care about you. Sharing your thoughts and memories can provide comfort and make coping easier. You may find that some friends or family members had similar experiences and can offer valuable insights. Guideline: Be open-minded while listening to their advice, but remember that ultimately, you know what works best for you. Step 3: Create a Memorial Space Setting up a small memorial space can serve as a reminder of your beloved pet fish. Place items such as their tank, decorations, photos, or other mementos in a designated area where you can visit whenever you want. Key Tip: Consider writing a heartfelt letter or poem expressing your feelings towards your fish. Keep it near the memorial space so you can read it whenever you miss them. Step 4: Hold a Ceremony (Optional) Organizing a simple ceremony can bring closure and peace after the death of your fish. Light candles, play calming music, say a few words, or release balloons into the sky. Invite close friends and family members to join you in honoring your pet fish's life. Guideline: Make sure the ceremony reflects your personal beliefs and values. There is no right or wrong way to do this - trust your instincts. Step 5: Take Care of Any Remaining Fish If there are more fish left in the aquarium, continue providing excellent care for them. Maintain regular feeding schedules, water changes, and cleaning routines. Watching over remaining fish can help you focus on something positive amidst your grief. Key Tip: Spending extra time observing and interacting with your surviving fish can strengthen your bond with them. Step 6: Reflect on Your Experience With Your Pet Fish Think back on the joyful moments spent together, lessons learned, and growth experienced because of owning a pet fish. Appreciate the unique connection you shared with your finned companion. Guideline: Try journaling or drawing as a creative outlet to express these reflections. Step 7: Gradually Reintroduce Routines and Activities When ready, start incorporating daily activities again at your own pace. Resuming hobbies, seeing friends, and engaging in self-care practices can aid in healing and reestablishing normalcy. Key Tip: Remember that recovery isn't linear - allow yourself flexibility during this transition period. Conclusion: The passing of a pet fish can leave a void in one's life, but by acknowledging your grief, seeking support, creating meaningful tributes, caring for surviving fish, reflecting on cherished memories, and gradually reintegrating into everyday life, you can begin to heal and move forward with resilience. Everyone thought it was the most special thing in the whole universe. It was an original mark that nobody else had ever seen before. One day, a small rocket came flying by. The rocket went around the mark in space and started to make circles. Then it went around the mark one more time and made even more circles. Title: How to Be a Classy and Sophisticated Individual Introduction: Being classy and sophisticated is about more than just wearing expensive clothes or having refined tastes. It's an attitude that reflects confidence, grace, intelligence, and good manners. This guide will help you cultivate those qualities and become a true "Sleassy" individual who turns heads with their charm and elegance. Step 1: Develop Confidence * Explanation: A significant aspect of being classy involves carrying yourself with self-assurance and poise. When you believe in your worth and abilities, others are more likely to take notice and respect you as well. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Practice positive affirmations daily. + Set achievable goals and celebrate small victories. + Engage in activities that make you feel accomplished and proud. + Learn new skills or hobbies to expand your knowledge base and interests. Step 2: Improve Your Communication Skills * Explanation: Effective communication is essential when striving for sophistication. By learning how to articulate your thoughts clearly and listen actively, you demonstrate maturity and intellect. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Speak slowly and deliberately, using proper grammar and pronunciation. Title: How to Get a Protective Order in Texas Introduction: A protective order is a court order that can help protect you from further harm by someone who has assaulted or threatened you. This article will guide you through the process of obtaining a protective order in the state of Texas. It's important to note that while this guide aims to be as comprehensive as possible, it doesn't replace legal advice. Consulting with an attorney is always recommended. Step 1: Determine if You Qualify for a Protective Order In Texas, you may qualify for a protective order if the person against whom you are seeking protection (the respondent): * Is your family member, household member, or dating partner; OR * Has committed sexual assault, stalking, human trafficking, or continuous violence against you. Key Tip: If you're unsure whether you qualify, consult with a lawyer or contact a local domestic violence shelter or advocacy organization for guidance. Step 2: Prepare Your Application You need to fill out an Application for Protection Order form. You can obtain this form from the district clerk's office at your county courthouse or download it online from the website of the Texas Office of Court Administration. Be sure to complete all sections accurately and thoroughly. Guideline: Provide specific details about incidents of abuse or threats, including dates, locations, witnesses, injuries, etc., when filling out the application. Step 3: File Your Application Once completed, file your application with the district clerk's office during business hours. There is no filing fee for protective orders in Texas. After filing, you'll receive several copies of your signed application stamped by the court. Key Tip: Keep one copy for yourself, serve another on the respondent, and keep the original served back to the court. Step 4: Serve the Respondent The respondent must be officially informed of the hearing date and contents of the application. This is called service of process. Law enforcement officers or private process servers can do this. They cannot be served by mail or hand-delivered by you. Guideline: Make sure the server completes an Affidavit of Service once they have successfully served the respondent. Step 5: Attend the Hearing Appear before the judge at the scheduled time and place indicated on your application. Bring any evidence supporting your case such as photographs, medical records, police reports, witness statements, etc. The respondent will also have an opportunity to present their side. Key Tip: Arrive early, dress appropriately, behave respectfully, answer truthfully, and address the judge as 'Your Honor.' Step 6: Understand the Terms of the Protective Order If granted, read the protective order carefully to understand what behaviors are prohibited by the respondent. Common terms include staying away from certain places, having no contact with you directly or indirectly, attending counseling, paying child support, etc. Guideline: Always carry a certified copy of your protective order and know its expiration date. Step 7: Enforcement and Violation Reporting Should the respondent violate the protective order, call law enforcement immediately. Also, return to court near the end of the protective order period to request an extension if necessary. Remember, getting a protective order is a serious matter that impacts both parties involved. Always consider consulting with a lawyer to ensure you fully understand the implications and processes involved. Cha-shitsu often are very small. The guests (the people who go to the tea ceremony) sometimes eat food and drink special Japanese wine called sake. Before they drink the matcha (green tea) they eat something sweet. Title: Differentiate Between Types of Pallets in Australia Introduction: Pallets are an essential component in many industries, facilitating efficient handling, storage, and transportation of goods. However, with various types available, understanding their differences is crucial for optimal usage. This tutorial focuses on differentiating between pallet types commonly used in Australia. Step 1: Understand What a Pallet Is A pallet is a flat transport structure that supports goods in a stable fashion while being lifted by a forklift, jack, or crane. They come in standard sizes but can also be custom-made depending on specific needs. Key Tip: Always consider the weight capacity and dimensions when choosing a pallet type. Step 2: Recognize Stringer Pallets Stringer pallets consist of three or more parallel pieces of timber or plastic called stringers. These run along the length of the unit and are spaced evenly apart to form a grid where boards (called deckboards) are placed across to create a solid platform surface. Guideline: Look out for the visible stringers at the sides as they're characteristic of this design. Step 3: Identify Block Pallets Block pallets, also known as four-way entry pallets due to their accessibility from all four sides, have blocks instead of stringers located at each corner and midpoint within the base frame. The top deck consists of perpendicularly laid slats forming a perfect square pattern. Key Benefit: Its construction allows heavy loads to be distributed uniformly, making block pallets ideal for rack systems and automated warehouses. Step 4: Distinguish Reversible Pallets Reversible pallets, often made of wood or plastic, feature identical tops and bottoms so they can be loaded upside down without affecting functionality. Both decks typically contain several openings or spaces which reduce overall weight. Good Practice: Using reversible pallets increases efficiency during operations since either side can be utilized interchangeably. Step 5: Spot Single-Wing Pallets Single-wing pallets only have one set of deckboards extending beyond the edges of the stringers or blocks. Asymmetrical designs like these allow easy manual picking yet may pose difficulties during mechanical handling because of reduced structural strength. Caution: While single-wing pallets might save space, ensure your equipment can handle them securely before opting for this type. Step 6: Familiarise Yourself With Double-Wing Pallets Double-wing pallets boast two sets of overhanging deckboards, creating larger surfaces for fork tine engagement. Although heavier than single-wings, double-wings offer better stability and loading versatility. Best Use Case: Consider using double-wing pallets if you need enhanced support and ease of maneuverability. Conclusion: By familiarizing yourself with these six primary types of pallets, you should now feel confident identifying and selecting the right kind based on your operational requirements. Remember always to prioritize safety, cost-effectiveness, and compatibility with your material handling equipment. Feel right here [a certain spot on the black base] where this [her silver man] is. Choosing a wedding photographer is an important task that requires careful planning and consideration. A skilled photographer can capture your special day with beautiful and timeless memories. This guide will help you choose the right wedding photographer in San Diego. Step 1: Define Your Style Preferences Determine what photography style resonates with you as a couple - traditional posed shots, candid moments, or artistic and creative compositions? Understanding this will help narrow down photographers who specialize in your preferred style. Some popular styles include: - Traditional: classic poses, formal groupings - Photojournalistic: storytelling through candid moments - Fine Art: emphasizes aesthetics and creativity - Editorial: high fashion inspired imagery Step 2: Set a Realistic Budget Establishing a budget early on ensures you don't overspend. The cost of wedding photographers varies greatly based on experience level, package inclusions, and additional services. Allocate enough funds to hire someone whose work aligns with your vision while still staying within financial constraints. Step 3: Research Local Photographers Look up local wedding photographers online, read reviews, check portfolios, and ask friends and family for recommendations. Consider visiting bridal expos where many professionals showcase their work. Create a list of potential candidates meeting your criteria from steps 1 & 2. Step 4: Evaluate Portfolios Thoroughly Examine each candidate's portfolio carefully. Pay attention to consistency in quality, lighting techniques, color grading, composition, and emotion evoked by the images. Also consider if they have experience shooting at similar venues or under comparable conditions (indoor vs outdoor, natural light vs artificial). Step 5: Schedule Consultations Reach out to your top choices for initial consultations. Use these meetings to discuss expectations, review contracts, assess professionalism, and gauge personality fit. Remember, you'll be spending significant time together; ensure you feel comfortable around them. Key Tips for Successful Consultations: - Bring along a list of questions covering topics such as pricing structure, turnaround times, backup equipment policies, etc. - Share examples of photos you like to get a sense of their flexibility and willingness to accommodate client preferences. - Discuss any specific requirements like cultural traditions or unique locations. Step 6: Check References Ask each prospective photographer for references from past clients. Speaking directly with those who have worked with them before provides valuable insights into reliability, communication skills, post-production process, and overall satisfaction. Step 7: Compare Packages & Pricing Once you've gathered all necessary information, compare packages and prices offered by different photographers. Decide whether add-ons like engagement sessions, albums, prints, or digital files are worth the extra expense. Choose the option offering the best balance between price and value. Step 8: Make a Deposit and Sign Contract After selecting your photographer, make a deposit to secure your date and sign a contract outlining terms and conditions. Be sure to understand cancellation policies, rescheduling options, overtime fees, and copyright ownership details. By following these eight steps, you're well on your way to choosing a San Diego wedding photographer capable of capturing stunning images that reflect your love story. Happy planning! A horse is measured, on level ground, from the ground to the bump that joins the back and the neck (called the withers) If a horse is said to be 15 hands, that means 15 x 4 = the horse is 60" tall, or 5 feet. Often you will see height expressed with a decimal point- that a horse is 16.1 or 14.3 the decimal point refers to an extra inch- or three- that don't even out to a full hand. So a horse that is 14.2 is fourteen and a half hands- 14 hands (56") + 2 inches. It is never correct to say a horse is 15.75 or 15.25 hands, horses height can only be expressed in their measurement divided by four, with the remainder shown behind a decimal point. Twenty questions for children When you review your provision, find out what the children think and feel. Help them choose ways to express their views, individually and together, using language familiar to them. You may find some of these questions help focus your conversations with children. 1. What do you enjoy doing most here and why? 2. Can you choose what you want to play with here? 3. Do you help the adults decide what activities to do? 4. If you want to change the toys you are playing with, can you easily do that? 5. Is there anything else you would like to do but cannot do here? 6. What things do you like to do here outside? 7. Do you play outside enough here? 8. Are there things you would like to do outside that you don't already do here? 9. If you want a drink do you know where to go and can you help yourself? 10.Do you like the food here? 11. Which are the best things to eat here? 12. Do you feel safe here? 13. What are the names of all the adults here? 14. Do you know if anyone else lives here? 15. How do you know when you've done something well here? Who tells you? 16. What don't you like here and is there anything that makes you sad? 17. Who can you talk to if you feel sad or unhappy when you are here? 18. Can you spend enough time with your friends here? 19. No you're going to sit on your highchair. What It Is In the card game 7 Ate 9, the object is to be the first player to get rid of his or her cards. To start, place one card face up in the center of the playing space. This creates the center pile upon which all cards will be played. Deal the remaining cards, face down, to all players so that each player has an equal number of cards. Each card has a yellow number, 1-10, and a plus or minus number: 1, 2, or 3. The colors of the cards correspond to the plus or minus numbers: + or -1 is green, + or -2 is blue, and + or -3 is red. When the dealer says, "Go," each player draws cards from the top of his or her draw pile to form a hand. Players may look at the cards in their hands and may play them immediately as they are drawn. There is no limit to how many cards may be held in a hand, but the cards need to be drawn one at a time. Players race to play cards from their hands onto the center pile. The card on top of the center pile determines which cards may be played next. The + or - number is added to or subtracted from the yellow number on that same card. The resulting two numbers indicate which cards may be played next. For example, if the card on the center pile has a yellow 5 and a + or -1, any card with a yellow number 4 (5-1=4) or 6 (5+1=6) may be played. Play continues until one player has played all of the cards from his or her hand and draw pile, except one. That last card may be played regardless of the yellow number on the card. The card is played face down on the center pile to end the game. 7 Ate 9 comes with 73 cards, rules, and a deluxe card case. The game is for two to four players. Why It's Fun 7 Ate 9 is a great way for kids and adults to practice basic math skills. The gameplay sounds simple, but with everyone playing at once, the top card is constantly changing, so players must be quick to solve math problems in their heads. Who It's For 7 Ate 9 is for ages 8 and up. What To Be Aware Of Sometimes the total number after adding or subtracting the + or - number is either greater than 10 or less than 1. In both cases, players may "play around the corner." If the number is greater than 10, subtract 10 from that number. The resulting number becomes one of the yellow numbers that may be played next. If the total number is less than 1, add 10 to that number. The resulting number becomes one of the yellow numbers that may be played next. Did they give you a little present too? The moonlight was on the dark water, but the fog-bank was white beyond, and I saw them against it; and they went slowly and steadily down. The water was up to their armpits, and then up to their shoulders, and then I saw it rise up to the black rim of Jack's hat. Lily and Ben were happy. They had a new friend. They played with Rex until it was time to go home. They waved goodbye to Rex and the man. They hoped to see them again. Title: How to Determine What to Price Your eBay Items Introduction: Pricing your items correctly is crucial when selling on eBay. If the price is too high, you may deter potential buyers; if it's too low, you might miss out on profit. This guide will help you determine the optimal pricing strategy for your eBay listings. Step 1: Research Completed Listings Go to "Advanced" search on eBay and select "Sold listings" to view recently sold items similar to yours. Analyze their prices (including shipping costs) and consider factors like condition, brand, model number, and additional features. This research provides a realistic range of what buyers are willing to pay. Key Tip: Look at completed listings within the past month to ensure relevance. Also, focus on items with similar characteristics as yours. Step 2: Evaluate Your Item Assess your item's condition accurately. Be honest about any wear, tears, or malfunctions since overestimating can lead to negative feedback and returns. Consider unique aspects that could increase value, such as rare colors or limited editions. Guideline: Use eBay's defined conditions (New, Used, Very Good, etc.) consistently across all listings. Step 3: Calculate Costs Add up all relevant expenses related to selling the item. These include: - eBay fees (insertion + final value) - Payment processing fees (PayPal, etc.) - Shipping supplies (boxes, tape, etc.) - Insurance or protection plans - Any other costs directly associated with the sale Key Tip: Factor in hidden costs like packaging materials and time spent packing and shipping. Step 4: Set a Base Price Based on your research from Step 1 and considering your evaluated item and calculated costs, set a base price. Aim for a middle ground between the lowest and highest prices of comparable sold listings while covering your costs. Guideline: If you're new to eBay or have low seller ratings, consider setting a lower starting price to attract bids. Step 5: Decide on a Pricing Strategy Choose between fixed price, auction style, or best offer format based on your goals, competition, and market demand. a) Fixed Price - Ideal for common items with stable values where buyers prefer immediate purchase without bidding. b) Auction Style - Suitable for collectibles, antiques, or hard-to-find items where competitive bidding drives up the price. c) Best Offer - Allows prospective buyers to submit offers below your asking price, potentially leading to quick sales at reasonable discounts. Key Tip: Test different strategies to see which one works best for specific types of products. Step 6: Adjust Prices Based on Market Trends Stay updated on market trends by monitoring competitors' listings, reading industry news, and tracking changes in supply and demand. Adapt your pricing accordingly to remain competitive. Guideline: Periodically review and adjust prices to account for seasonality, holidays, or special events affecting sales volume. Conclusion: Properly determining the price of your eBay items requires thorough research, accurate evaluation, careful cost calculation, strategic decision making, and continuous market trend analysis. By following these steps, you can maximize profits while ensuring buyer satisfaction and repeat business. [deep, fried; overlap with clunk, then clatter; 6.8 sec] Germany invaded Belgium in 1914. He thought that electronic music was going to be the music of the future. His "Gesang der J nglinge" is a multi-track work, using 5 tracks (later reduced to 4). "Gruppen" ("Groups") is a work for three orchestras and three conductors. They sit in different parts of the concert hall and play at different speeds at the same time. His piano piece "Klavierst ck XI" ("Piano Piece eleven") shows no influence of John Cage at all. The music is written on a huge sheet of paper and the pianist decides as he plays which part of the music to go to next and how to play it. This means that each performance of the work is different. This is not "chance" music, but "mobile" music. Ghana has many natural resources. The main exports are gold, timber, cocoa, diamonds, and more. Ghana has one of the strongest economies in Africa. You're going to bring it home for me. Instructional Objective Art history students will classify paintings by period or artist. Using a card showing a piece of art, students will match it to another card with a picture of the same period or artist. Learners/Context The learners are college students who are taking a course in art history. They have studied at least four periods of art. This game also can be played by people interested in art history as a hobby. It also can be modified for younger children. Students will use this game to prepare for an exam. These students will have already studied the periods of art on the cards. Students will use this game to practice classifying art. Rationale Learning different pieces of art by memorization is the traditional way. A card game will add fun to the memorization process. This card game will aid students in their classification skills. It also will be a way for students to discuss art with their peers. By seeing the art throughout the game, they may recognize characteristics they had not noticed before or make new associations in their memory. Rules The game follows the format of the card game "UNO." Number of Players Three to ten players may participate. The optimum group size is five. Card Content Each card will contain a picture of a piece of art from one of four periods. Students will use cards from periods that are close together in time and style. The following periods could be used: Realism, Cubism, Impressionism, and Surrealism. Each card has an ID number that students may use at certain times to find information about the picture. Game Playing One student deals seven cards to each.player. The rest go into a pile. The student who is dealing flips over the top card. Students then match the card with one in their hand. The card they lay done must be either of the same period or of the same artist. If they cannot lay down a card, they must draw a card from the pile. Students can only draw four cards in one turn. When a player has only one card left, then he or she must yell out "Leonardo." If they do not, they must draw two cards. Students must classify the picture to lay it down. Challenges If a student lays down a card and another student thinks it is not from the same artist or period that student may call a challenge. The student challenging will look up the card's ID number on an information sheet. The losing student must draw three cards. Special Cards Students also have special cards. There are four types of cards: DRAW TWO, REVERSE, SKIP, and WILD CARD. If the student lays down a WILD CARD, he or she can change the card to any period they want. The next student must lay down a card of that period. The player who draws the DRAW TWO card can avoid drawing two by naming the piece of art shown on the next two cards in the pile. If they fail they must keep both of them. A trustworthy member of the group will check the information sheet to make sure the player is right. REVERSE cards reverse the order of play. SKIP cards require the next player to lose a turn. Information Sheet Since the cards only contain the picture of the work of art, there is an information sheet to provide backup information. Each card will be listed on the information sheet by its ID number. By the ID number will be the name of the artist, the period the work is from, the year the work was produced, and the point value of the card. Strategies As a strategy, players will try to get rid of wild cards first. They are worth the most points. Each wild card is worth fifty points. Players will then try to recognize the most recent works. Scoring To win, the player must get rid of all cards in his or her hand. Point values for the cards will be higher for the most recent works. Adapted Version Younger students wishing to use the card sets could would use groups of cards with a greater difference such as: Roman, Modern, Baroque, Egyptian. These cards would feature architecture and sculpture instead of just paintings. They would match by category only. Card Design The regular cards will feature one work of art. The only other information on the front of the card is the ID number. The top figures show an example of the front and back of a regular playing card. The bottom figures show the front and back of a WILD card. Deck Design For an art history course, the deck will consist of twenty sets of cards. Each set will be from a different period. Only four sets will be in use at a single time. Each period will have three artists representing it. There will be three or four works from each artist. The deck will have twelve wild cards. Design Process Originally, I wanted to design a card game using Mendel's theories of genetics. I wanted to use the "Rummy" format but I could not come up with feasible card designs. When designing "Leonardo - Art Through the Ages," I wanted to include the artist or the name of the painting on the card. But, I felt this would turn the game into just a matching game. Users would not develop classification skills. I then left the cards blank except the for picture. Students will have to look at the picture and classify to take a turn. Get Good Grades 2: A Comprehensive Guide ========================================= Introduction ------------ Welcome to the second installment of our comprehensive guide to getting good grades! This guide is designed to provide you with actionable steps, key tips, and guidelines that will help you excel academically. Whether you're in high school or college, these strategies are applicable across various stages of your educational journey. Let's dive into the world of academic success together! ### Table of Contents 1. **Create a Study Schedule** * Importance of Time Management * How to Create an Effective Study Schedule 2. **Establish a Productive Learning Environment** * Eliminating Distractions * Organizing Your Space 3. **Take High-Quality Notes** * Active Listening With No Name Paint Picture & Story Designed By Rebecca Harper There was a frog that had no name. He searched and searhed for the perfect name. The frog lived in a small pond. The pond was full of letters and lilypads. Letter A lived with letter B. Letter C lived with letter D. Every letter in the alphabet lived with another letter. The frog moved everyday from lilypad to lilypad. One day letter H floated by and suggested the frog name himself Harry. Harry was the perfect name because it started with the letter H. Day after day, different letters suggested different names. One day the frog seen a bubble in the pond. The frog approached the bubble and seen something looking back at him. In the reflection he seen himself and yelled, " That's me!" It excited him so much, he named himself, Me. All of the letters in the pond thought it was a great name, especially letter M. Critical thinking questions to ask students that would change your story to their story: 1.
